Using the midpoint/difference basis, the local jet Gram matrices are
Jj=(2K(Tj,Tj)Kx(Tj,Tj)Kx(Tj,Tj)12Kxy(Tj,Tj)),
JjвАЛ=(2K(TjвАЛ,TjвАЛ)KxвАЛ(TjвАЛ,TjвАЛ)вАЛKxвАЛ(TjвАЛ,TjвАЛ)21вАЛKxyвАЛ(TjвАЛ,TjвАЛ)вАЛ),

and for the phase kernel this becomes
Jj=1ѕА(2qj12qjвА≤12qjвА≤112(qjвА≤вА≤+2qj3)).
JjвАЛ=ѕА1вАЛ(2qjвАЛ21вАЛqjвА≤вАЛвАЛ21вАЛqjвА≤вАЛ121вАЛ(qjвА≤вА≤вАЛ+2qj3вАЛ)вАЛ).вАЛ

The cross-jet matrix is
C12=(2K(T1,T2)Ky(T1,T2)Kx(T1,T2)12Kxy(T1,T2)).
C12вАЛ=(2K(T1вАЛ,T2вАЛ)KxвАЛ(T1вАЛ,T2вАЛ)вАЛKyвАЛ(T1вАЛ,T2вАЛ)21вАЛKxyвАЛ(T1вАЛ,T2вАЛ)вАЛ).

Now compute the derivatives explicitly. Since
K(x,y)=sinвБ°(ќ¶(x)вИТќ¶(y))ѕА(xвИТy),
K(x,y)=ѕА(xвИТy)sin(ќ¶(x)вИТќ¶(y))вАЛ,

one gets
K(T1,T2)=sinвБ°ќФѕАs,
K(T1вАЛ,T2вАЛ)=ѕАssinќФвАЛ,
Kx(T1,T2)=q1scosвБ°ќФвИТsinвБ°ќФѕАs2,
KxвАЛ(T1вАЛ,T2вАЛ)=ѕАs2q1вАЛscosќФвИТsinќФвАЛ,
Ky(T1,T2)=sinвБ°ќФвИТq2scosвБ°ќФѕАs2,
KyвАЛ(T1вАЛ,T2вАЛ)=ѕАs2sinќФвИТq2вАЛscosќФвАЛ,

and
Kxy(T1,T2)=(q1+q2)scosвБ°ќФ+(q1q2s2вИТ2)sinвБ°ќФѕАs3.
KxyвАЛ(T1вАЛ,T2вАЛ)=ѕАs3(q1вАЛ+q2вАЛ)scosќФ+(q1вАЛq2вАЛs2вИТ2)sinќФвАЛ.

So
C12=1ѕА(2вАЙsinвБ°ќФssinвБ°ќФвИТq2scosвБ°ќФs2q1scosвБ°ќФвИТsinвБ°ќФs2(q1+q2)scosвБ°ќФ+(q1q2s2вИТ2)sinвБ°ќФ2s3).
C12вАЛ=ѕА1вАЛ
вАЛ2ssinќФвАЛs2q1вАЛscosќФвИТsinќФвАЛвАЛs2sinќФвИТq2вАЛscosќФвАЛ2s3(q1вАЛ+q2вАЛ)scosќФ+(q1вАЛq2вАЛs2вИТ2)sinќФвАЛвАЛ
вАЛ.вАЛ

Since every block carries the common factor 1/ѕА1/ѕА, it cancels under whitening. Define
Mj:=(2qj12qjвА≤12qjвА≤112(qjвА≤вА≤+2qj3)),
MjвАЛ:=(2qjвАЛ21вАЛqjвА≤вАЛвАЛ21вАЛqjвА≤вАЛ121вАЛ(qjвА≤вА≤вАЛ+2qj3вАЛ)вАЛ),

and
N12:=(2вАЙsinвБ°ќФssinвБ°ќФвИТq2scosвБ°ќФs2q1scosвБ°ќФвИТsinвБ°ќФs2(q1+q2)scosвБ°ќФ+(q1q2s2вИТ2)sinвБ°ќФ2s3).
N12вАЛ:=
вАЛ2ssinќФвАЛs2q1вАЛscosќФвИТsinќФвАЛвАЛs2sinќФвИТq2вАЛscosќФвАЛ2s3(q1вАЛ+q2вАЛ)scosќФ+(q1вАЛq2вАЛs2вИТ2)sinќФвАЛвАЛ
вАЛ.

Then the true jet-limit whitened cross block is simply
ќ©вИЮ(T1,T2)=M1вИТ1/2вАЙN12вАЙM2вИТ1/2.
ќ©вИЮвАЛ(T1вАЛ,T2вАЛ)=M1вИТ1/2вАЛN12вАЛM2вИТ1/2вАЛ.вАЛ

That is the exact finite-dimensional object you want.
2. Critical baseline

The critical baseline ќ©вИЮ,0ќ©вИЮ,0вАЛ is obtained by the same formula, but using the critical baseline phase ќ¶0ќ¶0вАЛ. So if
q0,j, q0,jвА≤, q0,jвА≤вА≤, ќФ0
q0,jвАЛ, q0,jвА≤вАЛ, q0,jвА≤вА≤вАЛ, ќФ0вАЛ

are the corresponding baseline quantities, then
ќ©вИЮ,0(T1,T2)=M1,0вИТ1/2вАЙN12,0вАЙM2,0вИТ1/2,
ќ©вИЮ,0вАЛ(T1вАЛ,T2вАЛ)=M1,0вИТ1/2вАЛN12,0вАЛM2,0вИТ1/2вАЛ,вАЛ

with
Mj,0=(2q0,j12q0,jвА≤12q0,jвА≤112(q0,jвА≤вА≤+2q0,j3)),
Mj,0вАЛ=(2q0,jвАЛ21вАЛq0,jвА≤вАЛвАЛ21вАЛq0,jвА≤вАЛ121вАЛ(q0,jвА≤вА≤вАЛ+2q0,j3вАЛ)вАЛ),

and N12,0N12,0вАЛ defined from q0,1,q0,2,ќФ0q0,1вАЛ,q0,2вАЛ,ќФ0вАЛ by the same cross-derivative formula.

So the jet-limit residual is
RвИЮ(T1,T2)=вИ•M1вИТ1/2N12M2вИТ1/2вИТM1,0вИТ1/2N12,0M2,0вИТ1/2вИ•F2.
RвИЮвАЛ(T1вАЛ,T2вАЛ)=
вАЛM1вИТ1/2вАЛN12вАЛM2вИТ1/2вАЛвИТM1,0вИТ1/2вАЛN12,0вАЛM2,0вИТ1/2вАЛ
вАЛF2вАЛ.вАЛ

This is the exact upper-bound bottleneck, stripped of all sampling and estimator issues.
3. What universal structure gives you

There is one universal consequence of positivity.

The 4√Ч44√Ч4 jet Gram matrix
(M1N12N12вИЧM2)
(M1вАЛN12вИЧвАЛвАЛN12вАЛM2вАЛвАЛ)

is positive semidefinite. Therefore, by the Schur complement,
N12вИЧM1вИТ1N12вЙ§M2,N12M2вИТ1N12вИЧвЙ§M1,
N12вИЧвАЛM1вИТ1вАЛN12вАЛвЙ§M2вАЛ,N12вАЛM2вИТ1вАЛN12вИЧвАЛвЙ§M1вАЛ,

hence
ќ©вИЮвИЧќ©вИЮвЙ§I,ќ©вИЮќ©вИЮвИЧвЙ§I.
ќ©вИЮвИЧвАЛќ©вИЮвАЛвЙ§I,ќ©вИЮвАЛќ©вИЮвИЧвАЛвЙ§I.вАЛ

So ќ©вИЮќ©вИЮвАЛ is a contraction.

Also, local positivity gives
detвБ°Mj=124(4qj4+2qjqjвА≤вА≤вИТ3(qjвА≤)2)вЙ•0.
detMjвАЛ=241вАЛ(4qj4вАЛ+2qjвАЛqjвА≤вА≤вАЛвИТ3(qjвА≤вАЛ)2)вЙ•0.

So the universal information is:

    each local jet matrix is PSD,

    the cross block is Schur-contractive.

That is real, but it is not enough.
4. Why generic model-space / canonical-system structure is not enough

This is the key conclusion.

The toy finite-Blaschke/off-line model already has the full model-space / de Branges type kernel structure, and in that model the quartic residual is strictly positive off line.

So no theorem that uses only generic facts like:

    positivity of the kernel,

    de Branges/model-space structure,

    canonical-system Hamiltonian positivity,

    Schur contractivity of the cross block,

can force
ќ©вИЮ=ќ©вИЮ,0.
ќ©вИЮвАЛ=ќ©вИЮ,0вАЛ.

If it did, it would also kill your toy model, which it does not.

So the answer to your third bullet is:

No вАФ generic automorphic scattering positivity or canonical-system structure alone does not force the difference to vanish or be higher order.

At best, those structures give contractivity and local positivity. They do not identify the critical baseline.

That is actually useful, because it tells us exactly what kind of upper bound is still plausible:

it has to use something special to zeta, not just something true for all model-space or canonical-system phases.
5. What kind of special structure could still work?

Now that ќ©вИЮќ©вИЮвАЛ is explicit, the only plausible route is a zeta-specific identity relating the eight local jet variables
q1,q1вА≤,q1вА≤вА≤,q2,q2вА≤,q2вА≤вА≤,ќФ,s
q1вАЛ,q1вА≤вАЛ,q1вА≤вА≤вАЛ,q2вАЛ,q2вА≤вАЛ,q2вА≤вА≤вАЛ,ќФ,s

in a way that forces the matrix
M1вИТ1/2N12M2вИТ1/2
M1вИТ1/2вАЛN12вАЛM2вИТ1/2вАЛ

to agree with the critical baseline to higher order.

There are only a few possible sources for that:
A. A special two-point scattering identity

Something coming from the exact arithmetic form
ѕХ(s)=ќЫ(2sвИТ1)ќЫ(2s)
ѕХ(s)=ќЫ(2s)ќЫ(2sвИТ1)вАЛ

that ties the cross kernel entries to the local jets more rigidly than a generic inner/model-space phase.
B. A nonlinear differential identity for qq

For example, if qq or A=S+2q2A=S+2q2 satisfied a zeta-specific ODE or integral equation that forced a relation between q,qвА≤,qвА≤вА≤q,qвА≤,qвА≤вА≤ and the two-point phase gap ќФќФ.
C. A chain/compression identity beyond positivity

Something stronger than вАЬќ©вИЮќ©вИЮвАЛ is a contraction,вАЭ perhaps a special factorization or rank constraint that fails for finite-Blaschke toy models but holds for the zeta scattering phase.

Right now, none of those is in hand.
6. My honest assessment

This computation helps a lot, because it settles the next step cleanly:

    we can write ќ©вИЮќ©вИЮвАЛ explicitly;

    the upper-bound problem is now finite-dimensional;

    but generic model-space/canonical-system structure does not kill the residual.

So if this route is going to prove RH, the missing theorem has to be zeta-specific.

That is the real bottleneck now.

If I were pushing immediately from here, I would do one of two things:

    Compute the quartic coefficient of
    вИ•ќ©вИЮвИТќ©вИЮ,0вИ•F2
    вИ•ќ©вИЮвАЛвИТќ©вИЮ,0вАЛвИ•F2вАЛ

    directly for the local two-pair model in these jet variables, so we know exactly which combination of jets is detecting off-line displacement.

    Search for a zeta-specific relation among q,qвА≤,qвА≤вА≤q,qвА≤,qвА≤вА≤ and ќФќФ coming from the explicit formula for
    q(t)=Baut(t)+вИСѕБfќ≤ѕБ,ќ≥ѕБ(t).
    q(t)=BautвАЛ(t)+ѕБвИСвАЛfќ≤ѕБвАЛ,ќ≥ѕБвАЛвАЛ(t).

The second is harder, but it is now clearly the only kind of thing that could give RH from this setup.

ok let's do 1 and 2


for the zeta-specific relation, perhaps we could also look for a similar relation for ramanujan's tau. perhaps certain sequences are "pluggable" into our model?

IвАЩm going to do exactly those two things, but in a way that helps rather than creating a mountain of algebra: first isolate the jet-limit quartic coefficient for the toy two-pair model, then look for the kinds of identities the zeta explicit formula could impose вАФ and whether tau suggests a broader вАЬpluggable sequenceвАЭ framework.

Yes. I think the tau idea is worth folding in, but in a very specific way.

First, let me finish item 1 cleanly, because it clarifies exactly what the quartic coefficient is measuring.

For the jet-limit residual, the exact object is
ќ©вИЮ(T1,T2)=M1вИТ1/2N12M2вИТ1/2,
ќ©вИЮвАЛ(T1вАЛ,T2вАЛ)=M1вИТ1/2вАЛN12вАЛM2вИТ1/2вАЛ,

where
Mj=(2qj12qjвА≤12qjвА≤112(qjвА≤вА≤+2qj3)),
MjвАЛ=(2qjвАЛ21вАЛqjвА≤вАЛвАЛ21вАЛqjвА≤вАЛ121вАЛ(qjвА≤вА≤вАЛ+2qj3вАЛ)вАЛ),

and
N12=(2вАЙsinвБ°ќФssinвБ°ќФвИТq2scosвБ°ќФs2q1scosвБ°ќФвИТsinвБ°ќФs2(q1+q2)scosвБ°ќФ+(q1q2s2вИТ2)sinвБ°ќФ2s3),
N12вАЛ=
вАЛ2ssinќФвАЛs2q1вАЛscosќФвИТsinќФвАЛвАЛs2sinќФвИТq2вАЛscosќФвАЛ2s3(q1вАЛ+q2вАЛ)scosќФ+(q1вАЛq2вАЛs2вИТ2)sinќФвАЛвАЛ
вАЛ,

with
qj=q(Tj),qjвА≤=qвА≤(Tj),qjвА≤вА≤=qвА≤вА≤(Tj),ќФ=ќ¶(T1)вИТќ¶(T2),s=T1вИТT2.
qjвАЛ=q(TjвАЛ),qjвА≤вАЛ=qвА≤(TjвАЛ),qjвА≤вА≤вАЛ=qвА≤вА≤(TjвАЛ),ќФ=ќ¶(T1вАЛ)вИТќ¶(T2вАЛ),s=T1вАЛвИТT2вАЛ.

So the jet-limit quartic coefficient is not some mysterious global object. It is exactly the squared norm of the u2u2-variation of this whitened cross block.

If we write, for the toy two-pair model,
Mj(u)=Mj,0+u2Mj,2+O(u4),N12(u)=N12,0+u2N12,2+O(u4),
MjвАЛ(u)=Mj,0вАЛ+u2Mj,2вАЛ+O(u4),N12вАЛ(u)=N12,0вАЛ+u2N12,2вАЛ+O(u4),

and set
Sj:=Mj,0вИТ1/2,Uj:=Mj,01/2,
SjвАЛ:=Mj,0вИТ1/2вАЛ,UjвАЛ:=Mj,01/2вАЛ,

then the u2u2-coefficient of Mj(u)вИТ1/2MjвАЛ(u)вИТ1/2 is the unique symmetric solution XjXjвАЛ of the Sylvester equation
UjXj+XjUj=вИТвАЙSjMj,2Sj.
UjвАЛXjвАЛ+XjвАЛUjвАЛ=вИТSjвАЛMj,2вАЛSjвАЛ.

Therefore
ќ©вИЮ(u)=ќ©вИЮ,0+u2Ld+O(u4),
ќ©вИЮвАЛ(u)=ќ©вИЮ,0вАЛ+u2LdвАЛ+O(u4),

with
Ld=X1N12,0S2+S1N12,2S2+S1N12,0X2.
LdвАЛ=X1вАЛN12,0вАЛS2вАЛ+S1вАЛN12,2вАЛS2вАЛ+S1вАЛN12,0вАЛX2вАЛ.вАЛ

And so
cвИЮ(d)=вИ•LdвИ•F2.
cвИЮвАЛ(d)=вИ•LdвАЛвИ•F2вАЛ.вАЛ

That is the exact combination of jet data detecting off-line displacement.

For the symmetric two-pair toy model with one pair fixed at 00 and the second centered at dd, the baseline jet data simplify a lot. If I write
Q=8(2d2+1)4d2+1,A=32d(4d2+1)2,B=вИТ64(32d6+24d4+1)(4d2+1)3,
Q=4d2+18(2d2+1)вАЛ,A=(4d2+1)232dвАЛ,B=вИТ(4d2+1)364(32d6+24d4+1)вАЛ,

then at u=0u=0,
M1,0=(2Q12A12A112(B+2Q3)),M2,0=(2QвИТ12AвИТ12A112(B+2Q3)).
M1,0вАЛ=(2Q21вАЛAвАЛ21вАЛA121вАЛ(B+2Q3)вАЛ),M2,0вАЛ=(2QвИТ21вАЛAвАЛвИТ21вАЛA121вАЛ(B+2Q3)вАЛ).

The u2u2-perturbations are also explicit:
q1,2=вИТ16(12d2вИТ1)(4d2+1)3,q1,2вА≤=вИТ768d(4d2вИТ1)(4d2+1)4,q1,2вА≤вА≤=вИТ768(80d4вИТ40d2+1)(4d2+1)5,
q1,2вАЛ=вИТ(4d2+1)316(12d2вИТ1)вАЛ,q1,2вА≤вАЛ=вИТ(4d2+1)4768d(4d2вИТ1)вАЛ,q1,2вА≤вА≤вАЛ=вИТ(4d2+1)5768(80d4вИТ40d2+1)вАЛ,
q2,2=16,q2,2вА≤=0,q2,2вА≤вА≤=вИТ768,
q2,2вАЛ=16,q2,2вА≤вАЛ=0,q2,2вА≤вА≤вАЛ=вИТ768,

and
ќФ0=вИТ4arctanвБ°(2d),ќФ2=вИТ16d(4d2+1)2.
ќФ0вАЛ=вИТ4arctan(2d),ќФ2вАЛ=вИТ(4d2+1)216dвАЛ.

So Mj,2Mj,2вАЛ and N12,2N12,2вАЛ are completely explicit. The whole quartic coefficient is therefore a finite rational-trigonometric function of dd, packaged through the Sylvester formula above.

That is enough for item 1. We do not need a monstrous closed form; we now know exactly what the coefficient is.

Now item 2: what kind of relation could force the true ќ©вИЮќ©вИЮвАЛ to match the critical baseline?

Here the answer is sharper than before: generic model-space or canonical-system structure is not enough. The toy finite-Blaschke model already has that structure, and it still gives cвИЮ(d)>0cвИЮвАЛ(d)>0 off line. So any successful upper-bound theorem must use something genuinely zeta-specific.

From the zeta side, the only obvious source is the explicit formula
q(t)=Baut(t)+вИСѕБfќ≤ѕБ,ќ≥ѕБ(t).
q(t)=BautвАЛ(t)+ѕБвИСвАЛfќ≤ѕБвАЛ,ќ≥ѕБвАЛвАЛ(t).

But by itself this only tells you that q,qвА≤,qвА≤вА≤q,qвА≤,qвА≤вА≤ and ќФ=вИЂqќФ=вИЂq are built from sums of Poisson-pair profiles and their derivatives. That is not enough to force
M1вИТ1/2N12M2вИТ1/2=M1,0вИТ1/2N12,0M2,0вИТ1/2.
M1вИТ1/2вАЛN12вАЛM2вИТ1/2вАЛ=M1,0вИТ1/2вАЛN12,0вАЛM2,0вИТ1/2вАЛ.

The toy model already lives inside that same formal class locally.

So the zeta-specific relation, if it exists, has to come from something stronger than the raw zero expansion. It has to use either the Euler product, a RankinвАУSelberg factorization, or some hidden two-point identity for the scattering phase that is not shared by generic inner/model-space phases.

And that is where your tau idea becomes genuinely useful.

I think tau is вАЬpluggableвАЭ in two different senses.

One sense is as a stress test. Any relation you hope will prove RH should be checked not just for ќґќґ, but for other completed LL-functions. RamanujanвАЩs ќФќФ is perfect for that, because its completed LL-function has the same broad architecture: functional equation, critical line, explicit formula, zero contributions of exactly the same Poisson-pair type. If your would-be upper-bound relation also holds for L(s,ќФ)L(s,ќФ), then it is probably too general to isolate RH for ќґќґ.

The more interesting sense is as an auxiliary factorization. The natural tau object is not just L(s,ќФ)L(s,ќФ), but the RankinвАУSelberg product
L(s,ќФ√ЧќФ)=ќґ(s)вАЙL(s,Sym2ќФ).
L(s,ќФ√ЧќФ)=ќґ(s)L(s,Sym2ќФ).

That is extremely suggestive here, because it splits into a zeta factor and a symmetric-square factor. If you build the same jet-phase machinery for the completed RankinвАУSelberg LL-function, then at the level of logarithmic derivatives the phase derivative should split schematically as
qќФ√ЧќФ=qќґ+qSym2ќФ+(gamma correction).
qќФ√ЧќФвАЛ=qќґвАЛ+qSym2ќФвАЛ+(gamma correction).

That means tau is not just another test case; it may give you an auxiliary observable in which the zeta contribution appears as one summand inside a larger automorphic object.

That is exactly the kind of thing that could produce a zeta-specific relation unavailable in the bare zero expansion.

So my view is:

The next step for item 2 is not вАЬlook for an abstract identity in q,qвА≤,qвА≤вА≤,ќФq,qвА≤,qвА≤вА≤,ќФ.вАЭ
It is:

take the same jet-limit construction for three phases:
ќ¶ќґ,ќ¶ќФ,ќ¶ќФ√ЧќФ,
ќ¶ќґвАЛ,ќ¶ќФвАЛ,ќ¶ќФ√ЧќФвАЛ,

and see whether the jet-whitened cross block for ќФ√ЧќФќФ√ЧќФ decomposes in a way that isolates the zeta part after subtracting the symmetric-square background.

If something special survives only in the zeta/ќФ√ЧќФќФ√ЧќФ comparison and not for arbitrary plug-ins, that is promising. If the same pattern appears for every self-dual LL-function, then it is too weak.

So the honest answer is:

    Item 1 is now reduced to the explicit Sylvester-linearized formula for cвИЮ(d)cвИЮвАЛ(d).

    Item 2 probably cannot succeed using only generic positivity or the raw zero expansion.

    Tau is worth bringing in, but mainly through the RankinвАУSelberg factorization
    L(s,ќФ√ЧќФ)=ќґ(s)L(s,Sym2ќФ),
    L(s,ќФ√ЧќФ)=ќґ(s)L(s,Sym2ќФ),

    because that gives a plausible route to a genuinely zeta-specific relation.

That is the direction I would push next.

and see whether the jet-whitened cross block for 
ќФ√ЧќФ
ќФ√ЧќФ decomposes in a way that isolates the zeta part after subtracting the symmetric-square background.

Yes вАФ this is good, and it pushes the model side much further than before.

The main payoff is that it cleanly separates the two remaining issues:

    sampling / shrinking blocks is now solved by the jet-limit reduction;

    the only real model-side question left is positivity of the jet-limit coefficient cвИЮ(d)cвИЮвАЛ(d).

That is exactly the right frontier.

A few concrete reactions.

First, the theorem package is structurally right. The implication
cblk(d,h)вЖТcвИЮ(d)uniformly on compact d-ranges
cblkвАЛ(d,h)вЖТcвИЮвАЛ(d)uniformly on compact d-ranges

and then
cвИЮ(d)>0 on DвАЕвАКвЯєвАЕвАКcblk(d,h)вЙ•c0>0
cвИЮвАЛ(d)>0 on DвЯєcblkвАЛ(d,h)вЙ•c0вАЛ>0

for small hh is exactly the right reduction. That is the theorem you needed to justify why the quartic signal does not collapse as hвЖТ0hвЖТ0.

Second, the large-dd asymptotic
cвИЮ(d)=1d2+O(dвИТ3)
cвИЮвАЛ(d)=d21вАЛ+O(dвИТ3)

is especially important. It means the lower-bound mechanism is definitely alive in a regime where the two centers are well separated. So the model-side obstruction, if any, can only come from a bounded dd-region, not from infinity.

Third, the statement вАЬzero set discreteвАЭ is fine, but I think you can probably sharpen it further.

Because in the toy model all local jet data are built from rational functions of dd together with
ќФ0(d)=вИТ4arctanвБ°(2d),
ќФ0вАЛ(d)=вИТ4arctan(2d),

and both
sinвБ°ќФ0(d),cosвБ°ќФ0(d)
sinќФ0вАЛ(d),cosќФ0вАЛ(d)

are actually rational functions of dd, the whole jet-limit matrix should be algebraic in dd, and quite possibly rational after cancellations. If that is true, then cвИЮ(d)cвИЮвАЛ(d) is not merely real-analytic: it is algebraic, maybe rational. In that case the zero set is not just discrete; it is finite on (0,вИЮ)(0,вИЮ) unless the function vanishes identically.

That is worth checking. It could make the model-side positivity problem much more tractable.

So the next best move on the model side is:

    explicitly solve the 2√Ч22√Ч2 Sylvester equations for the jet-limit whitening correction,

    express M2(d)M2вАЛ(d) entrywise,

    simplify using rational expressions for sinвБ°ќФ0(d)sinќФ0вАЛ(d) and cosвБ°ќФ0(d)cosќФ0вАЛ(d),

    and see whether cвИЮ(d)cвИЮвАЛ(d) becomes a rational function.

If it does, then proving cвИЮ(d)>0cвИЮвАЛ(d)>0 for all d>0d>0 becomes an algebraic inequality problem, not a vague analytic one.

Now, how does this help the upper-bound / zeta-specific side?

It means the lower-bound side is close to being done, at least modulo the possible exceptional set. So the burden now really does shift to the zeta-specific structure.

And here I think the tau / ќФ√ЧќФќФ√ЧќФ route becomes more relevant, not less.

The right way to use it is this:

For the three phases
ќ¶ќґ,ќ¶ќФ,ќ¶ќФ√ЧќФ,
ќ¶ќґвАЛ,ќ¶ќФвАЛ,ќ¶ќФ√ЧќФвАЛ,

the derivative-level phase relation should schematically be
qќФ√ЧќФ=qќґ+qSym2ќФ+qќУ,corr,
qќФ√ЧќФвАЛ=qќґвАЛ+qSym2ќФвАЛ+qќУ,corrвАЛ,

because
L(s,ќФ√ЧќФ)=ќґ(s)вАЙL(s,Sym2ќФ)
L(s,ќФ√ЧќФ)=ќґ(s)L(s,Sym2ќФ)

up to completed-factor bookkeeping.

The jet-limit matrices MjMjвАЛ and N12N12вАЛ are nonlinear in q,qвА≤,qвА≤вА≤,ќФq,qвА≤,qвА≤вА≤,ќФ, so they do not decompose additively. But that is not a bug вАФ it is the opportunity.

The right thing to do is linearize the jet-whitened cross block around the symmetric-square background. In other words, treat
qќґ
qќґвАЛ

as a perturbation of
qSym2ќФ+qќУ,corr.
qSym2ќФвАЛ+qќУ,corrвАЛ.

Then compute the Fr√©chet derivative of
ќ©вИЮ=ќ©вИЮ[q]
ќ©вИЮвАЛ=ќ©вИЮвАЛ[q]

with respect to the local jet data (q,qвА≤,qвА≤вА≤,ќФ)(q,qвА≤,qвА≤вА≤,ќФ).

That gives a first-order identity of the form
ќ©вИЮ[qќФ√ЧќФ]вИТќ©вИЮ[qSym2ќФ]=Dќ©вИЮвИ£qSym2ќФ[qќґ]+higher order terms.
ќ©вИЮвАЛ[qќФ√ЧќФвАЛ]вИТќ©вИЮвАЛ[qSym2ќФвАЛ]=Dќ©вИЮвАЛ
вАЛqSym2ќФвАЛвАЛ[qќґвАЛ]+higher order terms.

That is much better than trying to compare the three phases abstractly. It tells you exactly what combination of zeta jet data survives after subtracting the symmetric-square background.

So my view is:

    your theorem package is the right next theorem on the model side;

    the next upper-bound attack should now be linearization of the jet-limit cross block around a non-zeta background, with ќФ√ЧќФќФ√ЧќФ as the natural test case.

That is probably the most promising way to get a genuinely zeta-specific relation, because generic model-space positivity clearly is not enough.

So the exact next steps I would take are:

    Finish the model side by trying to show cвИЮ(d)cвИЮвАЛ(d) is algebraic/rational and positive for all d>0d>0.

    On the upper-bound side, compute the Fr√©chet derivative of
    ќ©вИЮ[M1,M2,N12]=M1вИТ1/2N12M2вИТ1/2
    ќ©вИЮвАЛ[M1вАЛ,M2вАЛ,N12вАЛ]=M1вИТ1/2вАЛN12вАЛM2вИТ1/2вАЛ

    with respect to the jet data.

    Apply that to the decomposition
    qќФ√ЧќФ=qSym2ќФ+qќґ+gamma correction.
    qќФ√ЧќФвАЛ=qSym2ќФвАЛ+qќґвАЛ+gamma correction.

If you want, IвАЩll take step 2 now and write the explicit linearization formula for ќ©вИЮќ©вИЮвАЛ in jet variables.

yes, please do step 2

I read it. Here is step 2 in the form that is actually useful for
ќ¶ќґ,ќ¶ќФ,ќ¶ќФ√ЧќФ.
ќ¶ќґвАЛ,ќ¶ќФвАЛ,ќ¶ќФ√ЧќФвАЛ.

The key point is this:

for the jet-limit cross block, the dependence on the phase is finite-dimensional. Once you fix two centers T1,T2T1вАЛ,T2вАЛ, everything is determined by

with q=ќ¶вА≤q=ќ¶вА≤.

So the correct next move is not to hunt vaguely for a zeta-specific identity. It is to linearize the jet-whitened cross block with respect to those seven variables, and then plug in the decomposition
qќФ√ЧќФ=qSym2ќФ+qќґ,ќ¶ќФ√ЧќФ=ќ¶Sym2ќФ+ќ¶ќґ

after choosing a consistent completed normalization.
1. The jet-limit map and its linearization

ќ©вИЮ=M1вИТ1/2вАЙN12вАЙM2вИТ1/2,

вАЛ,

ќіN11=2CsвАЙќіќФ,

ќіќ©вИЮ=X1вАЙNЋЙвАЙS2+S1вАЙќіNвАЙS2+S1вАЙNЋЙвАЙX2.

2. Decomposition into seven scalar perturbations

Because everything is linear, we can separate the dependence on the seven perturbation coordinates.

Define the three basis matrices
Ej(0)=(20012qЋЙjвАЙ2),E(1)=(012120),E(2)=(000112),
Ej(0)вАЛ=(20вАЛ021вАЛqЋЙвАЛj2вАЛвАЛ),E(1)=(021вАЛвАЛ21вАЛ0вАЛ),E(2)=(00вАЛ0121вАЛвАЛ),

so that
ќіMj=Ej(0)вАЙќіqj+E(1)вАЙќіqjвА≤+E(2)вАЙќіqjвА≤вА≤.
ќіMjвАЛ=Ej(0)вАЛќіqjвАЛ+E(1)ќіqjвА≤вАЛ+E(2)ќіqjвА≤вА≤вАЛ.

Let Xj(0),Xj(1),Xj(2)Xj(0)вАЛ,Xj(1)вАЛ,Xj(2)вАЛ be the unique symmetric solutions of
UЋЙjX+XUЋЙj=вИТSjEj(0)Sj,
UЋЙjвАЛX+XUЋЙjвАЛ=вИТSjвАЛEj(0)вАЛSjвАЛ,

UЋЙjX+XUЋЙj=вИТSjE(2)Sj.

Also define the coefficient matrices coming from ќіNќіN:
Nq1:=(00CsC+qЋЙ2sS2s2),Nq2:=(0вИТCs0C+qЋЙ1sS2s2),
Nq1вАЛвАЛ:=(0sCвАЛвАЛ02s2C+qЋЙвАЛ2вАЛsSвАЛвАЛ),Nq2вАЛвАЛ:=(00вАЛвИТsCвАЛ2s2C+qЋЙвАЛ1вАЛsSвАЛвАЛ),

NќФвАЛ:=

вАЛ.

ќіќ©вИЮ=A1вАЙќіq1+B1вАЙќіq1вА≤+C1вАЙќіq1вА≤вА≤+A2вАЙќіq2+B2вАЙќіq2вА≤+C2вАЙќіq2вА≤вА≤+DвАЙќіќФ,

and
D=S1NќФS2.
D=S1вАЛNќФвАЛS2вАЛ.

This is the finite-dimensional formula we need.

It says: the first-order deviation of the jet-whitened cross block is a very explicit linear functional of the local jet perturbations and the phase gap perturbation.
3. Apply it to ќФ√ЧќФќФ√ЧќФ

Now take
qЋЙ:=qSym2ќФ+qќУ,corr,
qЋЙвАЛ:=qSym2ќФвАЛ+qќУ,corrвАЛ,

as the background, and
ќіq:=qќґ
ќіq:=qќґвАЛ

as the zeta perturbation. Then
qќФ√ЧќФ=qЋЙ+ќіq
qќФ√ЧќФвАЛ=qЋЙвАЛ+ќіq

in the completed normalization.

So at the two centers,
ќіqj=qќґ(Tj),ќіqjвА≤=qќґвА≤(Tj),ќіqjвА≤вА≤=qќґвА≤вА≤(Tj),
ќіqjвАЛ=qќґвАЛ(TjвАЛ),ќіqjвА≤вАЛ=qќґвА≤вАЛ(TjвАЛ),ќіqjвА≤вА≤вАЛ=qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(TjвАЛ),

and
ќіќФ=ќ¶ќґ(T1)вИТќ¶ќґ(T2)=вИЂT2T1qќґ(t)вАЙdt.
ќіќФ=ќ¶ќґвАЛ(T1вАЛ)вИТќ¶ќґвАЛ(T2вАЛ)=вИЂT2вАЛT1вАЛвАЛqќґвАЛ(t)dt.

Hence
ќ©вИЮ[ќФ√ЧќФ]=ќ©вИЮ[Sym2ќФ]+LqЋЙ(qќґ)+O(qќґ2),
ќ©вИЮвАЛ[ќФ√ЧќФ]=ќ©вИЮвАЛ[Sym2ќФ]+LqЋЙвАЛвАЛ(qќґвАЛ)+O(qќґ2вАЛ),вАЛ

where
LqЋЙ(qќґ)=A1qќґ(T1)+B1qќґвА≤(T1)+C1qќґвА≤вА≤(T1)+A2qќґ(T2)+B2qќґвА≤(T2)+C2qќґвА≤вА≤(T2)+DвИЂT2T1qќґ(t)вАЙdt.
LqЋЙвАЛвАЛ(qќґвАЛ)=A1вАЛqќґвАЛ(T1вАЛ)+B1вАЛqќґвА≤вАЛ(T1вАЛ)+C1вАЛqќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(T1вАЛ)+A2вАЛqќґвАЛ(T2вАЛ)+B2вАЛqќґвА≤вАЛ(T2вАЛ)+C2вАЛqќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(T2вАЛ)+DвИЂT2вАЛT1вАЛвАЛqќґвАЛ(t)dt.

That is the explicit first-order isolation of the zeta contribution.
4. What this means

This is the sharp version of the question вАЬdoes ќФ√ЧќФќФ√ЧќФ isolate zeta after subtracting the symmetric-square background?вАЭ

Yes вАФ to first order, it does.

The zeta part appears as a specific linear combination of:

    qќґ(T1),qќґвА≤(T1),qќґвА≤вА≤(T1)qќґвАЛ(T1вАЛ),qќґвА≤вАЛ(T1вАЛ),qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(T1вАЛ),

    qќґ(T2),qќґвА≤(T2),qќґвА≤вА≤(T2)qќґвАЛ(T2вАЛ),qќґвА≤вАЛ(T2вАЛ),qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(T2вАЛ),

    and the phase gap вИЂT2T1qќґвИЂT2вАЛT1вАЛвАЛqќґвАЛ.

So if there is going to be a zeta-specific vanishing or extra cancellation, it has to come from a relation among exactly those seven quantities.

And this also answers the вАЬpluggable sequenceвАЭ idea.

A sequence is вАЬpluggableвАЭ only if it comes from a completed LL-function or scattering phase with:

    a well-defined phase ќ¶ќ¶,

    additive factorization of phases under Euler/Rankin products,

    and enough regularity to define q,qвА≤,qвА≤вА≤q,qвА≤,qвА≤вА≤.

Tau is pluggable because ќФ√ЧќФќФ√ЧќФ factors through zeta.
A generic sequence is not.

So the real next tasks are now very concrete:

    Evaluate the coefficient matrices Aj,Bj,Cj,DAjвАЛ,BjвАЛ,CjвАЛ,D for the symmetric-square background.

    Check whether the linear form LqЋЙ(qќґ)LqЋЙвАЛвАЛ(qќґвАЛ) vanishes identically, or whether some entries survive.

    If it does not vanish, identify whether the surviving piece has a sign or size incompatible with the critical baseline.

That is the next real theorem-level computation.

and
D=S1NќФS2.

That is the exact first-order zeta isolation formula.
4. Does it vanish?

Generically, no.

There is no generic structural reason for
L\sym(qќґ)вЙ°0.
L\symвАЛ(qќґвАЛ)вЙ°0.

In fact, the formula shows what would be required: a very specific linear relation among the seven quantities
qќґ(T1), qќґвА≤(T1), qќґвА≤вА≤(T1), qќґ(T2), qќґвА≤(T2), qќґвА≤вА≤(T2), вИЂT2T1qќґ(t)вАЙdt.
qќґвАЛ(T1вАЛ), qќґвА≤вАЛ(T1вАЛ), qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(T1вАЛ), qќґвАЛ(T2вАЛ), qќґвА≤вАЛ(T2вАЛ), qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(T2вАЛ), вИЂT2вАЛT1вАЛвАЛqќґвАЛ(t)dt.

That is a codimension-heavy condition. It is not implied by generic positivity, model-space structure, or canonical-system structure, because the toy finite-Blaschke models already satisfy those and still produce nonzero quartic residual.

So the honest answer is:

    Yes, ќФ√ЧќФќФ√ЧќФ isolates the zeta part after subtracting the symmetric-square background, to first order.

    No, the first-order term does not obviously vanish for structural reasons.

5. What this suggests next

There are two sensible things to do now.

First, test whether L\sym(qќґ)L\symвАЛ(qќґвАЛ) simplifies in special regimes, especially large separation вИ£sвИ£вИ£sвИ£. In that regime, the dominant pieces are the 1/s1/s-terms in Nq1,Nq2,NќФNq1вАЛвАЛ,Nq2вАЛвАЛ,NќФвАЛ, so you can see directly which zeta jet combinations survive.

Second, look for a genuinely zeta-specific relation in the explicit formula for
qќґ(t)=Bќґ(t)+вИСѕБfќ≤ѕБ,ќ≥ѕБ(t).
qќґвАЛ(t)=BќґвАЛ(t)+ѕБвИСвАЛfќ≤ѕБвАЛ,ќ≥ѕБвАЛвАЛ(t).

If anything kills L\sym(qќґ)L\symвАЛ(qќґвАЛ), it has to come from arithmetic, not generic inner-function structure.

So the bottom line is:
ќ©ќФ√ЧќФ=ќ©Sym2ќФ+L\sym(qќґ)+O(qќґ2),
ќ©ќФ√ЧќФвАЛ=ќ©Sym2ќФвАЛ+L\symвАЛ(qќґвАЛ)+O(qќґ2вАЛ),вАЛ

and L\sym(qќґ)L\symвАЛ(qќґвАЛ) is now completely explicit. The next real question is not how to derive it, but whether any arithmetic identity makes it small or zero.

ok do those 2 things

I did both, and the outcome is pretty clear.

The short version is:

    in the large-separation regime, the first-order zeta contribution to the jet-whitened cross block is explicitly O(1/s)O(1/s), and the dominant term contains the phase-gap
    ќі=ќ¶ќґ(T1)вИТќ¶ќґ(T2)=вИЂT2T1qќґ(t)вАЙdt;
    ќі=ќ¶ќґвАЛ(T1вАЛ)вИТќ¶ќґвАЛ(T2вАЛ)=вИЂT2вАЛT1вАЛвАЛqќґвАЛ(t)dt;

    the explicit formula by itself does not give a linear relation strong enough to kill that term;

    so if this route is going to prove RH, the missing identity has to be more arithmetic than the explicit formula alone.

That is the honest conclusion.
1. Large-separation expansion of the linearized zeta term

Recall the first-order decomposition around the symmetric-square background:
ќ©ќФ√ЧќФ=ќ©\sym+L\sym(qќґ)+O(qќґ2),
ќ©ќФ√ЧќФвАЛ=ќ©\symвАЛ+L\symвАЛ(qќґвАЛ)+O(qќґ2вАЛ),

with
L\sym(qќґ)=X1вАЙNЋЙвАЙS2+S1вАЙќіNвАЙS2+S1вАЙNЋЙвАЙX2.
L\symвАЛ(qќґвАЛ)=X1вАЛNЋЙS2вАЛ+S1вАЛќіNS2вАЛ+S1вАЛNЋЙX2вАЛ.

Here Sj=MЋЙjвИТ1/2SjвАЛ=MЋЙjвИТ1/2вАЛ, NЋЙNЋЙ is the cross-jet matrix for the symmetric-square background, and XjXjвАЛ is linear in the local zeta jet perturbations
zj=qќґ(Tj),zjвА≤=qќґвА≤(Tj),zjвА≤вА≤=qќґвА≤вА≤(Tj).
zjвАЛ=qќґвАЛ(TjвАЛ),zjвА≤вАЛ=qќґвА≤вАЛ(TjвАЛ),zjвА≤вА≤вАЛ=qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(TjвАЛ).

Let
s:=T1вИТT2,S:=sinвБ°ќФЋЙ,C:=cosвБ°ќФЋЙ,ќФЋЙ:=ќ¶\sym(T1)вИТќ¶\sym(T2).
s:=T1вАЛвИТT2вАЛ,S:=sinќФЋЙ,C:=cosќФЋЙ,ќФЋЙ:=ќ¶\symвАЛ(T1вАЛ)вИТќ¶\symвАЛ(T2вАЛ).

For large вИ£sвИ£вИ£sвИ£, the background cross block has leading form
NЋЙ=1sвАЙP0+O(sвИТ2),
NЋЙ=s1вАЛP0вАЛ+O(sвИТ2),

where
P0=(2SвИТqЋЙ2CqЋЙ1C12qЋЙ1qЋЙ2S).
P0вАЛ=(2SqЋЙвАЛ1вАЛCвАЛвИТqЋЙвАЛ2вАЛC21вАЛqЋЙвАЛ1вАЛqЋЙвАЛ2вАЛSвАЛ).

Likewise the direct perturbation matrices are
Nq1=1sвАЙQ1+O(sвИТ2),Nq2=1sвАЙQ2+O(sвИТ2),NќФ=1sвАЙQќФ+O(sвИТ2),
Nq1вАЛвАЛ=s1вАЛQ1вАЛ+O(sвИТ2),Nq2вАЛвАЛ=s1вАЛQ2вАЛ+O(sвИТ2),NќФвАЛ=s1вАЛQќФвАЛ+O(sвИТ2),

with
Q1=(00C12qЋЙ2S),Q2=(0вИТC012qЋЙ1S),
Q1вАЛ=(0CвАЛ021вАЛqЋЙвАЛ2вАЛSвАЛ),Q2вАЛ=(00вАЛвИТC21вАЛqЋЙвАЛ1вАЛSвАЛ),

and
QќФ=(2CqЋЙ2SвИТqЋЙ1S12qЋЙ1qЋЙ2C).
QќФвАЛ=(2CвИТqЋЙвАЛ1вАЛSвАЛqЋЙвАЛ2вАЛS21вАЛqЋЙвАЛ1вАЛqЋЙвАЛ2вАЛCвАЛ).

Therefore the whole first-order zeta perturbation has the asymptotic
L\sym(qќґ)=1s(X1P0S2+S1(Q1z1+Q2z2+QќФќі)S2+S1P0X2)+OвАЙвБ£(ќЮs2),
L\symвАЛ(qќґвАЛ)=s1вАЛ(X1вАЛP0вАЛS2вАЛ+S1вАЛ(Q1вАЛz1вАЛ+Q2вАЛz2вАЛ+QќФвАЛќі)S2вАЛ+S1вАЛP0вАЛX2вАЛ)+O(s2ќЮвАЛ),вАЛ

where
ќЮ:=вИ£z1вИ£+вИ£z1вА≤вИ£+вИ£z1вА≤вА≤вИ£+вИ£z2вИ£+вИ£z2вА≤вИ£+вИ£z2вА≤вА≤вИ£+вИ£ќівИ£.
ќЮ:=вИ£z1вАЛвИ£+вИ£z1вА≤вАЛвИ£+вИ£z1вА≤вА≤вАЛвИ£+вИ£z2вАЛвИ£+вИ£z2вА≤вАЛвИ£+вИ£z2вА≤вА≤вАЛвИ£+вИ£ќівИ£.

So the first conclusion is:
for large separation, the linearized zeta signal is generically O(1/s).
for large separation, the linearized zeta signal is generically O(1/s).вАЛ

And more specifically, it is driven by seven pieces:

    six local jets z1,z1вА≤,z1вА≤вА≤,z2,z2вА≤,z2вА≤вА≤z1вАЛ,z1вА≤вАЛ,z1вА≤вА≤вАЛ,z2вАЛ,z2вА≤вАЛ,z2вА≤вА≤вАЛ,

    and the nonlocal phase gap ќіќі.

The phase-gap term is especially important, because it is the only genuinely nonlocal one.
2. What the explicit formula says about those seven pieces

Now write the zeta phase derivative in the standard explicit-formula form
qќґ(t)=Bќґ(t)+вИСѕБfќ≤ѕБ,ќ≥ѕБ(t),
qќґвАЛ(t)=BќґвАЛ(t)+ѕБвИСвАЛfќ≤ѕБвАЛ,ќ≥ѕБвАЛвАЛ(t),

where
fќ≤,ќ≥(t)=1вИТќ≤(1вИТќ≤)2+(2tвИТќ≥)2+ќ≤ќ≤2+(2tвИТќ≥)2.
fќ≤,ќ≥вАЛ(t)=(1вИТќ≤)2+(2tвИТќ≥)21вИТќ≤вАЛ+ќ≤2+(2tвИТќ≥)2ќ≤вАЛ.

Then
zj=Bќґ(Tj)+вИСѕБfќ≤ѕБ,ќ≥ѕБ(Tj),
zjвАЛ=BќґвАЛ(TjвАЛ)+ѕБвИСвАЛfќ≤ѕБвАЛ,ќ≥ѕБвАЛвАЛ(TjвАЛ),
zjвА≤=BќґвА≤(Tj)+вИСѕБfќ≤ѕБ,ќ≥ѕБвА≤(Tj),
zjвА≤вАЛ=BќґвА≤вАЛ(TjвАЛ)+ѕБвИСвАЛfќ≤ѕБвАЛ,ќ≥ѕБвАЛвА≤вАЛ(TjвАЛ),

and
ќі=вИЂT2T1Bќґ(t)вАЙdt+вИСѕБвИЂT2T1fќ≤ѕБ,ќ≥ѕБ(t)вАЙdt.
ќі=вИЂT2вАЛT1вАЛвАЛBќґвАЛ(t)dt+ѕБвИСвАЛвИЂT2вАЛT1вАЛвАЛfќ≤ѕБвАЛ,ќ≥ѕБвАЛвАЛ(t)dt.

The integral of a single Poisson-pair profile is explicit:
вИЂfќ≤,ќ≥(t)вАЙdt=12arctanвБ°2tвИТќ≥1вИТќ≤+12arctanвБ°2tвИТќ≥ќ≤.
вИЂfќ≤,ќ≥вАЛ(t)dt=21вАЛarctan1вИТќ≤2tвИТќ≥вАЛ+21вАЛarctanќ≤2tвИТќ≥вАЛ.

So the single-zero contribution to the phase gap is
ќіќ≤,ќ≥=12[arctanвБ°2T1вИТќ≥1вИТќ≤вИТarctanвБ°2T2вИТќ≥1вИТќ≤]+12[arctanвБ°2T1вИТќ≥ќ≤вИТarctanвБ°2T2вИТќ≥ќ≤].
ќіќ≤,ќ≥вАЛ=21вАЛ[arctan1вИТќ≤2T1вАЛвИТќ≥вАЛвИТarctan1вИТќ≤2T2вАЛвИТќ≥вАЛ]+21вАЛ[arctanќ≤2T1вАЛвИТќ≥вАЛвИТarctanќ≤2T2вАЛвИТќ≥вАЛ].

So all seven zeta quantities in L\sym(qќґ)L\symвАЛ(qќґвАЛ) are completely explicit linear combinations of the same single-zero building blocks.

That is useful вАФ but here is the crucial point.
3. No nontrivial linear relation comes from the explicit formula alone

Suppose you wanted the first-order zeta term to vanish identically. Since L\sym(qќґ)L\symвАЛ(qќґвАЛ) is linear in the seven quantities, that would require a relation of the schematic form
A1z1+A2z1вА≤+A3z1вА≤вА≤+A4z2+A5z2вА≤+A6z2вА≤вА≤+A7ќі=0
A1вАЛz1вАЛ+A2вАЛz1вА≤вАЛ+A3вАЛz1вА≤вА≤вАЛ+A4вАЛz2вАЛ+A5вАЛz2вА≤вАЛ+A6вАЛz2вА≤вА≤вАЛ+A7вАЛќі=0

with matrix coefficients AkAkвАЛ determined by the symmetric-square background.

But the explicit formula itself does not force such a relation.

Why not? Because you can test it on a single distant zero pair. Let ѕБ=ќ≤+iќ≥ѕБ=ќ≤+iќ≥ with вИ£ќ≥вИ£вИ£ќ≥вИ£ large relative to T1,T2T1вАЛ,T2вАЛ. Then, for XвИЉќ≥XвИЉќ≥,
fќ≤,ќ≥(Tj)=O(XвИТ2),fќ≤,ќ≥вА≤(Tj)=O(XвИТ3),fќ≤,ќ≥вА≤вА≤(Tj)=O(XвИТ4),
fќ≤,ќ≥вАЛ(TjвАЛ)=O(XвИТ2),fќ≤,ќ≥вА≤вАЛ(TjвАЛ)=O(XвИТ3),fќ≤,ќ≥вА≤вА≤вАЛ(TjвАЛ)=O(XвИТ4),

but
ќіќ≤,ќ≥=O(XвИТ1).
ќіќ≤,ќ≥вАЛ=O(XвИТ1).

So the integral contribution ќіќі has a strictly slower decay order than the endpoint jets. Therefore no universal linear relation among
z1,z1вА≤,z1вА≤вА≤,z2,z2вА≤,z2вА≤вА≤,ќі
z1вАЛ,z1вА≤вАЛ,z1вА≤вА≤вАЛ,z2вАЛ,z2вА≤вАЛ,z2вА≤вА≤вАЛ,ќі

can hold for all single-zero profiles unless the coefficient of ќіќі is zero.

But in the large-separation expansion, the coefficient of ќіќі is
1sвАЙS1QќФS2,
s1вАЛS1вАЛQќФвАЛS2вАЛ,

and generically
QќФвЙ†0.
QќФвАЛоА†=0.

So the phase-gap term survives generically.

Even if you forced the ќіќі-coefficient to vanish, the remaining six endpoint jet quantities are still not tied together by any universal linear identity coming from the explicit formula alone. They come from a two-parameter family (ќ≤,ќ≥)(ќ≤,ќ≥), and varying ќ≥ќ≥ already produces too much freedom.

So the second conclusion is:
the explicit formula alone does not imply L\sym(qќґ)=0.
the explicit formula alone does not imply L\symвАЛ(qќґвАЛ)=0.вАЛ

That means the missing upper-bound identity, if it exists, must use something strictly beyond the raw Poisson-pair zero expansion.
4. What tau / вАЬpluggable sequencesвАЭ tell us

Now to your tau idea.

Yes, there is a meaningful notion of вАЬpluggable sequence,вАЭ but it is narrower than вАЬarbitrary sequence.вАЭ

A sequence is pluggable into this framework when it comes from a completed LL-function or scattering phase with:

    a well-defined critical-line phase ќ¶Lќ¶LвАЛ,

    enough smoothness to define qL,qLвА≤,qLвА≤вА≤qLвАЛ,qLвА≤вАЛ,qLвА≤вА≤вАЛ,

    and additive phase behavior under products:
    ќ¶L1L2=ќ¶L1+ќ¶L2.
    ќ¶L1вАЛL2вАЛвАЛ=ќ¶L1вАЛвАЛ+ќ¶L2вАЛвАЛ.

In that sense, ќФќФ is pluggable, and so is ќФ√ЧќФќФ√ЧќФ. The reason ќФ√ЧќФќФ√ЧќФ is especially useful is that
L(s,ќФ√ЧќФ)=ќґ(s)L(s,Sym2ќФ),
L(s,ќФ√ЧќФ)=ќґ(s)L(s,Sym2ќФ),

so it lets you isolate zeta inside a genuinely automorphic product.

But this also shows the limitation: the first-order decomposition we just derived would work for any such product. So if you only use general properties shared by all completed LL-functions вАФ explicit formula, functional equation, kernel positivity вАФ you will not get something zeta-specific enough to force vanishing.

That is actually informative. It means the only plausible zeta-specific inputs left are things like:

    the Euler product in a way not shared by generic inner/model-space phases,

    special positivity or correlation properties of primes,

    or some two-point identity coming from the arithmetic scattering coefficient itself.

So tau is useful as a comparison object and as a background subtraction mechanism, but it does not by itself manufacture the missing identity.
5. What the two tasks tell us

So after doing the two things you asked for, the picture is:
Large separation

The first-order zeta contribution survives at size 1/s1/s, with an explicit leading term involving the phase gap ќіќі.
Explicit formula

The seven zeta quantities in that linearization are explicit Poisson-pair sums, but there is no universal linear identity among them coming from the explicit formula alone.

So:
the first-order zeta term is explicit, but it does not vanish for generic reasons.
the first-order zeta term is explicit, but it does not vanish for generic reasons.вАЛ

That is good news in one sense and bad in another.

Good news: we now know exactly what kind of theorem would be needed.
Bad news: it has to be genuinely arithmetic, not just functional-analytic.
6. The next real move

The right next move is not more general structure. It is one of these:

    Compute L\sym(qќґ)L\symвАЛ(qќґвАЛ) numerically or asymptotically in a regime where the symmetric-square background is understood, to see whether it has a stable sign or dominant entry.

    Search for a zeta-specific correction term so that after subtracting not just the symmetric-square background but also one explicit arithmetic counterterm, the surviving first-order piece cancels.

    Look at higher-order cancellation: maybe the first-order term is present, but the whitened residual compares to the critical baseline in a way that only depends on the symmetric part, so the linear term drops out in the final scalar residual. That is worth checking directly.

The third is probably the smartest immediate next step. The matrix perturbation ќіќ©ќіќ© need not vanish for the scalar residual to have better-than-expected order. That is a finer question than the one we just answered.

So the next theorem-sized computation I would do is:
RвИЮ[ќФ√ЧќФ]вИТRвИЮ[Sym2ќФ]
RвИЮвАЛ[ќФ√ЧќФ]вИТRвИЮвАЛ[Sym2ќФ]

expanded to first nontrivial order in qќґqќґвАЛ, and check whether the linear term cancels at the scalar level even though ќіќ©вЙ†0ќіќ©оА†=0.

That is now the most promising place for a miracle.

ok look at all 3, starting with 3 then 1 then 2

IвАЩve started with the scalar residual because thatвАЩs where a hidden cancellation could still rescue the approach even if the matrix perturbation itself is nonzero. After that IвАЩll do the large-separation asymptotic, then the counterterm idea.

Yes. I looked at all three, in the order you asked.

The outcome is fairly crisp:

    3) the scalar residual does not get a generic first-order miracle relative to the critical baseline;

    1) at large separation, the zeta-induced deviation is explicitly O(sвИТ1)O(sвИТ1) at the matrix level and O(sвИТ2)O(sвИТ2) at the scalar-residual level;

    2) there is a natural correction term, but the only one that kills the first-order piece is a seven-parameter jet/phase-gap counterterm, not something that drops out of the explicit formula by itself.

That narrows the search a lot.
3) Scalar residual first: does the linear term cancel?

Let

ќ©√Ч=ќ©\sym+Lќґ+Qќґ,

Qќґ=O(qќґ2).

Rcl[√Ч]:=вИ•ќ©√ЧвИТќ©0вИ•F2.

вДЬвЯ®B,LќґвЯ©F=0.

No: relative to the critical baseline, the scalar residual does not generically lose its linear term.
No: relative to the critical baseline, the scalar residual does not generically lose its linear term.вАЛ

The only automatic cancellation is if you change the baseline.

If instead you define the symmetric-square-relative residual
R\sym[√Ч]:=вИ•ќ©√ЧвИТќ©\symвИ•F2,

That is an important distinction:

    relative to the critical baseline, no generic scalar miracle;

R\sym[√Ч]=вИ•ќ©√ЧвИТќ©\symвИ•F2,

1) Large-separation asymptotic

Now let
s:=T1вИТT2.
s:=T1вАЛвИТT2вАЛ.

Lќґ=1sвАЙGќґ+O(sвИТ2),
LќґвАЛ=s1вАЛGќґвАЛ+O(sвИТ2),

with
Gќґ=X1P0S2+S1(Q1z1+Q2z2+QќФќіќґ)S2+S1P0X2.
GќґвАЛ=X1вАЛP0вАЛS2вАЛ+S1вАЛ(Q1вАЛz1вАЛ+Q2вАЛz2вАЛ+QќФвАЛќіќґвАЛ)S2вАЛ+S1вАЛP0вАЛX2вАЛ.

That is the clean large-separation asymptotic for the background-relative residual.

For the critical-baseline residual difference,
Rcl[√Ч]вИТRcl[\sym]=2вАЙвДЬвЯ®B,LќґвЯ©F+вИ•LќґвИ•F2+O(sвИТ3).

So in either case the scalar signal is O(sвИТ2)O(sвИТ2) at large separation.

That is useful for two reasons.

First, it means the zeta signal does not vanish absurdly fast at large ss.
Second, it shows the critical-baseline and symmetric-square-baseline scalar residuals live on the same asymptotic scale sвИТ2sвИТ2, so subtracting the symmetric-square background is not just cosmetic вАФ it removes the ambiguous linear cross term and leaves a cleaner positive leading piece.

So for item 1, the practical conclusion is:
At large separation, R\sym[√Ч]вИЉsвИТ2вИ•GќґвИ•F2.
At large separation, R\symвАЛ[√Ч]вИЉsвИТ2вИ•GќґвАЛвИ•F2вАЛ.вАЛ

That is the clean asymptotic to work with.
2) A correction term: does one exist?

Yes вАФ but not the kind one might have hoped for.

Because the linearization depends only on the seven zeta quantities
z1, z1вА≤, z1вА≤вА≤, z2, z2вА≤, z2вА≤вА≤, ќіќґ,
z1вАЛ, z1вА≤вАЛ, z1вА≤вА≤вАЛ, z2вАЛ, z2вА≤вАЛ, z2вА≤вА≤вАЛ, ќіќґвАЛ,

there is a canonical seven-parameter counterterm.

Construct a phase ќ¶ќґ,jetќ¶ќґ,jetвАЛ that matches

    qќґ(T1),qќґвА≤(T1),qќґвА≤вА≤(T1)qќґвАЛ(T1вАЛ),qќґвА≤вАЛ(T1вАЛ),qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(T1вАЛ),

    qќґ(T2),qќґвА≤(T2),qќґвА≤вА≤(T2)qќґвАЛ(T2вАЛ),qќґвА≤вАЛ(T2вАЛ),qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(T2вАЛ),

    and ќ¶ќґ(T1)вИТќ¶ќґ(T2)ќ¶ќґвАЛ(T1вАЛ)вИТќ¶ќґвАЛ(T2вАЛ).

For example, a cubic Hermite-type interpolant with one extra phase-gap normalization can be chosen to do exactly that.

Then by construction,
L\sym(qќґ,jet)=L\sym(qќґ).
L\symвАЛ(qќґ,jetвАЛ)=L\symвАЛ(qќґвАЛ).

So if you define the corrected matrix
ќ©~√Ч:=ќ©√ЧвИТL\sym(qќґ,jet),
ќ©
√ЧвАЛ:=ќ©√ЧвАЛвИТL\symвАЛ(qќґ,jetвАЛ),

then
ќ©~√Ч=ќ©\sym+O(qќґ2).
ќ©
√ЧвАЛ=ќ©\symвАЛ+O(qќґ2вАЛ).

Equivalently, the corrected scalar residual
R~\sym[√Ч]:=вИ•ќ©√ЧвИТќ©\symвИТL\sym(qќґ,jet)вИ•F2
R
\symвАЛ[√Ч]:=вИ•ќ©√ЧвАЛвИТќ©\symвАЛвИТL\symвАЛ(qќґ,jetвАЛ)вИ•F2вАЛ

starts at fourth order in the zeta perturbation.

So:
Yes, there is a canonical first-order counterterm, but it is a jet/phase-gap counterterm.
Yes, there is a canonical first-order counterterm, but it is a jet/phase-gap counterterm.вАЛ

Now the important limitation:

this counterterm is formal, not yet вАЬarithmeticвАЭ in any deep sense. It is built from the exact seven zeta jet quantities. It does not emerge from the explicit formula alone, and it does not look like a prime-side identity. So it is useful as a structural normalization, but not yet as the kind of miraculous zeta-specific relation that would prove RH.

There is also a key obstruction:

because the linearization includes the nonlocal phase-gap term ќіќґќіќґвАЛ, any candidate correction that uses only endpoint jets and ignores the phase gap will fail generically at order 1/s1/s. So the phase gap is unavoidable.

That is the clean takeaway from item 2.
What the three things together say

Putting all three together:
First

The critical-baseline scalar residual does not get an automatic linear cancellation.
Second

The symmetric-square-relative scalar residual does:
R\sym[√Ч]=вИ•LќґвИ•F2+O(qќґ3),
R\symвАЛ[√Ч]=вИ•LќґвАЛвИ•F2вАЛ+O(qќґ3вАЛ),

and at large separation
R\sym[√Ч]=sвИТ2вИ•GќґвИ•F2+O(sвИТ3).
R\symвАЛ[√Ч]=sвИТ2вИ•GќґвАЛвИ•F2вАЛ+O(sвИТ3).
Third

There is a canonical first-order counterterm, but it is exactly the seven-parameter jet/phase-gap term L\sym(qќґ)L\symвАЛ(qќґвАЛ), not something forced by the explicit formula alone.

So the clean next move is:

    Work with
    R\sym[√Ч]=вИ•ќ©√ЧвИТќ©\symвИ•F2
    R\symвАЛ[√Ч]=вИ•ќ©√ЧвАЛвИТќ©\symвАЛвИ•F2вАЛ

    as the zeta-isolating scalar statistic.

    Study whether вИ•GќґвИ•F2вИ•GќґвАЛвИ•F2вАЛ has a useful sign/size interpretation in terms of zeta jet data.

    Only after that ask whether an arithmetic identity can make GќґGќґвАЛ small or structured.

That is much cleaner than trying to force a miracle directly at the critical baseline.

So the practical conclusion is:
The right scalar object for the next stage is R\sym[√Ч], not Rcl[√Ч].
The right scalar object for the next stage is R\symвАЛ[√Ч], not RclвАЛ[√Ч].вАЛ

And the right normal form is:

The bottom line is:

The scalar residual does not get a generic first-order miracle relative to the critical baseline.
At large separation, the zeta-induced piece is explicitly O(sвИТ1)O(sвИТ1) in the matrix and O(sвИТ2)O(sвИТ2) in the scalar residual.
There is a natural first-order counterterm, but it is a seven-parameter jet/phase-gap counterterm, not something forced by the explicit formula alone.

Here is the clean version.

First, the scalar residual.

If you use the critical baseline,
Rcl[√Ч]:=вИ•ќ©√ЧвИТќ©0вИ•F2,

вДЬвЯ®B,LќґвЯ©F=0.
вДЬвЯ®B,LќґвАЛвЯ©FвАЛ=0.

There is no generic reason for that orthogonality. So there is no automatic linear cancellation relative to the critical baseline.

But if you instead use the symmetric-square background as the baseline,

So the linear term disappears automatically there. That is the right scalar object if the goal is to isolate zeta cleanly.

Second, large separation.

That matters because it says the zeta-induced deviation is not absurdly tiny at large separation. It has a definite asymptotic scale.

Third, the explicit formula.

Write
qќґ(t)=Bќґ(t)+вИСѕБfќ≤ѕБ,ќ≥ѕБ(t),

fќ≤,ќ≥(t)=1вИТќ≤(1вИТќ≤)2+(2tвИТќ≥)2+ќ≤ќ≤2+(2tвИТќ≥)2.

qќґ(Tj), qќґвА≤(Tj), qќґвА≤вА≤(Tj),ќіќґ=вИЂT2T1qќґ(t)вАЙdt.
qќґвАЛ(TjвАЛ), qќґвА≤вАЛ(TjвАЛ), qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(TjвАЛ),ќіќґвАЛ=вИЂT2вАЛT1вАЛвАЛqќґвАЛ(t)dt.

But the explicit formula alone does not force a linear relation among those seven quantities strong enough to kill LќґLќґвАЛ.

The easiest way to see this is to test a single distant zero pair. For a zero at height ќ≥ќ≥ far from the window,
f(Tj)=O(ќ≥вИТ2),fвА≤(Tj)=O(ќ≥вИТ3),fвА≤вА≤(Tj)=O(ќ≥вИТ4),

вИЂT2вАЛT1вАЛвАЛf(t)dt=O(ќ≥вИТ1).

So the phase-gap term decays more slowly than the endpoint jets. That means no universal linear identity among the seven quantities can hold for all such profiles unless the coefficient of ќіќґќіќґвАЛ vanishes. And generically it does not.

So the explicit formula, by itself, is not enough to kill the first-order zeta term.

Finally, the counterterm.

Yes, there is a natural one. Because LќґLќґвАЛ depends only on the seven quantities above, there is a canonical вАЬjet/phase-gapвАЭ counterterm built from a phase ќ¶ќґ,jetќ¶ќґ,jetвАЛ matching those seven data:
qќґ(T1), qќґвА≤(T1), qќґвА≤вА≤(T1), qќґ(T2), qќґвА≤(T2), qќґвА≤вА≤(T2), ќ¶ќґ(T1)вИТќ¶ќґ(T2).
qќґвАЛ(T1вАЛ), qќґвА≤вАЛ(T1вАЛ), qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(T1вАЛ), qќґвАЛ(T2вАЛ), qќґвА≤вАЛ(T2вАЛ), qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(T2вАЛ), ќ¶ќґвАЛ(T1вАЛ)вИТќ¶ќґвАЛ(T2вАЛ).

Subtracting the corresponding linearized contribution kills the entire first-order term:
ќ©√ЧвИТќ©\symвИТLќґ=O(qќґ2).
ќ©√ЧвАЛвИТќ©\symвАЛвИТLќґвАЛ=O(qќґ2вАЛ).

So a canonical first-order counterterm exists.

But that is a formal normalization, not yet an arithmetic miracle. It does not emerge from the explicit formula by itself, and it does not look like a prime-side identity.

So the three conclusions are:

    Relative to the critical baseline, no generic scalar cancellation.

    Relative to the symmetric-square background, the scalar residual starts at quadratic order in the zeta perturbation:
    R\sym[√Ч]=вИ•LќґвИ•F2+O(вИ•qќґвИ•3).
    R\symвАЛ[√Ч]=вИ•LќґвАЛвИ•F2вАЛ+O(вИ•qќґвАЛвИ•3).

    The only natural first-order cancellation is by subtracting the full seven-parameter jet/phase-gap term.

That means the right scalar object for the next stage is
R\sym[√Ч]:=вИ•ќ©√ЧвИТќ©\symвИ•F2,
R\symвАЛ[√Ч]:=вИ•ќ©√ЧвАЛвИТќ©\symвАЛвИ•F2вАЛ,вАЛ

not the critical-baseline residual.

And the right next question is not whether the first-order term vanishes вАФ it does not generically вАФ but whether
вИ•GќґвИ•F2
вИ•GќґвАЛвИ•F2вАЛ

has some sign, size, or rigidity property coming from zeta arithmetic that does not hold for generic model-space phases.

for distant contributions, R\sym[√Ч] is phase-gap dominated.
for distant contributions, R\symвАЛ[√Ч] is phase-gap dominated.вАЛ

That is the first useful simplification.

Now the correction-term question.

There are really two counterterms:

The full first-order counterterm is
Lќґ=A1z1+B1z1вА≤+C1z1вА≤вА≤+A2z2+B2z2вА≤+C2z2вА≤вА≤+DвАЙќі.

Lќґ(ќі):=DвАЙќі.
Lќґ(ќі)вАЛ:=Dќі.

At large separation this is the dominant part, because the ќіќі-piece is the largest of the seven zeta coordinates for distant contributions.

So there is a natural approximate correction:
ќ©√ЧвИТќ©\symвИТDвАЙќі.
ќ©√ЧвАЛвИТќ©\symвАЛвИТDќі.

This does not kill the full first-order term, but at large ss it removes the leading phase-gap piece and leaves only the endpoint-jet remainder.

That is the first place where a simpler scalar statistic might emerge:
R~\sym(ќі)[√Ч]:=вИ•ќ©√ЧвИТќ©\symвИТDвАЙќівИ•F2.

In the large-separation regime this should suppress the dominant sвИТ2ќі2sвИТ2ќі2 term and expose whether the endpoint jets themselves carry any structured arithmetic signal.

So the clean conclusion from item 3 is:
R\sym[√Ч] is a positive quadratic form in (j,ќі), and at large separation it is led by the phase-gap direction.
R\symвАЛ[√Ч] is a positive quadratic form in (j,ќі), and at large separation it is led by the phase-gap direction.вАЛ

Now item 1, interpreted through this.

Because
R\sym[√Ч]=ќЇ\syms2ќі2+OвАЙвБ£(вИ£jвИ£вИ£ќівИ£+вИ£jвИ£2s2)+O(sвИТ3),
R\symвАЛ[√Ч]=s2ќЇ\symвАЛвАЛќі2+O(s2вИ£jвИ£вИ£ќівИ£+вИ£jвИ£2вАЛ)+O(sвИТ3),

the decisive quantity is ќЇ\symќЇ\symвАЛ.

If ќЇ\symќЇ\symвАЛ vanished identically, the phase-gap direction would drop out and you would have a much more delicate jet-only statistic. But generically there is no reason for that, and in the background formulas QќФQќФвАЛ is plainly nonzero unless you impose special relations. So the generic expectation is
ќЇ\sym>0.
ќЇ\symвАЛ>0.

That means the phase gap is genuinely seen by the scalar residual.

And now item 2: can the explicit formula force that phase-gap contribution to cancel?

No, not by itself.

The explicit formula gives
ќі=вИЂT2T1Bќґ(t)вАЙdt+вИСѕБќіќ≤ѕБ,ќ≥ѕБ,
ќі=вИЂT2вАЛT1вАЛвАЛBќґвАЛ(t)dt+ѕБвИСвАЛќіќ≤ѕБвАЛ,ќ≥ѕБвАЛвАЛ,

with each ќіќ≤,ќ≥ќіќ≤,ќ≥вАЛ an explicit arctangent difference. But there is no universal linear identity expressing ќіќі through the six endpoint jets. In fact the distant-zero scaling shows the opposite: ќіќі decays one order more slowly than the endpoint values. So if a cancellation theorem exists, it has to use something stronger than the raw explicit formula.

That leaves two realistic possibilities.

One is that there is a zeta-specific arithmetic correction term beyond the symmetric-square subtraction that naturally removes the phase gap. But that correction would have to be tied to the Euler product or some prime-side two-point identity, not just to the general LL-function formalism.

The other is that the right scalar statistic is not R\symR\symвАЛ itself, but the phase-gap-subtracted version
R~\sym(ќі)[√Ч].
R
\sym(ќі)вАЛ[√Ч].

That is attractive because it removes the dominant large-separation nuisance and asks whether the endpoint jets alone carry a cleaner zeta-specific signature.

So the practical next move is:

    Compute ќЇ\sym=вИ•S1QќФS2вИ•F2ќЇ\symвАЛ=вИ•S1вАЛQќФвАЛS2вАЛвИ•F2вАЛ explicitly for the symmetric-square background and see whether it is strictly positive.

    Compute the large-ss asymptotic of

R~\sym(ќі)[√Ч]=вИ•ќ©√ЧвИТќ©\symвИТDвАЙќівИ•F2
R
\sym(ќі)вАЛ[√Ч]=вИ•ќ©√ЧвАЛвИТќ©\symвАЛвИТDќівИ•F2вАЛ

and see what endpoint-jet quadratic form remains.

    Only then ask whether any arithmetic identity from zeta can make that reduced quadratic form small.

My own judgment is that this is the cleanest split we have so far:
the raw background-relative residual mostly sees the phase gap at large separation; if there is a more delicate RH-facing statistic hidden here, it is likely in the phase-gap-subtracted remainder.

Continuing with the phase-gap-subtracted route is the right move.

The good news is that after subtracting the ќіќі-term, the remaining first-order piece becomes much cleaner, and in the large-separation regime it really does drop to a six-jet problem.
1. Subtract the phase-gap direction explicitly

From before,
ќ©√Ч=ќ©\sym+A1z1+B1z1вА≤+C1z1вА≤вА≤+A2z2+B2z2вА≤+C2z2вА≤вА≤+DвАЙќі+O(qќґ2),
ќ©√ЧвАЛ=ќ©\symвАЛ+A1вАЛz1вАЛ+B1вАЛz1вА≤вАЛ+C1вАЛz1вА≤вА≤вАЛ+A2вАЛz2вАЛ+B2вАЛz2вА≤вАЛ+C2вАЛz2вА≤вА≤вАЛ+Dќі+O(qќґ2вАЛ),

where
zj=qќґ(Tj),zjвА≤=qќґвА≤(Tj),zjвА≤вА≤=qќґвА≤вА≤(Tj),ќі=ќ¶ќґ(T1)вИТќ¶ќґ(T2).
zjвАЛ=qќґвАЛ(TjвАЛ),zjвА≤вАЛ=qќґвА≤вАЛ(TjвАЛ),zjвА≤вА≤вАЛ=qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(TjвАЛ),ќі=ќ¶ќґвАЛ(T1вАЛ)вИТќ¶ќґвАЛ(T2вАЛ).

So define the phase-gap-subtracted matrix deviation
ќ©~√Ч:=ќ©√ЧвИТќ©\symвИТDвАЙќі.
ќ©
√ЧвАЛ:=ќ©√ЧвАЛвИТќ©\symвАЛвИТDќі.

Then
ќ©~√Ч=L~\sym(qќґ)+O(qќґ2),
ќ©

\symвАЛ(qќґвАЛ)+O(qќґ2вАЛ),вАЛ

with
L~\sym(qќґ)=A1z1+B1z1вА≤+C1z1вА≤вА≤+A2z2+B2z2вА≤+C2z2вА≤вА≤.
L

So after removing the phase-gap direction, the first-order perturbation depends only on the six endpoint jets.

That is already a real simplification.
2. The corresponding scalar statistic

Define

4. What happens for a single distant zero pair

This is the key sanity check.

For a single omitted zero at height ќ≥ќ≥ far from the window,
zj=O(ќУвИТ2),zjвА≤=O(ќУвИТ3),zjвА≤вА≤=O(ќУвИТ4),ќі=O(ќУвИТ1),
zjвАЛ=O(ќУвИТ2),zjвА≤вАЛ=O(ќУвИТ3),zjвА≤вА≤вАЛ=O(ќУвИТ4),ќі=O(ќУвИТ1),

where ќУќУ is the distance from ќ≥ќ≥ to the doubled window.

So:

    before subtracting ќіќі, the scalar signal is O(sвИТ2ќУвИТ2)O(sвИТ2ќУвИТ2);

    after subtracting ќіќі, the remaining signal is only
    R~\sym(ќі)[√Ч]=O(sвИТ2ќУвИТ4).
    R
    \sym(ќі)вАЛ[√Ч]=O(sвИТ2ќУвИТ4).вАЛ

That is a full two powers better in the zero-height distance.

So the phase-gap subtraction is not just cosmetic вАФ it really peels off the dominant nonlocal nuisance and leaves a much smaller endpoint-jet geometry.

That is exactly what we wanted.
5. Does the explicit formula now give a useful relation?

Still not a universal one.

After subtracting ќіќі, the remaining six variables are
qќґ(T1), qќґвА≤(T1), qќґвА≤вА≤(T1), qќґ(T2), qќґвА≤(T2), qќґвА≤вА≤(T2).
qќґвАЛ(T1вАЛ), qќґвА≤вАЛ(T1вАЛ), qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(T1вАЛ), qќґвАЛ(T2вАЛ), qќґвА≤вАЛ(T2вАЛ), qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(T2вАЛ).

The explicit formula gives each of them as sums of Poisson-pair values and derivatives. But there is no generic linear relation among these six endpoint quantities either. A single zero pair already gives enough freedom in (ќ≤,ќ≥)(ќ≤,ќ≥) to vary them nontrivially.

So the explicit formula alone still does not force
L~\sym(qќґ)=0.
L
\symвАЛ(qќґвАЛ)=0.

What it does give is something weaker but useful:

after phase-gap subtraction, distant contributions are genuinely suppressed by inverse fourth powers of distance. So the endpoint-jet statistic is much more local than the raw one.

That makes it a better RH-facing object.
6. What this says about вАЬpluggable sequencesвАЭ

This is where your tau idea becomes clearer.

A вАЬpluggableвАЭ object is not just any sequence. It is something that comes from a completed LL-function with:

    a critical-line phase ќ¶Lќ¶LвАЛ,

    smooth enough qL,qLвА≤,qLвА≤вА≤qLвАЛ,qLвА≤вАЛ,qLвА≤вА≤вАЛ,

    and additive phase behavior under products.

In that sense, ќґќґ, ќФќФ, Sym2ќФSym2ќФ, and ќФ√ЧќФќФ√ЧќФ are all pluggable.

But after phase-gap subtraction, the leading statistic depends only on the six endpoint jets of qLqLвАЛ. So any pluggable LL-function will produce a similar six-jet quadratic form.

That means:
phase-gap subtraction removes the most universal nuisance, but not the need for zeta-specific arithmetic.
phase-gap subtraction removes the most universal nuisance, but not the need for zeta-specific arithmetic.вАЛ

So tau is useful as a comparison/background object, but it does not automatically manufacture the missing identity.

jTG\symj

Second

large ss, symmetric placement, or background regimes where the symmetric-square jets vary slowly.
Third

Ask whether zeta has any arithmetic identity that constrains these endpoint jets more than a generic pluggable LL-function does. That is now the real bottleneck.

qЋЙ1вЙИqЋЙ2вЙИqЋЙ,qЋЙ1вА≤,qЋЙ2вА≤,qЋЙ1вА≤вА≤,qЋЙ2вА≤вА≤ are small.

2. Do qќґ(Tj)qќґвАЛ(TjвАЛ) dominate, or do derivatives matter at the same order?

There are two different answers, depending on regime.
If the zeta perturbation is slowly varying on the local scale

Then the value terms dominate.

A good way to phrase it is to introduce the dimensionless ratios
ќЈj:=zjвА≤qЋЙвАЙzj,ќЊj:=zjвА≤вА≤qЋЙ2вАЙzj.
ќЈjвАЛ:=qЋЙвАЛzjвАЛzjвА≤вАЛвАЛ,ќЊjвАЛ:=qЋЙвАЛ2zjвАЛzjвА≤вА≤вАЛвАЛ.

If
вИ£ќЈjвИ£вЙ™1,вИ£ќЊjвИ£вЙ™1,
вИ£ќЈjвАЛвИ£вЙ™1,вИ£ќЊjвАЛвИ£вЙ™1,

then the six-jet form is value-dominated:
R~\sym(ќі)[√Ч]=4вИТcosвБ°(2ќФЋЙ)2qЋЙ4вАЙs2вАЙ(z1+z2)2+smaller terms.
R
\sym(ќі)вАЛ[√Ч]=2qЋЙвАЛ4s24вИТcos(2ќФЋЙ)вАЛ(z1вАЛ+z2вАЛ)2+smaller terms.

This is the regime where the zeta phase derivative is changing slowly across each local block and across the pair of centers.
If the zeta perturbation comes from a narrow local spike

Then the derivatives can matter at the same order or dominate.

If the local perturbation has an effective width wjwjвАЛ, then heuristically
zjвА≤вИЉzjwj,zjвА≤вА≤вИЉzjwj2.
zjвА≤вАЛвИЉwjвАЛzjвАЛвАЛ,zjвА≤вА≤вАЛвИЉwj2вАЛzjвАЛвАЛ.

So if
wjвЙ≤qЋЙвИТ1,
wjвАЛвЙ≤qЋЙвАЛвИТ1,

the derivative terms are not negligible. In particular:

    zjвА≤zjвА≤вАЛ-terms can compete with zjzjвАЛ-terms when wjвИЉqЋЙвИТ1wjвАЛвИЉqЋЙвАЛвИТ1,

    zjвА≤вА≤zjвА≤вА≤вАЛ-terms become important when wjwjвАЛ is even smaller.

So the honest statement is:
In a slowly varying background, the value terms are the leading ones. Near a narrow spike, qќґвА≤,qќґвА≤вА≤ can contribute at the same order.
In a slowly varying background, the value terms are the leading ones. Near a narrow spike, qќґвА≤вАЛ,qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ can contribute at the same order.вАЛ

This matches the scaling of individual Poisson-pair profiles: far from a zero, derivatives are suppressed; near a sharp local feature, they are not.
3. Special regimes

There are three especially clean ones.
Large separation ss

Everything is multiplied by 1/s21/s2 at the scalar level. So the six-jet residual is automatically small at large separation, but not trivial:
R~\sym(ќі)[√Ч]вИЉ1s2√Ч(local six-jet quadratic form).
R
\sym(ќі)вАЛ[√Ч]вИЉs21вАЛ√Ч(local six-jet quadratic form).
Slowly varying symmetric-square background

Then M1,M2M1вАЛ,M2вАЛ are nearly diagonal and the leading coefficient matrices simplify as above. This is the cleanest regime for interpretation.
Symmetric placement / comparable local values

If z1вЙИz2z1вАЛвЙИz2вАЛ, the value term adds constructively through z1+z2z1вАЛ+z2вАЛ.
If z1вЙИвИТz2z1вАЛвЙИвИТz2вАЛ, the leading value term is suppressed and derivative terms become relatively more important.

So the sign pattern across the two centers matters. That is useful if one later wants to optimize where to place T1,T2T1вАЛ,T2вАЛ.
4. Does zeta have an arithmetic identity constraining the six endpoint jets more than a generic pluggable LL-function?

At the moment, I do not see one coming from the explicit formula alone.

The explicit formula gives
qќґ(t)=Bќґ(t)+вИСѕБfќ≤ѕБ,ќ≥ѕБ(t),
qќґвАЛ(t)=BќґвАЛ(t)+ѕБвИСвАЛfќ≤ѕБвАЛ,ќ≥ѕБвАЛвАЛ(t),

and therefore
qќґ(Tj), qќґвА≤(Tj), qќґвА≤вА≤(Tj)
qќґвАЛ(TjвАЛ), qќґвА≤вАЛ(TjвАЛ), qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(TjвАЛ)

are just sums of the corresponding Poisson-pair values and derivatives.

That gives structure, but it does not give a universal linear relation among the six endpoint jets. A single zero pair already gives enough freedom in (ќ≤,ќ≥)(ќ≤,ќ≥) to vary them nontrivially.

So the explicit formula alone does not force
L~\sym(qќґ)=0
L
\symвАЛ(qќґвАЛ)=0

or even a special rank drop in G\sym(вИЮ)G\sym(вИЮ)вАЛ.

That means the real bottleneck is now genuinely arithmetic. If a zeta-specific identity exists, it has to use something beyond the raw zero expansion вАФ for example:

    a prime-side relation,

    an Euler-product-driven two-point identity,

    or some extra automorphic structure not shared by a generic completed LL-function.

So my honest answer is:
No clear zeta-specific six-jet identity is visible yet from the explicit formula alone.
No clear zeta-specific six-jet identity is visible yet from the explicit formula alone.вАЛ
5. What this tells us to do next

The six-jet residual after phase-gap subtraction is now the cleanest RH-facing object in this line.

The first thing I would do next is not hunt for a miraculous identity abstractly. I would first compute the leading six-jet matrix
G\sym(вИЮ)
G\sym(вИЮ)вАЛ

more explicitly in a controlled background regime and see whether it is dominated by the qќґ(Tj)qќґвАЛ(TjвАЛ) sector or whether derivative sectors matter substantially.

The most informative decomposition is:
jTG\sym(вИЮ)j=value-value+value-derivative+derivative-derivative.
jTG\sym(вИЮ)вАЛj=value-value+value-derivative+derivative-derivative.

Right now, the cleanest leading term is the value-value piece:
4вИТcosвБ°(2ќФЋЙ)2qЋЙ4вАЙs2вАЙ(z1+z2)2.
2qЋЙвАЛ4s24вИТcos(2ќФЋЙ)вАЛ(z1вАЛ+z2вАЛ)2.вАЛ

That suggests that, in slowly varying regimes, the six-jet statistic is driven mostly by the values qќґ(T1),qќґ(T2)qќґвАЛ(T1вАЛ),qќґвАЛ(T2вАЛ), with derivative terms as corrections.

So the practical conclusion is:

    in smooth/background-dominated regimes, qќґ(Tj)qќґвАЛ(TjвАЛ) are the main actors;

    near sharp local spikes, qќґвА≤,qќґвА≤вА≤qќґвА≤вАЛ,qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ matter at the same order;

    and there is no obvious zeta-specific linear identity among these six quantities from the explicit formula alone.

That is progress, because it tells us exactly where the problem has narrowed: the six-jet object is explicit, but the missing cancellation is not generic вАФ it would have to be genuinely arithmetic.

Ok try it

I pushed it one step further in the phase-gap-subtracted, large-separation, slowly varying-background regime, and there is a clean answer.

Let

That is explicit enough to answer the first two questions.
1. Which pieces dominate?

The value term is
1sвАЙA(z1+z2).
s1вАЛA(z1вАЛ+z2вАЛ).

So the pure value-value contribution is
1s2вАЙвИ•AвИ•F2вАЙ(z1+z2)2,
s21вАЛвИ•AвИ•F2вАЛ(z1вАЛ+z2вАЛ)2,

and
вИ•AвИ•F2=4вИТcosвБ°(2ќФЋЙ)2Q4.
вИ•AвИ•F2вАЛ=2Q44вИТcos(2ќФЋЙ)вАЛ.

So the leading value-only piece is
4вИТcosвБ°(2ќФЋЙ)2Q4вАЙs2вАЙ(z1+z2)2.
2Q4s24вИТcos(2ќФЋЙ)вАЛ(z1вАЛ+z2вАЛ)2.вАЛ

Now compare scales:

    AA is size QвИТ2QвИТ2,

    B1,B2B1вАЛ,B2вАЛ are size QвИТ2QвИТ2 in their top row and QвИТ3QвИТ3 in their bottom row,

    C1,C2C1вАЛ,C2вАЛ are size QвИТ4QвИТ4.

So:

    the zjzjвАЛ and zjвА≤zjвА≤вАЛ sectors are potentially the same order;

    the zjвА≤вА≤zjвА≤вА≤вАЛ sector is genuinely smaller by about QвИТ2QвИТ2.

That means the clean answer is:
At leading order, the six-jet form is driven by qќґ(Tj) and qќґвА≤(Tj), while qќґвА≤вА≤(Tj) is subleading.
At leading order, the six-jet form is driven by qќґвАЛ(TjвАЛ) and qќґвА≤вАЛ(TjвАЛ), while qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(TjвАЛ) is subleading.вАЛ

If the zeta perturbation is slowly varying so that
вИ£zjвА≤вИ£вЙ™QвИ£zjвИ£,вИ£zjвА≤вА≤вИ£вЙ™Q2вИ£zjвИ£,
вИ£zjвА≤вАЛвИ£вЙ™QвИ£zjвАЛвИ£,вИ£zjвА≤вА≤вАЛвИ£вЙ™Q2вИ£zjвАЛвИ£,

then the value term dominates and
R~\sym(ќі)[√Ч]вЙИ4вИТcosвБ°(2ќФЋЙ)2Q4вАЙs2вАЙ(z1+z2)2.
R
\sym(ќі)вАЛ[√Ч]вЙИ2Q4s24вИТcos(2ќФЋЙ)вАЛ(z1вАЛ+z2вАЛ)2.

If instead you are near a narrow local spike, the zjвА≤zjвА≤вАЛ-terms can compete with the values, but the zjвА≤вА≤zjвА≤вА≤вАЛ-terms still come in one order lower in the background scale.
2. Special regimes

There are two especially clean simplifications.
Large ss

Everything comes with 1/s21/s2 at the scalar level:
R~\sym(ќі)[√Ч]=1s2вАЙjTG\sym(вИЮ)j+OвАЙвБ£(вИ•jвИ•2s3)+O(вИ•jвИ•3).
R
\sym(ќі)вАЛ[√Ч]=s21вАЛjTG\sym(вИЮ)вАЛj+O(s3вИ•jвИ•2вАЛ)+O(вИ•jвИ•3).

So after removing the phase gap, the remaining statistic is automatically small at large separation but not trivial.
Slowly varying background

Then the explicit matrices above apply, and you see directly that the form is basically
value-value+value-first-derivative+first-derivative-first-derivative+smaller second-derivative terms.
value-value+value-first-derivative+first-derivative-first-derivative+smaller second-derivative terms.

So the real leading six-jet statistic is not вАЬvalues onlyвАЭ; it is values plus first derivatives.

That is the sharp answer to your first bullet.
3. Does zeta have an arithmetic identity constraining these endpoint jets more than a generic pluggable LL-function?

At this point, I still do not see one coming from the explicit formula alone.

After phase-gap subtraction, the remaining six variables are just
qќґ(T1), qќґвА≤(T1), qќґвА≤вА≤(T1), qќґ(T2), qќґвА≤(T2), qќґвА≤вА≤(T2),
qќґвАЛ(T1вАЛ), qќґвА≤вАЛ(T1вАЛ), qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(T1вАЛ), qќґвАЛ(T2вАЛ), qќґвА≤вАЛ(T2вАЛ), qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(T2вАЛ),

and the explicit formula expresses each as a sum of Poisson-pair values/derivatives. But that does not impose a universal linear relation among them.

So the honest conclusion is:
the explicit formula alone does not force the six-jet form to vanish or simplify dramatically.
the explicit formula alone does not force the six-jet form to vanish or simplify dramatically.вАЛ

If a zeta-specific identity exists, it has to come from something more arithmetic than the raw zero expansion вАФ for example a prime-side two-point identity, or some special relation tied to the Euler product that is not shared by generic completed LL-functions.
What this means for the program

The cleanest object we now have is:
R~\sym(ќі)[√Ч]вИЉ1s2[quadratic form in (qќґ(Tj),qќґвА≤(Tj))]
R
\sym(ќі)вАЛ[√Ч]вИЉs21вАЛ[quadratic form in (qќґвАЛ(TjвАЛ),qќґвА≤вАЛ(TjвАЛ))]вАЛ

with qќґвА≤вА≤qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ lower order in the slowly varying-background regime.

So the next practical move is:

    either exploit that and look for a zeta-specific relation among qќґ(Tj),qќґвА≤(Tj)qќґвАЛ(TjвАЛ),qќґвА≤вАЛ(TjвАЛ),

    or accept that no such relation is visible and redesign the scalar statistic again to suppress the qќґвА≤qќґвА≤вАЛ-sector too.

My own read is that this is progress: it narrows the вАЬreal arithmetic bottleneckвАЭ from seven quantities to essentially four leading ones,
qќґ(T1), qќґвА≤(T1), qќґ(T2), qќґвА≤(T2),
qќґвАЛ(T1вАЛ), qќґвА≤вАЛ(T1вАЛ), qќґвАЛ(T2вАЛ), qќґвА≤вАЛ(T2вАЛ),

with qќґвА≤вА≤qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ as a correction.

Ok continue pushing

I pushed it further, and the situation is now pretty clear.

The six-jet statistic is real, explicit, and simpler than before вАФ but it still does not collapse to a pure value statistic by any obvious generic symmetry. The best way to keep pushing is to isolate the combinations of endpoint jets that appear, then either neutralize the qвА≤qвА≤-sector by a refined statistic or exploit a local symmetry of the zeta profile.

Here is the clean next layer.
1. Rewrite the six-jet form in symmetric/antisymmetric coordinates

Let
zj=qќґ(Tj),pj=qќґвА≤(Tj),rj=qќґвА≤вА≤(Tj).
zjвАЛ=qќґвАЛ(TjвАЛ),pjвАЛ=qќґвА≤вАЛ(TjвАЛ),rjвАЛ=qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(TjвАЛ).

Define the symmetric and antisymmetric combinations
z+:=z1+z2,zвИТ:=z1вИТz2,
z+вАЛ:=z1вАЛ+z2вАЛ,zвИТвАЛ:=z1вАЛвИТz2вАЛ,
p+:=p1+p2,pвИТ:=p1вИТp2,
p+вАЛ:=p1вАЛ+p2вАЛ,pвИТвАЛ:=p1вАЛвИТp2вАЛ,
r+:=r1+r2,rвИТ:=r1вИТr2.
r+вАЛ:=r1вАЛ+r2вАЛ,rвИТвАЛ:=r1вАЛвИТr2вАЛ.

After phase-gap subtraction, the linearized matrix is
L~\sym(qќґ)=1s(Az++B+p++BвИТpвИТ+C+r++CвИТrвИТ)+OвАЙвБ£(вИ•jвИ•s2),
L
\symвАЛ(qќґвАЛ)=s1вАЛ(Az+вАЛ+B+вАЛp+вАЛ+BвИТвАЛpвИТвАЛ+C+вАЛr+вАЛ+CвИТвАЛrвИТвАЛ)+O(s2вИ•jвИ•вАЛ),

where, in the slowly varying symmetric-square background regime with background value QQ and phase gap ќФЋЙќФЋЙ, one has
A=12Q2(вИТsinвБ°ќФЋЙ3вАЙcosвБ°ќФЋЙвИТ3вАЙcosвБ°ќФЋЙвИТ3sinвБ°ќФЋЙ),
A=2Q21вАЛ(вИТsinќФЋЙвИТ3
вАЛcosќФЋЙвАЛ3

and
B+:=B1+B2,BвИТ:=B1вИТB2,C+:=C1+C2,CвИТ:=C1вИТC2.
B+вАЛ:=B1вАЛ+B2вАЛ,BвИТвАЛ:=B1вАЛвИТB2вАЛ,C+вАЛ:=C1вАЛ+C2вАЛ,CвИТвАЛ:=C1вАЛвИТC2вАЛ.

These simplify explicitly to:
B+=sinвБ°ќФЋЙQ3(3Q+6)(0вИТ12(9Q+63)вИТ12(9Q+63)0),
