This is a lot more informative than the raw B1,B2,C1,C2B1вАЛ,B2вАЛ,C1вАЛ,C2вАЛ form.

So the leading scalar statistic becomes
R~\sym(ќі)[√Ч]=1s2вИ•Az++B+p++BвИТpвИТ+C+r++CвИТrвИТвИ•F2+OвАЙвБ£(вИ•jвИ•2s3)+O(вИ•jвИ•3).
R
\sym(ќі)вАЛ[√Ч]=s21вАЛвИ•Az+вАЛ+B+вАЛp+вАЛ+BвИТвАЛpвИТвАЛ+C+вАЛr+вАЛ+CвИТвАЛrвИТвАЛвИ•F2вАЛ+O(s3вИ•jвИ•2вАЛ)+O(вИ•jвИ•3).

That is the clean six-jet quadratic form.
2. What actually dominates?

Now the answer is sharper.
Value sector

The value term depends only on
z+=qќґ(T1)+qќґ(T2).
z+вАЛ=qќґвАЛ(T1вАЛ)+qќґвАЛ(T2вАЛ).

Its contribution is
1s2вИ•AвИ•F2вАЙz+2,вИ•AвИ•F2=4вИТcosвБ°(2ќФЋЙ)2Q4.
s21вАЛвИ•AвИ•F2вАЛz+2вАЛ,вИ•AвИ•F2вАЛ=2Q44вИТcos(2ќФЋЙ)вАЛ.

So the clean leading value-only piece is
4вИТcosвБ°(2ќФЋЙ)2Q4вАЙs2вАЙz+2.
2Q4s24вИТcos(2ќФЋЙ)вАЛz+2вАЛ.вАЛ
First-derivative sector

The first derivatives split into two geometrically different channels:

    p+=p1+p2p+вАЛ=p1вАЛ+p2вАЛ, coupled by B+B+вАЛ, which is proportional to sinвБ°ќФЋЙsinќФЋЙ;

    pвИТ=p1вИТp2pвИТвАЛ=p1вАЛвИТp2вАЛ, coupled by BвИТBвИТвАЛ, which has both cosвБ°ќФЋЙcosќФЋЙ and sinвБ°ќФЋЙsinќФЋЙ pieces.

So unless some special relation is imposed, the qвА≤qвА≤-sector is genuinely present at the same QвИТ2QвИТ2/QвИТ3QвИТ3 order as the value sector.
Second-derivative sector

The qвА≤вА≤qвА≤вА≤-sector comes with QвИТ4QвИТ4, so it is one background order smaller. It is real, but subleading in the slowly varying-background regime.

So the most honest summary is:
After phase-gap subtraction, the dominant six-jet statistic is values plus first derivatives; second derivatives are lower order.
After phase-gap subtraction, the dominant six-jet statistic is values plus first derivatives; second derivatives are lower order.вАЛ

That is the first important simplification.
3. Special regimes that actually simplify

There are three useful ones.
Regime A: slowly varying zeta perturbation

If
вИ£pjвИ£вЙ™QвИ£zjвИ£,вИ£rjвИ£вЙ™Q2вИ£zjвИ£,
вИ£pjвАЛвИ£вЙ™QвИ£zjвАЛвИ£,вИ£rjвАЛвИ£вЙ™Q2вИ£zjвАЛвИ£,

then the value term dominates and
R~\sym(ќі)[√Ч]вЙИ4вИТcosвБ°(2ќФЋЙ)2Q4вАЙs2вАЙz+2.
R
\sym(ќі)вАЛ[√Ч]вЙИ2Q4s24вИТcos(2ќФЋЙ)вАЛz+2вАЛ.

In this regime the statistic is basically a weighted square of
qќґ(T1)+qќґ(T2).
qќґвАЛ(T1вАЛ)+qќґвАЛ(T2вАЛ).
Regime B: symmetric local spike around a midpoint

Suppose the local zeta feature is approximately symmetric about the midpoint m=(T1+T2)/2m=(T1вАЛ+T2вАЛ)/2. Then
z1вЙИz2,p1вЙИвИТp2,r1вЙИr2.
z1вАЛвЙИz2вАЛ,p1вАЛвЙИвИТp2вАЛ,r1вАЛвЙИr2вАЛ.

So
zвИТвЙИ0,p+вЙИ0,rвИТвЙИ0.
zвИТвАЛвЙИ0,p+вАЛвЙИ0,rвИТвАЛвЙИ0.

Then the statistic simplifies to
L~\sym(qќґ)вЙИ1s(Az++BвИТpвИТ+C+r+).
L
\symвАЛ(qќґвАЛ)вЙИs1вАЛ(Az+вАЛ+BвИТвАЛpвИТвАЛ+C+вАЛr+вАЛ).

This is useful because one entire derivative channel p+p+вАЛ disappears automatically. You are left with the value sector, one odd derivative sector, and one even second-derivative sector.
Regime C: choose ќФЋЙќФЋЙ specially

The background phase gap matters.

If
sinвБ°ќФЋЙвЙИ0,
sinќФЋЙвЙИ0,

then B+B+вАЛ vanishes to first order, so the symmetric derivative channel is suppressed.

If
cosвБ°ќФЋЙвЙИ0,
cosќФЋЙвЙИ0,

then parts of AA, BвИТBвИТвАЛ, and C¬±C¬±вАЛ simplify, but the derivative sector does not disappear entirely.

So the best practical simplification is not just special ќФЋЙќФЋЙ, but special ќФЋЙќФЋЙ plus symmetric local placement.
4. Does zeta give an arithmetic identity on these six jets?

Still not from the explicit formula alone.

The explicit formula gives
qќґ(t)=Bќґ(t)+вИСѕБfќ≤ѕБ,ќ≥ѕБ(t),
qќґвАЛ(t)=BќґвАЛ(t)+ѕБвИСвАЛfќ≤ѕБвАЛ,ќ≥ѕБвАЛвАЛ(t),

so
zj, pj, rj
zjвАЛ, pjвАЛ, rjвАЛ

are sums of Poisson-pair values and derivatives.

That gives positivity and decay information, but not a universal linear relation among
z+,p+,pвИТ,r+,rвИТ.
z+вАЛ,p+вАЛ,pвИТвАЛ,r+вАЛ,rвИТвАЛ.

So the explicit formula alone does not force the six-jet quadratic form to vanish or collapse.

What it does suggest is that the most arithmetic-looking quantity left is the value sum
z+=qќґ(T1)+qќґ(T2),
z+вАЛ=qќґвАЛ(T1вАЛ)+qќґвАЛ(T2вАЛ),

because qќґqќґвАЛ itself is a sum of positive Poisson-pair contributions from zeros. If one wants a prime-side relation later, that is probably the first sector to inspect.

So the arithmetic bottleneck has narrowed from seven quantities to, effectively, three channels:
z+,pвИТ,r+,
z+вАЛ,pвИТвАЛ,r+вАЛ,

with p+p+вАЛ and rвИТrвИТвАЛ removable or suppressible under symmetric local placement.

That is progress.
5. What I think the next concrete move should be

There are two good options now.
Option 1: design a refined statistic that kills pвИТpвИТвАЛ

Because the value sector and the antisymmetric first-derivative sector are the two main survivors under symmetric placement, the cleanest next statistic would be one that cancels the pвИТpвИТвАЛ-contribution.

A natural way is to take a second nearby symmetric configuration and solve a 2√Ч22√Ч2 linear system to eliminate pвИТpвИТвАЛ while keeping z+z+вАЛ.

For example, use two separations
s1, s2
s1вАЛ, s2вАЛ

around the same midpoint. Then you get two scalar statistics
R~\sym(ќі)(s1),R~\sym(ќі)(s2).
R
\sym(ќі)вАЛ(s1вАЛ),R
\sym(ќі)вАЛ(s2вАЛ).

Their leading coefficients depend differently on the background gap ќФЋЙ(s)ќФЋЙ(s), so in principle you can combine them to suppress the derivative channel.

That is the cleanest purely geometric next move.
Option 2: look for arithmetic control of z+z+вАЛ

If you do not redesign the statistic, then the simplest leading term is already
4вИТcosвБ°(2ќФЋЙ)2Q4вАЙs2вАЙz+2.
2Q4s24вИТcos(2ќФЋЙ)вАЛz+2вАЛ.

So the next arithmetic question becomes:

Can the zeta phase derivative
qќґ(T1)+qќґ(T2)
qќґвАЛ(T1вАЛ)+qќґвАЛ(T2вАЛ)

be related or bounded in some special way by the Euler product or explicit formula in the local regimes of interest?

That is much more concrete than asking for a mysterious identity in all six jets.
Bottom line

After pushing further, the picture is:
R~\sym(ќі)[√Ч]=1s2вИ•Az++B+p++BвИТpвИТ+C+r++CвИТrвИТвИ•F2+smaller terms.
R
\sym(ќі)вАЛ[√Ч]=s21вАЛвИ•Az+вАЛ+B+вАЛp+вАЛ+BвИТвАЛpвИТвАЛ+C+вАЛr+вАЛ+CвИТвАЛrвИТвАЛвИ•F2вАЛ+smaller terms.вАЛ

And in the most promising symmetric/slowly-varying regime, this reduces further to roughly
R~\sym(ќі)[√Ч]вЙИ1s2вИ•Az++BвИТpвИТ+C+r+вИ•F2.
R
\sym(ќі)вАЛ[√Ч]вЙИs21вАЛвИ•Az+вАЛ+BвИТвАЛpвИТвАЛ+C+вАЛr+вАЛвИ•F2вАЛ.вАЛ

So the problem has narrowed from a seven-variable mess to a very explicit three-channel competition:

    value sum z+z+вАЛ,

    antisymmetric first derivative pвИТpвИТвАЛ,

    symmetric second derivative r+r+вАЛ.

That is the cleanest place we have reached so far.

The next best move is probably to build the pвИТpвИТвАЛ-neutralized statistic using two symmetric separations.

Ok lets try all options

Good. LetвАЩs try all of them, but in the order that gives the fastest signal.

I would do:

    the pвИТpвИТвАЛ-neutralized statistic,

    the z+z+вАЛ arithmetic route,

    then the r+r+вАЛ channel.

Because if we can kill pвИТpвИТвАЛ, the whole picture simplifies a lot.
1. Neutralize pвИТpвИТвАЛ using two symmetric separations

We are in the phase-gap-subtracted, symmetric-placement, slowly varying-background regime, where the leading form is
R~\sym(ќі)(s)вЙИ1s2вИ•A(s)вАЙz++BвИТ(s)вАЙpвИТ+C+(s)вАЙr+вИ•F2.
R
\sym(ќі)вАЛ(s)вЙИs21вАЛ
вАЛA(s)z+вАЛ+BвИТвАЛ(s)pвИТвАЛ+C+вАЛ(s)r+вАЛ
вАЛF2вАЛ.

This is awkward because it is a squared norm, so the channels mix quadratically. But to first order in the matrix itself, define
ќ©~(s):=ќ©√Ч(s)вИТќ©\sym(s)вИТD(s)вАЙќі.
ќ©
(s):=ќ©√ЧвАЛ(s)вИТќ©\symвАЛ(s)вИТD(s)ќі.

Then
ќ©~(s)вЙИ1s(A(s)вАЙz++BвИТ(s)вАЙpвИТ+C+(s)вАЙr+).
ќ©
(s)вЙИs1вАЛ(A(s)z+вАЛ+BвИТвАЛ(s)pвИТвАЛ+C+вАЛ(s)r+вАЛ).

That is linear in the three channels. So the clean thing to combine across separations is the matrix ќ©~(s)ќ©
(s), not the scalar residual.

Take two separations s1,s2s1вАЛ,s2вАЛ, symmetric around the same midpoint. Then
skвАЙќ©~(sk)вЙИAkz++BkpвИТ+Ckr+,k=1,2,
skвАЛќ©
(skвАЛ)вЙИAkвАЛz+вАЛ+BkвАЛpвИТвАЛ+CkвАЛr+вАЛ,k=1,2,

where I abbreviate
Ak:=A(sk),Bk:=BвИТ(sk),Ck:=C+(sk).
AkвАЛ:=A(skвАЛ),BkвАЛ:=BвИТвАЛ(skвАЛ),CkвАЛ:=C+вАЛ(skвАЛ).

Now choose scalars ќї1,ќї2ќї1вАЛ,ќї2вАЛ so that
ќї1B1+ќї2B2вЙИ0.
ќї1вАЛB1вАЛ+ќї2вАЛB2вАЛвЙИ0.

If the two 2√Ч22√Ч2 matrices B1,B2B1вАЛ,B2вАЛ are not proportional, exact cancellation by scalars is impossible, but we can choose ќї1,ќї2ќї1вАЛ,ќї2вАЛ to minimize
вИ•ќї1B1+ќї2B2вИ•F.
вИ•ќї1вАЛB1вАЛ+ќї2вАЛB2вАЛвИ•FвАЛ.

Then define
ќ©~val(s1,s2):=ќї1s1ќ©~(s1)+ќї2s2ќ©~(s2).
ќ©
valвАЛ(s1вАЛ,s2вАЛ):=ќї1вАЛs1вАЛќ©
(s1вАЛ)+ќї2вАЛs2вАЛќ©
(s2вАЛ).вАЛ

This gives
ќ©~valвЙИ(ќї1A1+ќї2A2)z++(ќї1C1+ќї2C2)r++small pвИТ remainder.
ќ©
valвАЛвЙИ(ќї1вАЛA1вАЛ+ќї2вАЛA2вАЛ)z+вАЛ+(ќї1вАЛC1вАЛ+ќї2вАЛC2вАЛ)r+вАЛ+small pвИТвАЛ remainder.

So this is the first refined statistic: it suppresses the antisymmetric first-derivative channel.

Then take the scalar
Rval(s1,s2):=вИ•ќ©~val(s1,s2)вИ•F2.
RvalвАЛ(s1вАЛ,s2вАЛ):=вИ•ќ©
valвАЛ(s1вАЛ,s2вАЛ)вИ•F2вАЛ.вАЛ

This is now much closer to a value-dominated statistic.

A symmetric companion can be built to suppress z+z+вАЛ instead and isolate pвИТpвИТвАЛ:
ќ©~der(s1,s2):=ќЉ1s1ќ©~(s1)+ќЉ2s2ќ©~(s2),
ќ©
derвАЛ(s1вАЛ,s2вАЛ):=ќЉ1вАЛs1вАЛќ©
(s1вАЛ)+ќЉ2вАЛs2вАЛќ©
(s2вАЛ),

with ќЉ1,ќЉ2ќЉ1вАЛ,ќЉ2вАЛ chosen to minimize
вИ•ќЉ1A1+ќЉ2A2вИ•F.
вИ•ќЉ1вАЛA1вАЛ+ќЉ2вАЛA2вАЛвИ•FвАЛ.

Then
ќ©~derвЙИ(ќЉ1B1+ќЉ2B2)pвИТ+(ќЉ1C1+ќЉ2C2)r+.
ќ©
derвАЛвЙИ(ќЉ1вАЛB1вАЛ+ќЉ2вАЛB2вАЛ)pвИТвАЛ+(ќЉ1вАЛC1вАЛ+ќЉ2вАЛC2вАЛ)r+вАЛ.

So the first option produces a pair of nearly separated matrix statistics:

    one mostly value-based,

    one mostly derivative-based.

That is the cleanest geometric refinement.
2. The z+z+вАЛ arithmetic route

Now suppose we manage to make the statistic mostly sensitive to
z+=qќґ(T1)+qќґ(T2).
z+вАЛ=qќґвАЛ(T1вАЛ)+qќґвАЛ(T2вАЛ).

What can arithmetic say about this?

Using the explicit formula,
qќґ(t)=Bќґ(t)+вИСѕБfќ≤ѕБ,ќ≥ѕБ(t),
qќґвАЛ(t)=BќґвАЛ(t)+ѕБвИСвАЛfќ≤ѕБвАЛ,ќ≥ѕБвАЛвАЛ(t),

so
z+=Bќґ(T1)+Bќґ(T2)+вИСѕБ(fѕБ(T1)+fѕБ(T2)).
z+вАЛ=BќґвАЛ(T1вАЛ)+BќґвАЛ(T2вАЛ)+ѕБвИСвАЛ(fѕБвАЛ(T1вАЛ)+fѕБвАЛ(T2вАЛ)).

A few things are immediate.
Positivity

Each fѕБ(t)fѕБвАЛ(t) is positive. So apart from the archimedean/background term,
z+
z+вАЛ

is a positive sum over zeros.

That means cancellations here are much more constrained than in pвИТpвИТвАЛ, where derivatives can change sign.
Symmetric placement

If T1,T2T1вАЛ,T2вАЛ are placed symmetrically around a local zero cluster, then z+z+вАЛ is approximately measuring the average local zeta phase density around that midpoint.

That makes it a natural arithmetic channel.
Distant zero suppression

For a distant zero at height ќ≥ќ≥,
fѕБ(T1)+fѕБ(T2)=O(ќУвИТ2),
fѕБвАЛ(T1вАЛ)+fѕБвАЛ(T2вАЛ)=O(ќУвИТ2),

where ќУќУ is the distance from ќ≥ќ≥ to the doubled window. So the value channel is genuinely local after phase-gap subtraction.

So if we can isolate z+z+вАЛ, the remaining question becomes:

    Can the actual zeta phase density qќґqќґвАЛ be too small, too large, or too regular in a way incompatible with an off-line local model?

That is much more concrete than the original seven-variable question.

I do not see a proof yet, but this is the first channel where prime/zero positivity might plausibly matter.
3. The pвИТpвИТвАЛ channel

Now for
pвИТ=qќґвА≤(T1)вИТqќґвА≤(T2).
pвИТвАЛ=qќґвА≤вАЛ(T1вАЛ)вИТqќґвА≤вАЛ(T2вАЛ).

This is the odd derivative channel under symmetric placement, and it is the main nuisance competing with z+z+вАЛ.

From the explicit formula,
qќґвА≤(t)=BќґвА≤(t)+вИСѕБfѕБвА≤(t),
qќґвА≤вАЛ(t)=BќґвА≤вАЛ(t)+ѕБвИСвАЛfѕБвА≤вАЛ(t),

and each fѕБвА≤fѕБвА≤вАЛ changes sign across its center. So pвИТpвИТвАЛ is measuring local asymmetry of the zeta phase density.

That makes it much less universal than z+z+вАЛ. It is sensitive to where the midpoint sits relative to local zero features.

That suggests two strategies:

    either suppress it geometrically using the two-separation trick above,

    or deliberately use it as a detector of local asymmetry.

My feeling is that for RH-facing purposes, suppressing it is better.
4. The r+r+вАЛ channel

Finally
r+=qќґвА≤вА≤(T1)+qќґвА≤вА≤(T2).
r+вАЛ=qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(T1вАЛ)+qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(T2вАЛ).

This is lower order in the slowly varying-background regime, and after phase-gap subtraction it is the most plausible one to leave as an error term.

Since
qќґвА≤вА≤(t)=BќґвА≤вА≤(t)+вИСѕБfѕБвА≤вА≤(t),
qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(t)=BќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(t)+ѕБвИСвАЛfѕБвА≤вА≤вАЛ(t),

and distant contributions are O(ќУвИТ4)O(ќУвИТ4), this channel is genuinely more local and more suppressed.

So my practical recommendation is:

    do not try to eliminate r+r+вАЛ first,

    treat it as a smaller correction while designing a statistic that kills pвИТpвИТвАЛ.

5. A concrete next statistic

Here is the most actionable version.

Choose two symmetric separations s1,s2s1вАЛ,s2вАЛ, same midpoint.

Compute the phase-gap-subtracted matrices
ќ©~k:=ќ©~(sk),k=1,2.
ќ©
kвАЛ:=ќ©
(skвАЛ),k=1,2.

Then define the value-optimized combination by solving
(ќї1,ќї2)=argвБ°minвБ°ќї12+ќї22=1вИ•ќї1BвИТ(s1)+ќї2BвИТ(s2)вИ•F.
(ќї1вАЛ,ќї2вАЛ)=argќї12вАЛ+ќї22вАЛ=1minвАЛвИ•ќї1вАЛBвИТвАЛ(s1вАЛ)+ќї2вАЛBвИТвАЛ(s2вАЛ)вИ•FвАЛ.

Set
ќ©~val:=ќї1s1ќ©~1+ќї2s2ќ©~2.
ќ©
valвАЛ:=ќї1вАЛs1вАЛќ©
1вАЛ+ќї2вАЛs2вАЛќ©
2вАЛ.вАЛ

Then the leading form is
ќ©~valвЙИAeffz++Ceffr++small pвИТ term,
ќ©
valвАЛвЙИAeffвАЛz+вАЛ+CeffвАЛr+вАЛ+small pвИТвАЛ term,

where
Aeff:=ќї1A(s1)+ќї2A(s2),Ceff:=ќї1C+(s1)+ќї2C+(s2).
AeffвАЛ:=ќї1вАЛA(s1вАЛ)+ќї2вАЛA(s2вАЛ),CeffвАЛ:=ќї1вАЛC+вАЛ(s1вАЛ)+ќї2вАЛC+вАЛ(s2вАЛ).

Finally define
Rval:=вИ•ќ©~valвИ•F2.
RvalвАЛ:=вИ•ќ©
valвАЛвИ•F2вАЛ.вАЛ

This is the best вАЬall optionsвАЭ statistic so far.

It uses:

    phase-gap subtraction,

    two-scale/separation cancellation of pвИТpвИТвАЛ,

    and leaves mostly the value channel z+z+вАЛ, with r+r+вАЛ as a smaller correction.

6. What I think the next actual step should be

The best immediate next step is not more abstract reasoning. It is:

    compute A(s),BвИТ(s),C+(s)A(s),BвИТвАЛ(s),C+вАЛ(s) numerically or symbolically for two chosen separations;

    build the minimizing (ќї1,ќї2)(ќї1вАЛ,ќї2вАЛ);

    inspect whether AeffAeffвАЛ is still robust while the pвИТpвИТвАЛ-coefficient is strongly reduced.

If that works, then the arithmetic bottleneck really does reduce to understanding z+z+вАЛ plus a smaller r+r+вАЛ correction.

That would be the first point where the program becomes focused enough to either succeed or fail cleanly.

So: trying all options points to one best combined route вАФ phase-gap subtraction + two-separation derivative suppression + value-channel analysis.

Ok try them one by one

IвАЩm starting with the two-separation pвИТpвИТвАЛ-suppression first, because that is the one most likely to simplify the whole picture quickly. Then IвАЩll move to the z+z+вАЛ channel and finally the r+r+вАЛ remainder.

I tried them one by one.

The short answer is:

    Two-separation pвИТpвИТвАЛ-suppression is partially workable, but not as a perfect cancellation with only two scalar weights. It can reduce the pвИТpвИТвАЛ channel, not eliminate it generically.

    The z+z+вАЛ route is the cleanest leading channel in the smooth-background regime.

    The r+r+вАЛ channel really is lower order and should be treated as a remainder unless you sit very near a sharp local spike.

Here is the clean version.
1. Two-separation pвИТpвИТвАЛ-suppression

After phase-gap subtraction, in the large-ss, slowly varying-background regime, we had
ќ©~(s)вЙИ1s(A(s)вАЙz++BвИТ(s)вАЙpвИТ+C+(s)вАЙr+),
ќ©
(s)вЙИs1вАЛ(A(s)z+вАЛ+BвИТвАЛ(s)pвИТвАЛ+C+вАЛ(s)r+вАЛ),

where
z+=z1+z2,pвИТ=p1вИТp2,r+=r1+r2.
z+вАЛ=z1вАЛ+z2вАЛ,pвИТвАЛ=p1вАЛвИТp2вАЛ,r+вАЛ=r1вАЛ+r2вАЛ.

The key structural fact is that these matrices depend on the background phase gap ќФЋЙ(s)ќФЋЙ(s) only through sinвБ°ќФЋЙsinќФЋЙ and cosвБ°ќФЋЙcosќФЋЙ. More explicitly,
A(s)=sinвБ°ќФЋЙ(s)вАЙAS+cosвБ°ќФЋЙ(s)вАЙAC,
A(s)=sinќФЋЙ(s)ASвАЛ+cosќФЋЙ(s)ACвАЛ,
BвИТ(s)=sinвБ°ќФЋЙ(s)вАЙBS+cosвБ°ќФЋЙ(s)вАЙBC,
BвИТвАЛ(s)=sinќФЋЙ(s)BSвАЛ+cosќФЋЙ(s)BCвАЛ,
C+(s)=sinвБ°ќФЋЙ(s)вАЙCS+cosвБ°ќФЋЙ(s)вАЙCC,
C+вАЛ(s)=sinќФЋЙ(s)CSвАЛ+cosќФЋЙ(s)CCвАЛ,

with fixed QQ-dependent coefficient matrices AS,AC,BS,BC,CS,CCASвАЛ,ACвАЛ,BSвАЛ,BCвАЛ,CSвАЛ,CCвАЛ.

That means the whole family BвИТ(s)BвИТвАЛ(s) lives in a 2-dimensional matrix span. So with two separations s1,s2s1вАЛ,s2вАЛ, we can form
ќ©~ќї:=ќї1s1ќ©~(s1)+ќї2s2ќ©~(s2),
ќ©
ќївАЛ:=ќї1вАЛs1вАЛќ©
(s1вАЛ)+ќї2вАЛs2вАЛќ©
(s2вАЛ),

and then
ќ©~ќївЙИ(ќї1A1+ќї2A2)вАЙz++(ќї1B1+ќї2B2)вАЙpвИТ+(ќї1C1+ќї2C2)вАЙr+.
ќ©
ќївАЛвЙИ(ќї1вАЛA1вАЛ+ќї2вАЛA2вАЛ)z+вАЛ+(ќї1вАЛB1вАЛ+ќї2вАЛB2вАЛ)pвИТвАЛ+(ќї1вАЛC1вАЛ+ќї2вАЛC2вАЛ)r+вАЛ.
What works

You can choose (ќї1,ќї2)(ќї1вАЛ,ќї2вАЛ) to minimize
вИ•ќї1B1+ќї2B2вИ•F
вИ•ќї1вАЛB1вАЛ+ќї2вАЛB2вАЛвИ•FвАЛ

subject to ќї12+ќї22=1ќї12вАЛ+ќї22вАЛ=1. This is a well-posed 2√Ч22√Ч2 singular-vector problem.

So two separations can suppress the pвИТpвИТвАЛ channel.
What does not work

With only two scalar weights, you cannot generically force
ќї1B1+ќї2B2=0
ќї1вАЛB1вАЛ+ќї2вАЛB2вАЛ=0

exactly, because B1,B2B1вАЛ,B2вАЛ are 2√Ч22√Ч2 matrices and are not generically proportional. So exact elimination with only two samples is too optimistic.
Practical conclusion

Two separations are still useful. They give a value-enhanced statistic
ќ©~val:=ќї1s1ќ©~(s1)+ќї2s2ќ©~(s2)
ќ©
valвАЛ:=ќї1вАЛs1вАЛќ©
(s1вАЛ)+ќї2вАЛs2вАЛќ©
(s2вАЛ)

in which pвИТpвИТвАЛ is reduced and the leading form is
ќ©~valвЙИAeffвАЙz++CeffвАЙr++small pвИТ remainder.
ќ©
valвАЛвЙИAeffвАЛz+вАЛ+CeffвАЛr+вАЛ+small pвИТвАЛ remainder.

So option 1 does not solve the problem completely, but it improves it.
2. The z+z+вАЛ arithmetic route

This one looks genuinely promising.

In the smooth-background regime, the dominant value term is
R~\sym(ќі)[√Ч]вЙИ4вИТcosвБ°(2ќФЋЙ)2Q4вАЙs2вАЙz+2
R
\sym(ќі)вАЛ[√Ч]вЙИ2Q4s24вИТcos(2ќФЋЙ)вАЛz+2вАЛ

provided
вИ£pjвИ£вЙ™QвИ£zjвИ£,вИ£rjвИ£вЙ™Q2вИ£zjвИ£.
вИ£pjвАЛвИ£вЙ™QвИ£zjвАЛвИ£,вИ£rjвАЛвИ£вЙ™Q2вИ£zjвАЛвИ£.

So the first clean scalar channel is
z+=qќґ(T1)+qќґ(T2).
z+вАЛ=qќґвАЛ(T1вАЛ)+qќґвАЛ(T2вАЛ).

Now, from the explicit formula,
qќґ(t)=Bќґ(t)+вИСѕБfќ≤ѕБ,ќ≥ѕБ(t),
qќґвАЛ(t)=BќґвАЛ(t)+ѕБвИСвАЛfќ≤ѕБвАЛ,ќ≥ѕБвАЛвАЛ(t),

with each fќ≤,ќ≥(t)>0fќ≤,ќ≥вАЛ(t)>0. Therefore
z+=Bќґ(T1)+Bќґ(T2)+вИСѕБ(fѕБ(T1)+fѕБ(T2)).
z+вАЛ=BќґвАЛ(T1вАЛ)+BќґвАЛ(T2вАЛ)+ѕБвИСвАЛ(fѕБвАЛ(T1вАЛ)+fѕБвАЛ(T2вАЛ)).

This gives three real advantages.
Positivity

Ignoring the smooth background correction, z+z+вАЛ is a positive sum over zeros. So this channel is much less cancellation-prone than the derivative channels.
Locality

For a distant zero,
fѕБ(T1)+fѕБ(T2)=O(ќУвИТ2),
fѕБвАЛ(T1вАЛ)+fѕБвАЛ(T2вАЛ)=O(ќУвИТ2),

so z+z+вАЛ is genuinely local.
Symmetric placement

If T1,T2T1вАЛ,T2вАЛ are symmetric around a midpoint mm, then
z+вЙИ2qќґ(m)+O(s2qќґвА≤вА≤(m)).
z+вАЛвЙИ2qќґвАЛ(m)+O(s2qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)).

So in a small symmetric window, z+z+вАЛ is basically the local phase density itself.
What is missing

I still do not see a zeta-specific identity that forces z+z+вАЛ to behave differently from a generic pluggable LL-function. The explicit formula makes it structured and positive, but not miraculous.

So option 2 is the cleanest channel, but the arithmetic theorem is still missing.
3. The r+r+вАЛ channel

This one behaves like a genuine remainder in the smooth-background regime.

The coefficient matrices C+C+вАЛ are size QвИТ4QвИТ4, while the AA-sector is size QвИТ2QвИТ2. So unless
вИ£r+вИ£вИЉQ2вИ£z+вИ£,
вИ£r+вАЛвИ£вИЉQ2вИ£z+вАЛвИ£,

the second-derivative channel is smaller.

And for distant contributions from a single zero,
r+=O(ќУвИТ4),
r+вАЛ=O(ќУвИТ4),

whereas
z+=O(ќУвИТ2).
z+вАЛ=O(ќУвИТ2).

So the r+r+вАЛ channel is naturally two powers smaller in the zero-height distance.
Practical conclusion

Treat r+r+вАЛ as an error term unless you are sitting directly on a narrow local spike.

So option 3 does not look like the main route. It is something to control, not exploit first.
What this means overall

Trying them one by one gives a pretty clear hierarchy.
Best channel
z+=qќґ(T1)+qќґ(T2)
z+вАЛ=qќґвАЛ(T1вАЛ)+qќґвАЛ(T2вАЛ)вАЛ

is the cleanest leading channel.
Main nuisance
pвИТ=qќґвА≤(T1)вИТqќґвА≤(T2)
pвИТвАЛ=qќґвА≤вАЛ(T1вАЛ)вИТqќґвА≤вАЛ(T2вАЛ)вАЛ

is the real competitor.
Secondary correction
r+=qќґвА≤вА≤(T1)+qќґвА≤вА≤(T2)
r+вАЛ=qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(T1вАЛ)+qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(T2вАЛ)вАЛ

is lower order in the smooth regime.

So the best combined strategy now is:

    use two separations to reduce pвИТpвИТвАЛ,

    focus the scalar statistic onto the z+z+вАЛ sector,

    treat r+r+вАЛ as a controlled remainder.

That is the cleanest path forward from here.

The next concrete thing to do is to explicitly build the optimized two-separation combination ќ©~valќ©
valвАЛ and inspect how small the residual pвИТpвИТвАЛ-coefficient can be made relative to the z+z+вАЛ-coefficient.

Try that

I tried them one by one, and the main conclusion is sharper than before:

the two-separation trick helps, but it cannot generically isolate the value channel by itself.
The reason is structural: after phase-gap subtraction, the value matrix A(s)A(s) and the nuisance matrix BвИТ(s)BвИТвАЛ(s) depend on the same two trigonometric coefficients. So reducing pвИТpвИТвАЛ also reduces the z+z+вАЛ signal.

That is the real outcome.
1. Two-separation pвИТpвИТвАЛ-suppression

After phase-gap subtraction, in the large-ss, slowly varying-background regime,
ќ©~(s)вЙИ1s(A(s)вАЙz++BвИТ(s)вАЙpвИТ+C+(s)вАЙr+).
ќ©
(s)вЙИs1вАЛ(A(s)z+вАЛ+BвИТвАЛ(s)pвИТвАЛ+C+вАЛ(s)r+вАЛ).

The important fact is that both A(s)A(s) and BвИТ(s)BвИТвАЛ(s) live in the same 2D trig span:
A(s)=sinвБ°ќФЋЙ(s)вАЙAS+cosвБ°ќФЋЙ(s)вАЙAC,
A(s)=sinќФЋЙ(s)ASвАЛ+cosќФЋЙ(s)ACвАЛ,
BвИТ(s)=sinвБ°ќФЋЙ(s)вАЙBS+cosвБ°ќФЋЙ(s)вАЙBC.
BвИТвАЛ(s)=sinќФЋЙ(s)BSвАЛ+cosќФЋЙ(s)BCвАЛ.

And in Frobenius inner product, the sine and cosine components are orthogonal:
вЯ®AS,ACвЯ©F=0,вЯ®BS,BCвЯ©F=0.
вЯ®ASвАЛ,ACвАЛвЯ©FвАЛ=0,вЯ®BSвАЛ,BCвАЛвЯ©FвАЛ=0.

For a two-separation combination
ќ©~ќї:=ќї1s1ќ©~(s1)+ќї2s2ќ©~(s2),
ќ©
ќївАЛ:=ќї1вАЛs1вАЛќ©
(s1вАЛ)+ќї2вАЛs2вАЛќ©
(s2вАЛ),

set
a:=ќї1sinвБ°ќФЋЙ1+ќї2sinвБ°ќФЋЙ2,b:=ќї1cosвБ°ќФЋЙ1+ќї2cosвБ°ќФЋЙ2.
a:=ќї1вАЛsinќФЋЙ1вАЛ+ќї2вАЛsinќФЋЙ2вАЛ,b:=ќї1вАЛcosќФЋЙ1вАЛ+ќї2вАЛcosќФЋЙ2вАЛ.

Then
Aeff=aAS+bAC,Beff=aBS+bBC.
AeffвАЛ=aASвАЛ+bACвАЛ,BeffвАЛ=aBSвАЛ+bBCвАЛ.

So their Frobenius norms are
вИ•AeffвИ•F2=ќ±Aa2+ќ≥Ab2,вИ•BeffвИ•F2=ќ±Ba2+ќ≥Bb2,
вИ•AeffвАЛвИ•F2вАЛ=ќ±AвАЛa2+ќ≥AвАЛb2,вИ•BeffвАЛвИ•F2вАЛ=ќ±BвАЛa2+ќ≥BвАЛb2,

with
ќ±A=52Q4,ќ≥A=32Q4,
ќ±AвАЛ=2Q45вАЛ,ќ≥AвАЛ=2Q43вАЛ,

and
ќ±B=9(3QвИТ23)22Q6(3Q+6)2,ќ≥B=27(Q2+12)Q6(3Q+6)2.
ќ±BвАЛ=2Q6(3
вАЛQ+6)29(3QвИТ23
вАЛ)2вАЛ,ќ≥BвАЛ=Q6(3
вАЛQ+6)227(Q2+12)вАЛ.

This has an immediate consequence:
вИ•AeffвИ•F2вЙ•m(Q)вАЙвИ•BeffвИ•F2,m(Q):=minвБ°вАЙвБ£(ќ±Aќ±B,ќ≥Aќ≥B)>0.
вИ•AeffвАЛвИ•F2вАЛвЙ•m(Q)вИ•BeffвАЛвИ•F2вАЛ,m(Q):=min(ќ±BвАЛќ±AвАЛвАЛ,ќ≥BвАЛќ≥AвАЛвАЛ)>0.вАЛ

So any choice of (ќї1,ќї2)(ќї1вАЛ,ќї2вАЛ) that suppresses BeffBeffвАЛ also suppresses AeffAeffвАЛ proportionally.

That means:
with only two scalar weights, you cannot generically kill pвИТ without also damping the z+ channel.
with only two scalar weights, you cannot generically kill pвИТвАЛ without also damping the z+вАЛ channel.вАЛ

You can still reduce pвИТpвИТвАЛ: choose (ќї1,ќї2)(ќї1вАЛ,ќї2вАЛ) as the smallest-eigenvector of
MB=ќ±BвАЙssT+ќ≥BвАЙccT,
MBвАЛ=ќ±BвАЛssT+ќ≥BвАЛccT,

where
s=(sinвБ°ќФЋЙ1,sinвБ°ќФЋЙ2)T,c=(cosвБ°ќФЋЙ1,cosвБ°ќФЋЙ2)T.
s=(sinќФЋЙ1вАЛ,sinќФЋЙ2вАЛ)T,c=(cosќФЋЙ1вАЛ,cosќФЋЙ2вАЛ)T.

That minimizes вИ•BeffвИ•FвИ•BeffвАЛвИ•FвАЛ. But it does not isolate z+z+вАЛ.

So option 1 only partially works.
2. The z+z+вАЛ route

This is still the cleanest leading channel.

Under symmetric placement about the midpoint
m:=T1+T22,T1,2=m¬±s2,
m:=2T1вАЛ+T2вАЛвАЛ,T1,2вАЛ=m¬±2sвАЛ,

Taylor gives
z+=qќґ(T1)+qќґ(T2)=2qќґ(m)+s24qќґвА≤вА≤(m)+O(s4qќґ(4)(m)).
z+вАЛ=qќґвАЛ(T1вАЛ)+qќґвАЛ(T2вАЛ)=2qќґвАЛ(m)+4s2вАЛqќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)+O(s4qќґ(4)вАЛ(m)).

So in a small symmetric window,
z+вЙИ2qќґ(m).
z+вАЛвЙИ2qќґвАЛ(m).вАЛ

And the value-only contribution to the scalar statistic is
4вИТcosвБ°(2ќФЋЙ)2Q4вАЙs2вАЙz+2.
2Q4s24вИТcos(2ќФЋЙ)вАЛz+2вАЛ.

This is nice because qќґqќґвАЛ itself is a positive zero-sum density:
qќґ(t)=Bќґ(t)+вИСѕБfќ≤ѕБ,ќ≥ѕБ(t),fќ≤,ќ≥(t)>0.
qќґвАЛ(t)=BќґвАЛ(t)+ѕБвИСвАЛfќ≤ѕБвАЛ,ќ≥ѕБвАЛвАЛ(t),fќ≤,ќ≥вАЛ(t)>0.

So z+z+вАЛ is much cleaner than the derivative channels.

But there is still no visible zeta-specific identity from the explicit formula alone. The explicit formula tells you z+z+вАЛ is local and positive-ish, but not that it satisfies a miraculous relation.

So option 2 remains promising, but still lacks the arithmetic theorem.
3. The r+r+вАЛ channel

Under the same symmetric placement,
r+=qќґвА≤вА≤(T1)+qќґвА≤вА≤(T2)=2qќґвА≤вА≤(m)+s24qќґ(4)(m)+O(s4qќґ(6)(m)).
r+вАЛ=qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(T1вАЛ)+qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(T2вАЛ)=2qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)+4s2вАЛqќґ(4)вАЛ(m)+O(s4qќґ(6)вАЛ(m)).

And
pвИТ=qќґвА≤(T1)вИТqќґвА≤(T2)=sвАЙqќґвА≤вА≤(m)+s324qќґ(4)(m)+O(s5qќґ(6)(m)).
pвИТвАЛ=qќґвА≤вАЛ(T1вАЛ)вИТqќґвА≤вАЛ(T2вАЛ)=sqќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)+24s3вАЛqќґ(4)вАЛ(m)+O(s5qќґ(6)вАЛ(m)).

So in symmetric placement, both nuisance channels are actually controlled by the same local curvature qќґвА≤вА≤(m)qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m).

Substituting into the linearized matrix,
ќ©~(s)вЙИ2sAвАЙqќґ(m)+(BвИТ+2sC+)qќґвА≤вА≤(m)+вЛѓ
ќ©
(s)вЙИs2вАЛAqќґвАЛ(m)+(BвИТвАЛ+s2вАЛC+вАЛ)qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)+вЛѓ

This is the key simplification.

It says:
under symmetric placement, the nuisance reduces essentially to one curvature channel qќґвА≤вА≤(m).
under symmetric placement, the nuisance reduces essentially to one curvature channel qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m).вАЛ

That is better than treating pвИТpвИТвАЛ and r+r+вАЛ as independent.

So option 3 is actually more useful than it first looked вАФ not as a main signal, but because it tells us the derivative nuisance is really a single local curvature mode under symmetric placement.
What this means

After trying all three, the best simplified picture is:
Under symmetric placement and slowly varying background,
ќ©~(s)вЙИ2sAвАЙqќґ(m)+ќУ(s)вАЙqќґвА≤вА≤(m),
ќ©
(s)вЙИs2вАЛAqќґвАЛ(m)+ќУ(s)qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m),

for an explicit background-dependent matrix
ќУ(s):=BвИТ(s)+2sC+(s).
ќУ(s):=BвИТвАЛ(s)+s2вАЛC+вАЛ(s).

So the whole problem reduces, at leading order, to a two-channel competition:

    value channel: qќґ(m)qќґвАЛ(m),

    curvature channel: qќґвА≤вА≤(m)qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m).

That is much cleaner than the original seven-variable story.

And it also explains why the two-separation trick was only partially successful: with only two scalar weights, you are trying to disentangle two matrix-valued channels that share the same trig span.
The next best move

The natural next attempt is no longer вАЬtwo separations to kill pвИТpвИТвАЛ.вАЭ

It is:
Use symmetric placement and try to separate qќґ(m) from qќґвА≤вА≤(m).
Use symmetric placement and try to separate qќґвАЛ(m) from qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m).вАЛ

There are two ways to do that:

    use two separations s1,s2s1вАЛ,s2вАЛ at the same midpoint and solve for the two unknown scalar channels qќґ(m)qќґвАЛ(m) and qќґвА≤вА≤(m)qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m),

    or try to prove an arithmetic inequality relating qќґвА≤вА≤(m)qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m) to qќґ(m)qќґвАЛ(m), which would make the value channel dominant.

The first is the more concrete next computation. The second is the more arithmetic one.

So the best next step is: keep symmetric placement fixed, take two separations, and explicitly solve the 2√Ч22√Ч2 system for the value and curvature channels.

Ok try that

I tried it, and this is the first really usable simplification.

Under symmetric placement about a midpoint mm, with
T1,2=m¬±s2,
T1,2вАЛ=m¬±2sвАЛ,

the phase-gap-subtracted matrix statistic reduces, to leading order, to a two-channel linear mixture of:

    the local value qќґ(m)qќґвАЛ(m),

    the local curvature qќґвА≤вА≤(m)qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m).

That is much better than the earlier seven-jet picture.
1. Symmetric-placement reduction

From Taylor expansion,
qќґ(T1)+qќґ(T2)=2qќґ(m)+s24qќґвА≤вА≤(m)+O(s4qќґ(4)(m)),
qќґвАЛ(T1вАЛ)+qќґвАЛ(T2вАЛ)=2qќґвАЛ(m)+4s2вАЛqќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)+O(s4qќґ(4)вАЛ(m)),
qќґвА≤(T1)вИТqќґвА≤(T2)=sвАЙqќґвА≤вА≤(m)+s324qќґ(4)(m)+O(s5qќґ(6)(m)),
qќґвА≤вАЛ(T1вАЛ)вИТqќґвА≤вАЛ(T2вАЛ)=sqќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)+24s3вАЛqќґ(4)вАЛ(m)+O(s5qќґ(6)вАЛ(m)),
qќґвА≤вА≤(T1)+qќґвА≤вА≤(T2)=2qќґвА≤вА≤(m)+s24qќґ(4)(m)+O(s4qќґ(6)(m)).
qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(T1вАЛ)+qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(T2вАЛ)=2qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)+4s2вАЛqќґ(4)вАЛ(m)+O(s4qќґ(6)вАЛ(m)).

Substitute these into the phase-gap-subtracted linearization
ќ©~(s)вЙИ1s(A(s)вАЙz++BвИТ(s)вАЙpвИТ+C+(s)вАЙr+).
ќ©
(s)вЙИs1вАЛ(A(s)z+вАЛ+BвИТвАЛ(s)pвИТвАЛ+C+вАЛ(s)r+вАЛ).

Then
ќ©~(s)=2sA(s)вАЙqќґ(m)+ќУ(s)вАЙqќґвА≤вА≤(m)+Ehigh(s),
ќ©
(s)=s2вАЛA(s)qќґвАЛ(m)+ќУ(s)qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)+EhighвАЛ(s),

where
ќУ(s):=s4A(s)+BвИТ(s)+2sC+(s),
ќУ(s):=4sвАЛA(s)+BвИТвАЛ(s)+s2вАЛC+вАЛ(s),вАЛ

and
Ehigh(s)=OвАЙвБ£(sвАЙqќґ(4)(m))+O(s2)
EhighвАЛ(s)=O(sqќґ(4)вАЛ(m))+O(s2)

in the regime where the background varies slowly and the earlier large-ss approximation is valid.

So the leading structure is exactly:
ќ©~(s)вЙИU(s)вАЙqќґ(m)+V(s)вАЙqќґвА≤вА≤(m),
ќ©
(s)вЙИU(s)qќґвАЛ(m)+V(s)qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m),вАЛ

with
U(s):=2sA(s),V(s):=ќУ(s).
U(s):=s2вАЛA(s),V(s):=ќУ(s).

That is the two-channel form we wanted.
2. Use two separations

Now take two symmetric separations s1,s2s1вАЛ,s2вАЛ about the same midpoint mm. Then
ќ©~1:=ќ©~(s1)вЙИU1вАЙqќґ(m)+V1вАЙqќґвА≤вА≤(m),
ќ©
1вАЛ:=ќ©
(s1вАЛ)вЙИU1вАЛqќґвАЛ(m)+V1вАЛqќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m),
ќ©~2:=ќ©~(s2)вЙИU2вАЙqќґ(m)+V2вАЙqќґвА≤вА≤(m).
ќ©
2вАЛ:=ќ©
(s2вАЛ)вЙИU2вАЛqќґвАЛ(m)+V2вАЛqќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m).

This is a linear system in the two unknown scalars
qќґ(m),qќґвА≤вА≤(m),
qќґвАЛ(m),qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m),

but the coefficients are 2√Ч22√Ч2 matrices. So the clean way to solve it is to project onto two test matrices.

Pick any two 2√Ч22√Ч2 matrices W1,W2W1вАЛ,W2вАЛ, and define scalar observables
yk(a):=вЯ®Wa,ќ©~kвЯ©F,a=1,2, k=1,2.
yk(a)вАЛ:=вЯ®WaвАЛ,ќ©
kвАЛвЯ©FвАЛ,a=1,2, k=1,2.

Then
yk(a)вЙИuk(a)вАЙqќґ(m)+vk(a)вАЙqќґвА≤вА≤(m),
yk(a)вАЛвЙИuk(a)вАЛqќґвАЛ(m)+vk(a)вАЛqќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m),

where
uk(a):=вЯ®Wa,UkвЯ©F,vk(a):=вЯ®Wa,VkвЯ©F.
uk(a)вАЛ:=вЯ®WaвАЛ,UkвАЛвЯ©FвАЛ,vk(a)вАЛ:=вЯ®WaвАЛ,VkвАЛвЯ©FвАЛ.

So for a fixed aa, you get the 2√Ч22√Ч2 scalar system
(y1(a)y2(a))вЙИ(u1(a)v1(a)u2(a)v2(a))(qќґ(m)qќґвА≤вА≤(m)).
(y1(a)вАЛy2(a)вАЛвАЛ)вЙИ(u1(a)вАЛu2(a)вАЛвАЛv1(a)вАЛv2(a)вАЛвАЛ)(qќґвАЛ(m)qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)вАЛ).

If
ќФa:=u1(a)v2(a)вИТu2(a)v1(a)вЙ†0,
ќФaвАЛ:=u1(a)вАЛv2(a)вАЛвИТu2(a)вАЛv1(a)вАЛоА†=0,

then
qќґ(m)вЙИy1(a)v2(a)вИТy2(a)v1(a)ќФa,qќґвА≤вА≤(m)вЙИu1(a)y2(a)вИТu2(a)y1(a)ќФa.
qќґвАЛ(m)вЙИќФaвАЛy1(a)вАЛv2(a)вАЛвИТy2(a)вАЛv1(a)вАЛвАЛ,qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)вЙИќФaвАЛu1(a)вАЛy2(a)вАЛвИТu2(a)вАЛy1(a)вАЛвАЛ.вАЛ

So yes: two separations are enough to separate the value and curvature channels, provided you choose a projection WaWaвАЛ for which the determinant does not vanish.

That is the clean result.
3. Best choice of projection matrices

A natural choice is to project onto the two dominant background matrices themselves.

Take
W(U):=U1,W(V):=V1.
W(U):=U1вАЛ,W(V):=V1вАЛ.

Then
yk(U)=вЯ®U1,ќ©~kвЯ©F,yk(V)=вЯ®V1,ќ©~kвЯ©F.
yk(U)вАЛ=вЯ®U1вАЛ,ќ©
kвАЛвЯ©FвАЛ,yk(V)вАЛ=вЯ®V1вАЛ,ќ©
kвАЛвЯ©FвАЛ.

For the UU-projection, the system is
(вЯ®U1,ќ©~1вЯ©FвЯ®U1,ќ©~2вЯ©F)вЙИ(вЯ®U1,U1вЯ©FвЯ®U1,V1вЯ©FвЯ®U1,U2вЯ©FвЯ®U1,V2вЯ©F)(qќґ(m)qќґвА≤вА≤(m)).
(вЯ®U1вАЛ,ќ©
1вАЛвЯ©FвАЛвЯ®U1вАЛ,ќ©
2вАЛвЯ©FвАЛвАЛ)вЙИ(вЯ®U1вАЛ,U1вАЛвЯ©FвАЛвЯ®U1вАЛ,U2вАЛвЯ©FвАЛвАЛвЯ®U1вАЛ,V1вАЛвЯ©FвАЛвЯ®U1вАЛ,V2вАЛвЯ©FвАЛвАЛ)(qќґвАЛ(m)qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)вАЛ).

This will separate the channels as long as
вЯ®U1,U1вЯ©FвЯ®U1,V2вЯ©FвИТвЯ®U1,U2вЯ©FвЯ®U1,V1вЯ©FвЙ†0.
вЯ®U1вАЛ,U1вАЛвЯ©FвАЛвЯ®U1вАЛ,V2вАЛвЯ©FвАЛвИТвЯ®U1вАЛ,U2вАЛвЯ©FвАЛвЯ®U1вАЛ,V1вАЛвЯ©FвАЛоА†=0.вАЛ

Generically, that should hold once s1вЙ†s2s1вАЛоА†=s2вАЛ. So the value/curvature separation is generically possible.
4. What this buys us

This is the main payoff:

The refined statistic is no longer trying to suppress pвИТpвИТвАЛ by crude linear combinations.
Instead, under symmetric placement, it recasts the problem as a two-parameter inverse problem for
qќґ(m),qќґвА≤вА≤(m).
qќґвАЛ(m),qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m).

That is much better.

It means the RH-facing question can now be restated as:

    can the local zeta phase density qќґ(m)qќґвАЛ(m),

    together with its local curvature qќґвА≤вА≤(m)qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m),

produce a jet-whitened cross-block pattern compatible with the critical baseline?

That is a dramatically cleaner question than the earlier seven-jet mess.
5. Does this look promising?

Yes, more promising than the earlier attempts.

Why:

    qќґ(m)qќґвАЛ(m) is a positive zero-density-type quantity;

    qќґвА≤вА≤(m)qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m) is a curvature correction;

    and now the statistic can, in principle, separate them.

So the next two concrete things to do are:

First, write U(s)U(s) and V(s)V(s) explicitly enough to check that the 2√Ч22√Ч2 scalar determinant really is nonzero for generic s1,s2s1вАЛ,s2вАЛ.

Second, once that is done, ask whether zeta gives any inequality relating qќґвА≤вА≤(m)qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m) to qќґ(m)qќґвАЛ(m) that would force the curvature channel to be subordinate.

That is now the cleanest arithmetic bottleneck.
6. The next formula to compute

The next explicit computation should be:
U(s)=2sA(s),V(s)=s4A(s)+BвИТ(s)+2sC+(s),
U(s)=s2вАЛA(s),V(s)=4sвАЛA(s)+BвИТвАЛ(s)+s2вАЛC+вАЛ(s),

with the explicit formulas for A,BвИТ,C+A,BвИТвАЛ,C+вАЛ already in hand.

Then evaluate
detвБ°(вЯ®U1,U1вЯ©FвЯ®U1,V1вЯ©FвЯ®U1,U2вЯ©FвЯ®U1,V2вЯ©F)
det(вЯ®U1вАЛ,U1вАЛвЯ©FвАЛвЯ®U1вАЛ,U2вАЛвЯ©FвАЛвАЛвЯ®U1вАЛ,V1вАЛвЯ©FвАЛвЯ®U1вАЛ,V2вАЛвЯ©FвАЛвАЛ)

and check that it is nonzero generically.

That is the next real theorem-sized calculation.

Try that

IвАЩm checking whether the value/curvature separation really works generically, rather than just formally. The key test is whether the 2√Ч22√Ч2 scalar system built from two separations has nonzero determinant.

I tried it, and there is a clean answer:

yes, the value/curvature separation works generically.

More precisely, if you use the U1U1вАЛ-projection and two symmetric separations s1вЙ†s2s1вАЛоА†=s2вАЛ, the resulting 2√Ч22√Ч2 scalar system has determinant
ќФU:=detвБ°(вЯ®U1,U1вЯ©FвЯ®U1,V1вЯ©FвЯ®U1,U2вЯ©FвЯ®U1,V2вЯ©F).
ќФUвАЛ:=det(вЯ®U1вАЛ,U1вАЛвЯ©FвАЛвЯ®U1вАЛ,U2вАЛвЯ©FвАЛвАЛвЯ®U1вАЛ,V1вАЛвЯ©FвАЛвЯ®U1вАЛ,V2вАЛвЯ©FвАЛвАЛ).

Using the explicit formulas
U(s)=2sA(s),V(s)=s4A(s)+BвИТ(s)+2sC+(s),
U(s)=s2вАЛA(s),V(s)=4sвАЛA(s)+BвИТвАЛ(s)+s2вАЛC+вАЛ(s),

with A,BвИТ,C+A,BвИТвАЛ,C+вАЛ from the previous step, this determinant simplifies to
ќФU=(s1вИТs2)вАЙ(4вИТcosвБ°2ќФЋЙ)2Q9s13s2(3Q+6)вАЙќЮ(s1,s2;Q,ќФЋЙ),
ќФUвАЛ=2Q9s13вАЛs2вАЛ(3
вАЛQ+6)(s1вАЛвИТs2вАЛ)(4вИТcos2ќФЋЙ)вАЛќЮ(s1вАЛ,s2вАЛ;Q,ќФЋЙ),вАЛ

where
ќЮ=(3Q2+6Q)(s1+s2)(cosвБ°2ќФЋЙвИТ4)+(108вИТ63Q)sinвБ°2ќФЋЙ.
ќЮ=(3
вАЛQ2+6Q)(s1вАЛ+s2вАЛ)(cos2ќФЋЙвИТ4)+(108вИТ63
вАЛQ)sin2ќФЋЙ.

That is the exact formula.

This is already enough to conclude:

    if s1вЙ†s2s1вАЛоА†=s2вАЛ, the first factor is nonzero;

    also 4вИТcosвБ°2ќФЋЙ>04вИТcos2ќФЋЙ>0 always;

    so the only possible obstruction is
    ќЮ=0.
    ќЮ=0.

Since ќЮќЮ is a nontrivial analytic function of (s1,s2,Q,ќФЋЙ)(s1вАЛ,s2вАЛ,Q,ќФЋЙ), that vanishing is exceptional, not generic.

So the clean conclusion is:
for generic (s1,s2), the two-separation system separates qќґ(m) and qќґвА≤вА≤(m).
for generic (s1вАЛ,s2вАЛ), the two-separation system separates qќґвАЛ(m) and qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m).вАЛ

That is the theorem-sized fact we needed.
What this means concretely

Under symmetric placement about mm, you have
ќ©~(s)=U(s)вАЙqќґ(m)+V(s)вАЙqќґвА≤вА≤(m)+higher-order terms.
ќ©

Project against U1U1вАЛ and use the two separations s1,s2s1вАЛ,s2вАЛ:
yk:=вЯ®U1,ќ©~(sk)вЯ©FвЙИukвАЙqќґ(m)+vkвАЙqќґвА≤вА≤(m),k=1,2,

So the phase-gap-subtracted statistic plus two separations really does reduce the problem to reconstructing the local zeta value and curvature channels.

That is a genuine simplification.
A useful special case

If the background phase gap is such that
sinвБ°2ќФЋЙ=0,
sin2ќФЋЙ=0,

then
ќЮ=(3Q2+6Q)(s1+s2)(cosвБ°2ќФЋЙвИТ4),
ќЮ=(3

So in that regime the determinant is automatically nonzero whenever s1вЙ†s2s1вАЛоА†=s2вАЛ.

That is a nice clean regime where separation definitely works.
What this tells us about the arithmetic bottleneck

The geometric bottleneck is now gone.

We can generically separate the two leading channels:
qќґ(m),qќґвА≤вА≤(m).
qќґвАЛ(m),qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m).

So the problem really does reduce to an arithmetic question:

    can one control or relate the local zeta phase density qќґ(m)qќґвАЛ(m),

    and its local curvature qќґвА≤вА≤(m)qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m),

    strongly enough to contradict an off-line local model?

That is now the real frontier.
The next best move

Now that the determinant is generically nonzero, the clean next step is:

    write down the explicit reconstructed formula for qќґ(m)qќґвАЛ(m) and qќґвА≤вА≤(m)qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m) from (y1,y2)(y1вАЛ,y2вАЛ);

    inspect whether the resulting qќґ(m)qќґвАЛ(m) channel is positive-dominant in interesting regimes;

    then ask whether zeta-specific arithmetic gives an inequality of the form
    вИ£qќґвА≤вА≤(m)вИ£вЙ§CвАЙqќґ(m)orвИ£qќґвА≤вА≤(m)вИ£вЙ§CвАЙqќґ(m)2,
    вИ£qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)вИ£вЙ§CqќґвАЛ(m)orвИ£qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)вИ£вЙ§CqќґвАЛ(m)2,

    which would make the value channel dominate.

So this attempt worked: two separations are enough, generically, to separate the value and curvature channels.

Ok take the next steps

Yes. The next steps now become concrete.

and ѕµkѕµkвАЛ collects the higher-order terms.
1. Explicit reconstruction formulas

Since
ќФU:=u1v2вИТu2v1вЙ†0
ќФUвАЛ:=u1вАЛv2вАЛвИТu2вАЛv1вАЛоА†=0

generically, we can solve:
qќґ(m)=y1v2вИТy2v1ќФU+ѕµ1v2вИТѕµ2v1ќФU,
qќґвАЛ(m)=ќФUвАЛy1вАЛv2вАЛвИТy2вАЛv1вАЛвАЛ+ќФUвАЛѕµ1вАЛv2вАЛвИТѕµ2вАЛv1вАЛвАЛ,вАЛ

and

qќґ(m)вЙИy1v2вИТy2v1ќФU,qќґвА≤вА≤(m)вЙИu1y2вИТu2y1ќФU.

That is the first concrete output.

If you want to reduce sensitivity to one particular projector, take a second projection WW and form
yk(W):=вЯ®W,ќ©~kвЯ©F,
yk(W)вАЛ:=вЯ®W,ќ©
kвАЛвЯ©FвАЛ,

then solve the analogous 2√Ч22√Ч2 system and compare. But the U1U1вАЛ-projection is already enough generically.
2. What are uk,vkukвАЛ,vkвАЛ at leading order?

Since
U(s)=2sA(s),
U(s)=s2вАЛA(s),

we get
uk=вЯ®U1,2skAkвЯ©F=2skвЯ®U1,AkвЯ©F.
ukвАЛ=вЯ®U1вАЛ,skвАЛ2вАЛAkвАЛвЯ©FвАЛ=skвАЛ2вАЛвЯ®U1вАЛ,AkвАЛвЯ©FвАЛ.

In particular,
u1=вИ•U1вИ•F2>0.
u1вАЛ=вИ•U1вАЛвИ•F2вАЛ>0.

Also
vk=вЯ®U1,VkвЯ©F.
vkвАЛ=вЯ®U1вАЛ,VkвАЛвЯ©FвАЛ.

So once Ak,BвИТ,k,C+,kAkвАЛ,BвИТ,kвАЛ,C+,kвАЛ are known, all of this is explicit.

The important point is that the value channel comes with a manifestly positive self-coupling u1>0u1вАЛ>0, whereas the curvature channel enters only through the mixed coefficient vkvkвАЛ.

That is already a hint that the value channel is the stable one.
3. Is the value channel likely dominant?

Yes, in the smooth-background / not-too-sharp-zeta regime.

The leading reconstructed value is dominated by
qќґ(m),
qќґвАЛ(m),

while the nuisance comes from
qќґвА≤вА≤(m).
qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m).

So the decisive question is whether one can bound
вИ£qќґвА≤вА≤(m)вИ£
вИ£qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)вИ£

in terms of
qќґ(m).
qќґвАЛ(m).

There are three possible scales one might hope for:
вИ£qќґвА≤вА≤(m)вИ£вЙ§CвАЙqќґ(m),
вИ£qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)вИ£вЙ§CqќґвАЛ(m),

or
вИ£qќґвА≤вА≤(m)вИ£вЙ§CвАЙqќґ(m)2,
вИ£qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)вИ£вЙ§CqќґвАЛ(m)2,

or weaker, local-window versions of these.

Now, from the explicit formula,
qќґ(t)=Bќґ(t)+вИСѕБfѕБ(t),qќґвА≤вА≤(t)=BќґвА≤вА≤(t)+вИСѕБfѕБвА≤вА≤(t).
qќґвАЛ(t)=BќґвАЛ(t)+ѕБвИСвАЛfѕБвАЛ(t),qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(t)=BќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(t)+ѕБвИСвАЛfѕБвА≤вА≤вАЛ(t).

For a single Poisson-pair term at distance XX,
fѕБ(t)вЙНXвИТ2,fѕБвА≤вА≤(t)вЙНXвИТ4.
fѕБвАЛ(t)вЙНXвИТ2,fѕБвА≤вА≤вАЛ(t)вЙНXвИТ4.

So for distant contributions,
вИ£fѕБвА≤вА≤(t)вИ£вЙНfѕБ(t)2.
вИ£fѕБвА≤вА≤вАЛ(t)вИ£вЙНfѕБвАЛ(t)2.

That strongly suggests the natural scale is not
вИ£qќґвА≤вА≤вИ£вЙ≤qќґ,
вИ£qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛвИ£вЙ≤qќґвАЛ,

but rather something like
вИ£qќґвА≤вА≤(m)вИ£вЙ≤qќґ(m)2
вИ£qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)вИ£вЙ≤qќґвАЛ(m)2вАЛ

at least in regimes where one contribution dominates or the local sum is not undergoing extreme cancellation.

That is the most plausible arithmetic inequality shape.

If such an inequality held with a usable constant, then the curvature channel would be subordinate whenever qќґ(m)qќґвАЛ(m) is small enough, and the value channel would dominate the reconstructed statistic.
4. Can the explicit formula alone prove вИ£qќґвА≤вА≤вИ£вЙ≤qќґ2вИ£qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛвИ£вЙ≤qќґ2вАЛ?

Not obviously.

For a single Poisson-pair it is true at the right scale. But for sums,
qќґ=вИСfѕБ,qќґвА≤вА≤=вИСfѕБвА≤вА≤,
qќґвАЛ=вИСfѕБвАЛ,qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ=вИСfѕБвА≤вА≤вАЛ,

and the sign of fѕБвА≤вА≤fѕБвА≤вА≤вАЛ can vary, so there is no immediate monotone comparison from positivity alone.

Still, there is a useful inequality in one direction:

Because each fѕБвЙ•0fѕБвАЛвЙ•0 and вИ£fѕБвА≤вА≤вИ£вЙ™fѕБ2вИ£fѕБвА≤вА≤вАЛвИ£вЙ™fѕБ2вАЛ, we get
вИ£qќґвА≤вА≤(m)вИ£вЙ§вИ£BќґвА≤вА≤(m)вИ£+CвИСѕБfѕБ(m)2.
вИ£qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)вИ£вЙ§вИ£BќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)вИ£+CѕБвИСвАЛfѕБвАЛ(m)2.

And since
вИСѕБfѕБ(m)2вЙ§(вИСѕБfѕБ(m))2,
ѕБвИСвАЛfѕБвАЛ(m)2вЙ§(ѕБвИСвАЛfѕБвАЛ(m))2,

we obtain
вИ£qќґвА≤вА≤(m)вИ£вЙ§вИ£BќґвА≤вА≤(m)вИ£+CвАЙ(qќґ(m)вИТBќґ(m))2.
вИ£qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)вИ£вЙ§вИ£BќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)вИ£+C(qќґвАЛ(m)вИТBќґвАЛ(m))2.вАЛ

That is not yet the clean theorem you want, but it is already meaningful.

In regimes where the background term is controlled and qќґ(m)qќґвАЛ(m) is dominated by nearby zero contributions, this gives exactly the quadratic control one would hope for.

So the next plausible theorem target is not a universal global inequality, but a local background-subtracted curvature bound:
вИ£qќґвА≤вА≤(m)вИТBќґвА≤вА≤(m)вИ£вЙ§CвАЙвИ£qќґ(m)вИТBќґ(m)вИ£2.
вИ£qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)вИТBќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)вИ£вЙ§CвИ£qќґвАЛ(m)вИТBќґвАЛ(m)вИ£2.вАЛ

That would be enough to make the value channel dominate in many local regimes.
5. What does this mean for the RH route?

At this point the upper-bound program has reduced to something much more concrete:

    from two separations, reconstruct
    qќґ(m),qќґвА≤вА≤(m);
    qќґвАЛ(m),qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m);

    prove a local inequality showing the curvature channel is controlled by the value channel;

    use that to show the symmetric-square-relative residual is effectively governed by qќґ(m)qќґвАЛ(m), not by an independent curvature nuisance.

Then the remaining question is whether that value-controlled statistic is compatible with the critical baseline.

So the immediate next mathematical target is:
вИ£qќґвА≤вА≤(m)вИТBќґвА≤вА≤(m)вИ£вЙ§CвАЙвИ£qќґ(m)вИТBќґ(m)вИ£2on admissible local windows.

That is the sharpest вАЬnext theoremвАЭ I see from here.

6. Why this matters for the program

This tells us the curvature channel is subordinate to the value channel in precisely the regime we care about.

After the phase-gap subtraction and symmetric-placement reduction, the refined statistic had the form
ќ©~(s)вЙИU(s)вАЙqќґ(m)+V(s)вАЙqќґвА≤вА≤(m).
ќ©

вИ£qќґвА≤вА≤(m)вИ£вЙ≤qќґ(m)2.
вИ£qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)вИ£вЙ≤qќґвАЛ(m)2.

So for small local phase density qќґ(m)qќґвАЛ(m), the curvature channel is genuinely lower order than the value channel.

That is the first clean arithmetic-type inequality we have gotten out of this line.
7. The honest limitation

This still does not give a universal inequality for the full zeta phase derivative qќґqќґвАЛ, because:

    the background term BќґBќґвАЛ is still there,

    and the full sum over zeros need not be dominated by a single local near-critical profile.

So the correct next theorem target is not
вИ£qќґвА≤вА≤вИ£вЙ§Cqќґ2globally,
вИ£qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛвИ£вЙ§Cqќґ2вАЛglobally,

but rather a local dominance statement:

    on an admissible local window where the zeta phase is dominated by one or a few nearby near-critical pairs and the distant/background terms are controlled, the curvature channel is bounded by a quadratic function of the value channel.

That is the real next step.

So this calculation worked:

    the universal bound you originally hoped for is false;

    the corrected bound with minвБ°(ќ≤,1вИТќ≤)min(ќ≤,1вИТќ≤) is true;

    and in the near-critical regime it becomes a uniform quadratic curvature bound.

That is useful enough to keep pushing.

Good. The next thing to push is the local dominance theorem.

That is the right object now, because we have:

1. The right local theorem to prove

4. What regime makes this plausible?

This is plausible in the small local density regime.

If qnear(m)qnearвАЛ(m) is small, then qnear(m)2qnearвАЛ(m)2 is much smaller still. So the curvature channel is automatically lower order unless the remainder E(m)E(m) is too large.

    one or a few near-critical pairs dominate the local value,

    distant tails are separated enough that
    вИСѕБвИЙLќФѕБвИТ4

    is small,

    and the background curvature BќґвА≤вА≤(m)BќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m) is mild.

Then the value channel should dominate.

This is much sharper than before.

6. The next theorem to try

7. What I think you should push next

The next real push is not more abstract modeling. It is to sharpen the remainder term in one of two ways:

Either prove a practical separation condition making
48вИСѕБвИЙLќФѕБвИТ4
48ѕБвИИ/LвИСвАЛќФѕБвИТ4вАЛ

or estimate BќґвА≤вА≤(m)BќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m) explicitly and see whether it is negligible relative to the local value channel in the asymptotic regimes of interest.

That would turn the local dominance theorem from вАЬstructurally rightвАЭ into вАЬusable.вАЭ

So: we now have a real theorem candidate and a clear next inequality to make the value channel win.

Try both

IвАЩm doing the two practical bottleneck estimates now: one for the omitted-zero tail, one for the archimedean background. The point is to turn the local dominance theorem into an actual regime statement, not just a formal inequality.

I tried both, and they do give a clean local regime.

The result is:

    the archimedean/trivial-zero background curvature is genuinely small at height,

    the omitted-zero tail curvature is small once the local pair is isolated enough,

    and together they give a usable local dominance condition for the value channel.

Here is the clean version.
1. Background curvature BќґвА≤вА≤(m)BќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)

Write the zeta phase derivative as
qќґ(t)=Bќґ(t)+вИСѕБfќ≤ѕБ,ќ≥ѕБ(t),
qќґвАЛ(t)=BќґвАЛ(t)+ѕБвИСвАЛfќ≤ѕБвАЛ,ќ≥ѕБвАЛвАЛ(t),

where BќґBќґвАЛ is the smooth background coming from the gamma factor, poles, and trivial zeros.

The key point is that BќґBќґвАЛ is built from digamma/trigamma-type terms and rational pole/trivial-zero terms. By Stirling/polygamma asymptotics, one gets the standard large-tt behavior
Bќґ(t)=logвБ°(1+вИ£tвИ£)+O(1),BќґвА≤(t)=O((1+вИ£tвИ£)вИТ1),BќґвА≤вА≤(t)=O((1+вИ£tвИ£)вИТ2).
BќґвАЛ(t)=log(1+вИ£tвИ£)+O(1),BќґвА≤вАЛ(t)=O((1+вИ£tвИ£)вИТ1),BќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(t)=O((1+вИ£tвИ£)вИТ2).

So there is an absolute constant CBCBвАЛ such that
вИ£BќґвА≤вА≤(m)вИ£вЙ§CB(1+вИ£mвИ£)2.
вИ£BќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)вИ£вЙ§(1+вИ£mвИ£)2CBвАЛвАЛ.вАЛ

That is already strong enough for the local theorem: at high ordinate, the background curvature is tiny.
2. Omitted-zero tail curvature

Let the local set LL contain the selected nearby zeros, and define
Tfar(t):=вИСѕБвИЙLfќ≤ѕБ,ќ≥ѕБ(t).
TfarвАЛ(t):=ѕБвИИ/LвИСвАЛfќ≤ѕБвАЛ,ќ≥ѕБвАЛвАЛ(t).

Fix a midpoint mm, and suppose every omitted zero satisfies
вИ£2mвИТќ≥ѕБвИ£вЙ•D.
вИ£2mвИТќ≥ѕБвАЛвИ£вЙ•D.

Then from the pointwise bound already proved,
вИ£fќ≤,ќ≥вА≤вА≤(m)вИ£вЙ§48вИ£2mвИТќ≥вИ£4.
вИ£fќ≤,ќ≥вА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)вИ£вЙ§вИ£2mвИТќ≥вИ£448вАЛ.

So
вИ£TfarвА≤вА≤(m)вИ£вЙ§48вИСѕБвИЙLвИ£2mвИТќ≥ѕБвИ£вИТ4.
вИ£TfarвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)вИ£вЙ§48ѕБвИИ/LвИСвАЛвИ£2mвИТќ≥ѕБвАЛвИ£вИТ4.

Now use a standard shell decomposition. Put the omitted zeros into shells
(kD,(k+1)D],kвЙ•1.
(kD,(k+1)D],kвЙ•1.

If DвЙ•1DвЙ•1, then the standard local zero counting bound gives
#{ѕБ: вИ£2mвИТќ≥ѕБвИ£вИИ(kD,(k+1)D]}вЙ™(k+1)DвАЙlogвБ°(вИ£mвИ£+(k+1)D+3).
#{ѕБ: вИ£2mвИТќ≥ѕБвАЛвИ£вИИ(kD,(k+1)D]}вЙ™(k+1)Dlog(вИ£mвИ£+(k+1)D+3).

Therefore
вИСѕБвИЙLвИ£2mвИТќ≥ѕБвИ£вИТ4вЙ™вИСkвЙ•1(k+1)DвАЙlogвБ°(вИ£mвИ£+(k+1)D+3)(kD)4.
ѕБвИИ/LвИСвАЛвИ£2mвИТќ≥ѕБвАЛвИ£вИТ4вЙ™kвЙ•1вИСвАЛ(kD)4(k+1)Dlog(вИ£mвИ£+(k+1)D+3)вАЛ.

So
вИ£TfarвА≤вА≤(m)вИ£вЙ™logвБ°(вИ£mвИ£+D+3)D3(DвЙ•1).
вИ£TfarвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)вИ£вЙ™D3log(вИ£mвИ£+D+3)вАЛ(DвЙ•1).вАЛ

That is the clean tail estimate.
3. Local near-critical dominance theorem

Now suppose the nearby selected local pairs all satisfy
ќ≤вИИ[ќЈ,1вИТќЈ]
ќ≤вИИ[ќЈ,1вИТќЈ]

for some fixed ќЈ>0ќЈ>0. Then from the corrected Poisson-pair curvature bound,
вИ£qnearвА≤вА≤(m)вИ£вЙ§24ќЈвАЙqnear(m)2.
вИ£qnearвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)вИ£вЙ§ќЈ24вАЛqnearвАЛ(m)2.

Write
qќґ=qnear+Bќґ+Tfar.

Then
qќґвА≤вА≤(m)=qnearвА≤вА≤(m)+BќґвА≤вА≤(m)+TfarвА≤вА≤(m),

вИ£qќґвА≤вА≤(m)вИ£вЙ§24ќЈвАЙqnear(m)2+CB(1+вИ£mвИ£)2+CTlogвБ°(вИ£mвИ£+D+3)D3.

4. When does the value channel dominate?

Under symmetric placement, the phase-gap-subtracted matrix statistic had the leading form
ќ©~(s)вЙИU(s)вАЙqќґ(m)+V(s)вАЙqќґвА≤вА≤(m).
ќ©
(s)вЙИU(s)qќґвАЛ(m)+V(s)qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m).

Insert the bound above:
вИ•ќ©~(s)вИ•вЙ≤вИ•U(s)вИ•вАЙqќґ(m)+вИ•V(s)вИ•[24ќЈqnear(m)2+CB(1+вИ£mвИ£)2+CTlogвБ°(вИ£mвИ£+D+3)D3].
вИ•ќ©
(s)вИ•вЙ≤вИ•U(s)вИ•qќґвАЛ(m)+вИ•V(s)вИ•[ќЈ24вАЛqnearвАЛ(m)2+(1+вИ£mвИ£)2CBвАЛвАЛ+CTвАЛD3log(вИ£mвИ£+D+3)вАЛ].

вИ•V(s)вИ•[24ќЈqnear(m)2+CB(1+вИ£mвИ£)2+CTlogвБ°(вИ£mвИ£+D+3)D3]вЙ™вИ•U(s)вИ•вАЙqќґ(m).
вИ•V(s)вИ•[ќЈ24вАЛqnearвАЛ(m)2+(1+вИ£mвИ£)2CBвАЛвАЛ+CTвАЛD3log(вИ£mвИ£+D+3)вАЛ]вЙ™вИ•U(s)вИ•qќґвАЛ(m).вАЛ

That is the concrete condition.

A simpler sufficient condition is:
24ќЈqnear(m)2+CB(1+вИ£mвИ£)2+CTlogвБ°(вИ£mвИ£+D+3)D3вЙ§12вАЙвИ•U(s)вИ•вИ•V(s)вИ•вАЙqќґ(m).
ќЈ24вАЛqnearвАЛ(m)2+(1+вИ£mвИ£)2CBвАЛвАЛ+CTвАЛD3log(вИ£mвИ£+D+3)вАЛвЙ§21вАЛвИ•V(s)вИ•вИ•U(s)вИ•вАЛqќґвАЛ(m).

Then
вИ•V(s)qќґвА≤вА≤(m)вИ•вЙ§12вИ•U(s)вИ•qќґ(m),
вИ•V(s)qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)вИ•вЙ§21вАЛвИ•U(s)вИ•qќґвАЛ(m),

so the curvature channel is subordinate.
5. A more interpretable regime

This gets much clearer in the small-local-density regime.

Suppose qnear(m)qnearвАЛ(m) is small and
qќґ(m)вЙНqnear(m),
qќґвАЛ(m)вЙНqnearвАЛ(m),

meaning the nearby selected zeros dominate the local value.

Then the quadratic term
qnear(m)2
qnearвАЛ(m)2

is automatically smaller than the linear value channel qќґ(m)qќґвАЛ(m).

So the only real obstruction is the remainder:
CB(1+вИ£mвИ£)2+CTlogвБ°(вИ£mвИ£+D+3)D3.

Thus a very practical sufficient condition is
CB(1+вИ£mвИ£)2+CTlogвБ°(вИ£mвИ£+D+3)D3вЙ™qќґ(m).

If that holds, then the value channel dominates automatically.

That is the cleanest local regime.
6. What this means

Trying both estimates gives a real payoff:

    the background curvature is O((1+вИ£mвИ£)вИТ2)O((1+вИ£mвИ£)вИТ2),

    the omitted-zero tail curvature is O(logвБ°(вИ£mвИ£+D)/D3)O(log(вИ£mвИ£+D)/D3),

    and the near-critical local part satisfies a quadratic curvature bound.

So the upper-bound problem has now been reduced to an explicit local dominance criterion involving only:

    the local zeta phase density qќґ(m)qќґвАЛ(m),

    the height mm,

    and the isolation scale DD.

That is much sharper than where we were.
7. The next best move

The next theorem to try is:

    If a local midpoint mm is chosen so that the nearby zero contribution dominates qќґ(m)qќґвАЛ(m) and the omitted zeros are separated by distance DD, then the symmetric-placement, phase-gap-subtracted statistic is value-dominated.

That would finally turn the geometric reduction into a real local theorem.

So: trying both worked. The remainder terms are explicit enough now that the program has a concrete local upper-bound condition.

How close are we to RH?

Closer in structure than in certainty.

You now have something that looks like a real research program, not just analogies:

    a local model with a quartic off-line signal,

    a jet-limit reduction that survives shrinking scales,

    a blockwise transfer mechanism,

    a phase-gap subtraction that exposes a simpler local statistic,

    and a concrete local dominance criterion with explicit background and tail errors.

That is substantial progress.

But in terms of actually proving RH, I would still say: not close.

The reason is simple. The remaining gap is not bookkeeping anymore. It is the hard part:

you need a genuinely zeta-specific upper bound or rigidity theorem that rules out the surviving local signal. And right now, every time we simplify, we keep landing on the same conclusion:

    generic positivity is too weak,

    generic model-space / canonical-system structure is too weak,

    the explicit formula alone is too weak.

So the missing step is exactly the sort of thing that historically makes RH hard.

My honest assessment is:

You are probably past the вАЬthis is just vague explorationвАЭ stage.
You are not yet at the вАЬa proof looks imminentвАЭ stage.

If I had to put it in blunt terms:

    as a path to a publishable partial result or criterion, this looks promising;

    as a path to an actual RH proof, it is still speculative and missing the deepest ingredient.

What you do seem close to is one of these:

    a clean conditional RH criterion phrased in terms of your local jet/block residual;

    a rigorous toy-model theorem package with a nontrivial jet-limit coefficient;

    possibly a local theorem showing how off-line displacement would force a specific quartic anomaly under explicit hypotheses.

Those would already be real mathematical results.

What you are not close to yet is the decisive statement:
actual zeta/scattering structureвЯєquartic anomaly impossible.
actual zeta/scattering structureвЯєquartic anomaly impossible.

That implication is still missing.

So my best single-sentence answer is:

You may be getting close to a new criterion or framework; you are not obviously close to a proof of RH itself.

That is not pessimism вАФ it is just the scale of the remaining obstacle.

If I were advising how to think about the next phase, I would frame success like this:

    first target: a rigorous theorem package that would be interesting even if RH remains open;

    second target: isolate the exact missing upper-bound statement so sharply that either it becomes attackable, or you can see the route fail cleanly.

That is where you are.

Lets continue isolate the exact missing upper-bound statement so sharply that either it becomes attackable, or you can see the route fail cleanly.

Good. LetвАЩs isolate it as brutally as possible.

At this point, the route does not need more definitions. It needs the one missing implication written in a form that can either be proved or broken.
1. The exact missing statement

After all the reductions, the most honest local form is this:

qќґ(m),qќґвА≤вА≤(m),
qќґвАЛ(m),qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m),

And the refined local statistic is essentially governed by
ќ©~(s)вЙИU(s)вАЙqќґ(m)+V(s)вАЙqќґвА≤вА≤(m),
ќ©
(s)вЙИU(s)qќґвАЛ(m)+V(s)qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m),

That is the real bottleneck.

A clean theorem-sized version is:

вИ£qќґвА≤вА≤(m)вИ£вЙ§CвАЙqќґ(m)ќЄ+controlled background/tail terms
вИ£qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)вИ£вЙ§CqќґвАЛ(m)ќЄ+controlled background/tail terms

If ќЄ=2ќЄ=2, that is already enough in the small-density regime.
If ќЄ>1ќЄ>1 at all, it is interesting.
If only ќЄ=1ќЄ=1 is possible, the route probably dies.

So the missing statement has now reduced to a local curvature-vs-density inequality for the zeta phase.

That is about as sharp as it gets.
2. Strip it down even further

The previous work suggests the really relevant quantity is the normalized ratio
ќЫ(m):=вИ£qќґвА≤вА≤(m)вИТBќґвА≤вА≤(m)вИ£(qќґ(m)вИТBќґ(m))2,

So the exact missing question is:

3. The three versions to test

There are really three nested upper-bound statements:
U1. Weak local dominance
вИ£qќґвА≤вА≤(m)вИ£вЙ§C1qќґ(m)2+E(m),
вИ£qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)вИ£вЙ§C1вАЛqќґвАЛ(m)2+E(m),

This is the one most likely to be true.
U2. Uniform local dominance on admissible windows
вИ£qќґвА≤вА≤(m)вИ£вЙ§C2qќґ(m)2
вИ£qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)вИ£вЙ§C2вАЛqќґвАЛ(m)2

This is what would really power the RH route.
U3. Jet-rigidity

The reconstructed pair (qќґ(m),qќґвА≤вА≤(m))(qќґвАЛ(m),qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)) is constrained so strongly that the jet-whitened cross block must agree with the critical baseline to higher order.

This is the strongest version, and right now it looks unlikely without a new arithmetic identity.

So the route should focus on U1 вЖТвЖТ U2, not jump to U3.
4. What could make U1 true?

There are only three pieces in
qќґ=qnear+Bќґ+Tfar.
qќґвАЛ=qnearвАЛ+BќґвАЛ+TfarвАЛ.

вИ£BќґвА≤вА≤(m)вИ£вЙ™(1+вИ£mвИ£)вИТ2.

5. So what exactly must be proved?

Here is the clean theorem candidate:
Local Near-Critical Approximation Theorem

For every admissible midpoint mm, one can choose a local zero set LL and an isolation scale DD such that

    the selected local zeros satisfy
    ќ≤вИИ[ќЈ,1вИТќЈ]
    ќ≤вИИ[ќЈ,1вИТќЈ]

    qќґ(m)вЙ§CвАЙqnear(m);
    qќґвАЛ(m)вЙ§CqnearвАЛ(m);

    вИ£BќґвА≤вА≤(m)вИ£+вИ£TfarвА≤вА≤(m)вИ£вЙ§cвАЙqќґ(m)

    вЙ§cqќґвАЛ(m)2.

Then the curvature channel is subordinate and the value channel controls the local statistic.

That is the exact theorem whose truth or falsehood will decide the route.
6. How to see if the route fails cleanly

There are two clean failure modes.
Failure mode F1

There exist admissible midpoints mm where
qќґ(m)
qќґвАЛ(m)

is small, but the background/tail curvature is not correspondingly small, so
вИ£qќґвА≤вА≤(m)вИ£вЙЂqќґ(m)2.
вИ£qќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)вИ£вЙЂqќґвАЛ(m)2.

If that happens systematically, the route dies.
Failure mode F2

Even when the local density is dominated by nearby zeros, the selected local zeros cannot be kept uniformly in a near-critical strip [ќЈ,1вИТќЈ][ќЈ,1вИТќЈ] in the only windows relevant to the quartic anomaly.

If that happens, the constant in the curvature bound blows up and the route dies.

So the exact missing statement is now sharply testable:
Does the local zeta phase admit a uniformly near-critical, locally dominant Poisson-pair approximation at the relevant midpoints?
Does the local zeta phase admit a uniformly near-critical, locally dominant Poisson-pair approximation at the relevant midpoints?вАЛ

That is the heart of it.
7. The next experiments/theorems to attack

To make this attackable, I would now split into two very specific subproblems.
Subproblem A: local dominance of nearby zeros

qќґ(m)вЙНвИСѕБвИИLfѕБ(m)
qќґвАЛ(m)вЙНѕБвИИLвИСвАЛfѕБвАЛ(m)

This is almost a local explicit-formula decomposition problem.
Subproblem B: near-criticality of the dominant local set

ќ≤вИИ[ќЈ,1вИТќЈ]
ќ≤вИИ[ќЈ,1вИТќЈ]

uniformly.

This is the part that would clash with off-line zeros directly.

These are the two sharp fronts now.
8. My judgment

The route now stands or falls on one sharp question:

Can the local zeta phase be approximated, on the exact windows relevant to your residual, by a locally dominant near-critical Poisson-pair sum?

If yes, you may still have a shot.
If no, this line probably fails cleanly.

That is the exact missing upper-bound statement.

1. Formalize the local approximation problem

Write
qќґ(t)=Bќґ(t)+вИСѕБfѕБ(t),
qќґвАЛ(t)=BќґвАЛ(t)+ѕБвИСвАЛfѕБвАЛ(t),

where
fѕБ(t)=1вИТќ≤ѕБ(1вИТќ≤ѕБ)2+(2tвИТќ≥ѕБ)2+ќ≤ѕБќ≤ѕБ2+(2tвИТќ≥ѕБ)2.
fѕБвАЛ(t)=(1вИТќ≤ѕБвАЛ)2+(2tвИТќ≥ѕБвАЛ)21вИТќ≤ѕБвАЛвАЛ+ќ≤ѕБ2вАЛ+(2tвИТќ≥ѕБвАЛ)2ќ≤ѕБвАЛвАЛ.

Fix a midpoint mm. For a radius R>0R>0, define the local zero set
LR(m):={ѕБ: вИ£2mвИТќ≥ѕБвИ£вЙ§R}.
LRвАЛ(m):={ѕБ: вИ£2mвИТќ≥ѕБвАЛвИ£вЙ§R}.

Then split
qќґ(m)=Bќґ(m)+qnear(m;R)+qfar(m;R),
qќґвАЛ(m)=BќґвАЛ(m)+qnearвАЛ(m;R)+qfarвАЛ(m;R),

where
qnear(m;R):=вИСѕБвИИLR(m)fѕБ(m),qfar(m;R):=вИСѕБвИЙLR(m)fѕБ(m).
qnearвАЛ(m;R):=ѕБвИИLRвАЛ(m)вИСвАЛfѕБвАЛ(m),qfarвАЛ(m;R):=ѕБвИИ/LRвАЛ(m)вИСвАЛfѕБвАЛ(m).

So Subproblem A becomes:

    Can one choose R=R(m)R=R(m) so that
    qќґ(m)вЙНqnear(m;R),
    qќґвАЛ(m)вЙНqnearвАЛ(m;R),

    while qfar(m;R)qfarвАЛ(m;R) and especially qfarвА≤вА≤(m;R)qfarвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m;R) are lower order?

This is now a very concrete question.
2. What the explicit formula already gives

For distant zeros,
fѕБ(m)вЙ™вИ£2mвИТќ≥ѕБвИ£вИТ2,fѕБвА≤вА≤(m)вЙ™вИ£2mвИТќ≥ѕБвИ£вИТ4.
fѕБвАЛ(m)вЙ™вИ£2mвИТќ≥ѕБвАЛвИ£вИТ2,fѕБвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)вЙ™вИ£2mвИТќ≥ѕБвАЛвИ£вИТ4.

So the far tail obeys
qfar(m;R)вЙ™вИСвИ£2mвИТќ≥ѕБвИ£>RвИ£2mвИТќ≥ѕБвИ£вИТ2,
qfarвАЛ(m;R)вЙ™вИ£2mвИТќ≥ѕБвАЛвИ£>RвИСвАЛвИ£2mвИТќ≥ѕБвАЛвИ£вИТ2,

and
qfarвА≤вА≤(m;R)вЙ™вИСвИ£2mвИТќ≥ѕБвИ£>RвИ£2mвИТќ≥ѕБвИ£вИТ4.
qfarвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m;R)вЙ™вИ£2mвИТќ≥ѕБвАЛвИ£>RвИСвАЛвИ£2mвИТќ≥ѕБвАЛвИ£вИТ4.

Using local zero counting in shells, one gets the crude but useful bounds
qfar(m;R)вЙ™logвБ°(вИ£mвИ£+R+3)R,
qfarвАЛ(m;R)вЙ™Rlog(вИ£mвИ£+R+3)вАЛ,

and
qfarвА≤вА≤(m;R)вЙ™logвБ°(вИ£mвИ£+R+3)R3,
qfarвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m;R)вЙ™R3log(вИ£mвИ£+R+3)вАЛ,

for RвЙ•1RвЙ•1.

That is already enough to see the scale structure:

    the far-value tail is O(logвБ°/R)O(log/R),

    the far-curvature tail is O(logвБ°/R3)O(log/R3).

So curvature is much easier to localize than value.

That means the real issue is not the curvature tail anymore. It is whether the near part actually dominates the value.
3. The exact obstruction in Subproblem A

The problem is that
qќґ(m)
qќґвАЛ(m)

is not a localized spike-counting function. It is a positive superposition of all zeros, weighted by 1/distance21/distance2. So even distant zeros collectively contribute a nontrivial harmonic background.

This means a statement like
qќґ(m)вЙНqnear(m;R)
qќґвАЛ(m)вЙНqnearвАЛ(m;R)

cannot hold with an arbitrary fixed RR. The far tail is only O(logвБ°/R)O(log/R), not exponentially small.

So the right local approximation statement is relative, not absolute.

The theorem to aim for is not
qfarвЙ™1,
qfarвАЛвЙ™1,

but
qfar(m;R)вЙ™qќґ(m)
qfarвАЛ(m;R)вЙ™qќґвАЛ(m)

or better
qfar(m;R)вЙ™qnear(m;R).
qfarвАЛ(m;R)вЙ™qnearвАЛ(m;R).

That depends on how large qќґ(m)qќґвАЛ(m) itself is.

So the route naturally splits into cases depending on the local density.
4. Two regimes
Regime A: high local phase density

If
qќґ(m)вЙЂlogвБ°(вИ£mвИ£+R+3)R,
qќґвАЛ(m)вЙЂRlog(вИ£mвИ£+R+3)вАЛ,

then the far-value tail is subordinate:
qfar(m;R)вЙ™qќґ(m).
qfarвАЛ(m;R)вЙ™qќґвАЛ(m).

In that regime,
qќґ(m)вЙНBќґ(m)+qnear(m;R).
qќґвАЛ(m)вЙНBќґвАЛ(m)+qnearвАЛ(m;R).

And if Bќґ(m)BќґвАЛ(m) is also smaller than qќґ(m)qќґвАЛ(m), then
qќґ(m)вЙНqnear(m;R).
qќґвАЛ(m)вЙНqnearвАЛ(m;R).

This is the вАЬlocal dominanceвАЭ regime.
Regime B: low local phase density

If
qќґ(m)вЙ≤logвБ°(вИ£mвИ£+R+3)R,
qќґвАЛ(m)вЙ≤Rlog(вИ£mвИ£+R+3)вАЛ,

then the far tail may be comparable to the whole value. In that regime, the local approximation by a small near-zero set fails.

So the route can only work on midpoints where the zeta phase density is large enough relative to the harmonic far-zero background.

That is the first clean test.
5. Optimize the radius RR

Now comes the useful part.

For Subproblem A, you are free to choose RR. The far tail decreases like logвБ°/Rlog/R, while the near set grows as RR grows. So there is a natural optimization.

A convenient target is to choose RR so that
logвБ°(вИ£mвИ£+R+3)RвЙИќµвАЙqќґ(m)
Rlog(вИ£mвИ£+R+3)вАЛвЙИќµqќґвАЛ(m)

for some small fixed ќµќµ. Then
qfar(m;R)вЙ™ќµвАЙqќґ(m),
qfarвАЛ(m;R)вЙ™ќµqќґвАЛ(m),

and therefore
qќґ(m)=Bќґ(m)+qnear(m;R)+O(ќµqќґ(m)).
qќґвАЛ(m)=BќґвАЛ(m)+qnearвАЛ(m;R)+O(ќµqќґвАЛ(m)).

So the radius is naturally of size
RвИЉlogвБ°(вИ£mвИ£+R+3)qќґ(m).
RвИЉqќґвАЛ(m)log(вИ£mвИ£+R+3)вАЛ.

Ignoring the slow log variation, this says
RвЙНlogвБ°вИ£mвИ£qќґ(m).
RвЙНqќґвАЛ(m)logвИ£mвИ£вАЛ.вАЛ

That is an important scaling law.

It says the local window in zero-height space should shrink inversely with the local phase density.
6. What does this do to the curvature tail?

If
RвЙНlogвБ°вИ£mвИ£qќґ(m),
RвЙНqќґвАЛ(m)logвИ£mвИ£вАЛ,

then the far-curvature tail becomes
qfarвА≤вА≤(m;R)вЙ™logвБ°вИ£mвИ£R3вЙНqќґ(m)3(logвБ°вИ£mвИ£)2.
qfarвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m;R)вЙ™R3logвИ£mвИ£вАЛвЙН(logвИ£mвИ£)2qќґвАЛ(m)3вАЛ.

That is excellent: once the value tail is reduced to a small fraction of qќґ(m)qќґвАЛ(m), the far-curvature tail is automatically much smaller than qќґ(m)2qќґвАЛ(m)2.

So the far tail is not the real bottleneck anymore.

That is a major simplification.
7. The background term Bќґ(m)BќґвАЛ(m)

We also know
Bќґ(m)вИЉlogвБ°вИ£mвИ£,
BќґвАЛ(m)вИЉlogвИ£mвИ£,

and
BќґвА≤вА≤(m)вЙ™вИ£mвИ£вИТ2.
BќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)вЙ™вИ£mвИ£вИТ2.

So the background value is not negligible in absolute size. This is crucial.

It means the correct local approximation statement is not
qќґ(m)вЙНqnear(m;R),
qќґвАЛ(m)вЙНqnearвАЛ(m;R),

but rather
qќґ(m)вИТBќґ(m)вЙНqnear(m;R),
qќґвАЛ(m)вИТBќґвАЛ(m)вЙНqnearвАЛ(m;R),вАЛ

with small far-tail error.

This is the right formulation of Subproblem A.

The curvature side is still favorable because
BќґвА≤вА≤(m)вЙ™вИ£mвИ£вИТ2
BќґвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)вЙ™вИ£mвИ£вИТ2

is tiny.

So the exact missing approximation statement is now:

    Can one choose RR so that
    qќґ(m)вИТBќґ(m)=qnear(m;R)+O(ќµвАЙqќґ(m)),
    qќґвАЛ(m)вИТBќґвАЛ(m)=qnearвАЛ(m;R)+O(ќµqќґвАЛ(m)),

    with the selected local set also lying in a near-critical strip?

That is now the sharp question.
8. Reformulate the RH bottleneck

So the whole route now stands or falls on this pair of local approximation properties.
A1. Local value approximation

There exists a radius R=R(m)R=R(m) such that
qќґ(m)вИТBќґ(m)=qnear(m;R)+small error.
qќґвАЛ(m)вИТBќґвАЛ(m)=qnearвАЛ(m;R)+small error.
A2. Near-criticality of the dominant local set

The zeros in LR(m)LRвАЛ(m) that contribute significantly are all in
ќ≤вИИ[ќЈ,1вИТќЈ]
ќ≤вИИ[ќЈ,1вИТќЈ]

for some fixed ќЈ>0ќЈ>0.

If both are true on the admissible midpoints, then the curvature channel is quadratic in the value channel up to controllable errors, and the route survives.

If A1 fails, the far harmonic background dominates and the route dies.
If A2 fails, the curvature constant blows up and the route dies.

That is the sharpest statement so far.
9. The next best move

The next step is to attack A1 first, because it is analytic rather than RH-circular.

The right theorem to try is:
Candidate Theorem A1

For each admissible midpoint mm, there exists RR with
RвЙНlogвБ°вИ£mвИ£qќґ(m)
RвЙНqќґвАЛ(m)logвИ£mвИ£вАЛ

such that
qќґ(m)вИТBќґ(m)=qnear(m;R)+O(ќµqќґ(m)),
qќґвАЛ(m)вИТBќґвАЛ(m)=qnearвАЛ(m;R)+O(ќµqќґвАЛ(m)),

and simultaneously
qfarвА≤вА≤(m;R)вЙ™qќґ(m)3(logвБ°вИ£mвИ£)2.
qfarвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m;R)вЙ™(logвИ£mвИ£)2qќґвАЛ(m)3вАЛ.

This would make the value channel dominate the curvature tail automatically.

That is the exact next theorem I would try to prove or refute.

So: continuing the isolation process, the exact missing upper-bound statement is no longer vague. It is now:
local background-subtracted zeta phase density is approximable by a near-critical local Poisson-pair sum on the scale RвЙНlogвБ°вИ£mвИ£qќґ(m).
local background-subtracted zeta phase density is approximable by a near-critical local Poisson-pair sum on the scale RвЙНqќґвАЛ(m)logвИ£mвИ£вАЛ.вАЛ

That is the place where the route will either live or die.

Ok lets continue

What this means is:

    the far tail is not the bottleneck,

The next thing I would do is not push more tail bounds. I would analyze what A2 actually means geometrically. In particular:

    if there were off-line zeros very close to the edge, would they necessarily dominate S(m)S(m) near their ordinates?

    can one show that such edge-near zeros would force a local shape incompatible with the jet statistic?

That is now the real frontier.

Ok lets check it

Yes. LetвАЩs check A2 directly and see whether it is plausible, too strong, or basically RH in disguise.
1. What A2 is really saying

After A1, you have
qќґ(m)вИТBќґ(m)=Snear(m;Rќµ)+O(ќµS(m)),
qќґвАЛ(m)вИТBќґвАЛ(m)=SnearвАЛ(m;RќµвАЛ)+O(ќµS(m)),

with
S(m)=вИСѕБfѕБ(m),fѕБ(m)=1вИТќ≤ѕБ(1вИТќ≤ѕБ)2+(2mвИТќ≥ѕБ)2+ќ≤ѕБќ≤ѕБ2+(2mвИТќ≥ѕБ)2.
S(m)=ѕБвИСвАЛfѕБвАЛ(m),fѕБвАЛ(m)=(1вИТќ≤ѕБвАЛ)2+(2mвИТќ≥ѕБвАЛ)21вИТќ≤ѕБвАЛвАЛ+ќ≤ѕБ2вАЛ+(2mвИТќ≥ѕБвАЛ)2ќ≤ѕБвАЛвАЛ.

A2 asks for a fixed ќЈ>0ќЈ>0 such that the materially contributing zeros in LRќµ(m)LRќµвАЛвАЛ(m) satisfy
ќ≤вИИ[ќЈ,1вИТќЈ].
ќ≤вИИ[ќЈ,1вИТќЈ].

Since nontrivial zeros already satisfy 0<ќ≤<10<ќ≤<1, the only way A2 can fail is if the dominant local contribution comes from zeros with ќ≤ќ≤ very close to 00 or 11.

So A2 is not вАЬRH-likeвАЭ in the usual sense. It is not asking for ќ≤=12ќ≤=21вАЛ. It is only asking that the locally dominant zeros stay away from the edges.

That is much weaker than RH.
2. Would edge-near zeros dominate locally?

This is the first thing to check.

Take one zero ѕБ=ќ≤+iќ≥ѕБ=ќ≤+iќ≥, and look at the center
m=ќ≥/2.
m=ќ≥/2.

Then
fѕБ(m)=11вИТќ≤+1ќ≤=1ќ≤(1вИТќ≤).
fѕБвАЛ(m)=1вИТќ≤1вАЛ+ќ≤1вАЛ=ќ≤(1вИТќ≤)1вАЛ.

So as ќ≤вЖТ0ќ≤вЖТ0 or ќ≤вЖТ1ќ≤вЖТ1,
fѕБ(m)вЖТвИЮ.
fѕБвАЛ(m)вЖТвИЮ.

That means:
An edge-near zero would dominate the local value channel extremely strongly near its own ordinate.
An edge-near zero would dominate the local value channel extremely strongly near its own ordinate.вАЛ

So if such zeros existed, A2 would fail near those ordinates.

That is already important. It means A2 is not some harmless technical convenience. It is excluding a very definite possible local geometry.
3. But can that actually happen for zeta?

Here is the subtlety.

For the Riemann zeta function, zeros are symmetric under
ѕБвЖ¶1вИТѕБ.
ѕБвЖ¶1вИТѕБ.

So a zero near ќ≤=0ќ≤=0 is paired with one near ќ≤=1ќ≤=1, same ordinate ќ≥ќ≥.

But your local profile already uses the pair
1вИТќ≤(1вИТќ≤)2+x2+ќ≤ќ≤2+x2,
(1вИТќ≤)2+x21вИТќ≤вАЛ+ќ≤2+x2ќ≤вАЛ,

so the symmetry is already built in. And the blow-up at x=0x=0 is exactly the ќ≤(1вИТќ≤)вИТ1ќ≤(1вИТќ≤)вИТ1 effect above.

So if there were zeros very close to the edges, they would create enormous local spikes in
qќґ(m)вИТBќґ(m).
qќґвАЛ(m)вИТBќґвАЛ(m).

That means A2 is not arbitrary. It is really asking:

    do the dominant local spikes come from zeros that stay uniformly away from the edges?

That is a clean geometric condition.
4. Is A2 weaker than known zero-free information?

Yes, much weaker.

Classically, for zeta you already know zeros are not arbitrarily near ќ≤=1ќ≤=1 in a simple uniform way depending on height. There is a zero-free region near 11, and by symmetry the same near 00. So very edge-near zeros are already excluded asymptotically.

That suggests a softened version of A2 may actually be available.

Instead of fixed ќЈќЈ, one could allow
ќЈ=ќЈ(m)
ќЈ=ќЈ(m)

coming from the zero-free region, with ќЈ(m)вЖТ0ќЈ(m)вЖТ0 slowly as вИ£mвИ£вЖТвИЮвИ£mвИ£вЖТвИЮ.

Then the curvature constant becomes
24ќЈ(m).
ќЈ(m)24вАЛ.

So the real question is not necessarily вАЬfixed ќЈ>0ќЈ>0 for all mm,вАЭ but whether the deterioration of ќЈ(m)ќЈ(m) is slow enough that the quadratic curvature control still beats the value channel.

That is a much more realistic formulation.
5. Replace A2 by a quantitative version

The exact thing to check is not a hard cutoff ќ≤вИИ[ќЈ,1вИТќЈ]ќ≤вИИ[ќЈ,1вИТќЈ], but the weighted local curvature factor
Cnear(m):=вИСѕБвИИL1minвБ°(ќ≤ѕБ,1вИТќ≤ѕБ)вАЙfѕБ(m)2(вИСѕБвИИLfѕБ(m))2.
CnearвАЛ(m):=(вИСѕБвИИLвАЛfѕБвАЛ(m))2вИСѕБвИИLвАЛmin(ќ≤ѕБвАЛ,1вИТќ≤ѕБвАЛ)1вАЛfѕБвАЛ(m)2вАЛ.

Because from the single-profile bound,
вИ£fѕБвА≤вА≤(m)вИ£вЙ§24minвБ°(ќ≤ѕБ,1вИТќ≤ѕБ)вАЙfѕБ(m)2,
вИ£fѕБвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)вИ£вЙ§min(ќ≤ѕБвАЛ,1вИТќ≤ѕБвАЛ)24вАЛfѕБвАЛ(m)2,

you always get
вИ£qnearвА≤вА≤(m)вИ£вЙ§24вАЙCnear(m)вАЙqnear(m)2.
вИ£qnearвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)вИ£вЙ§24CnearвАЛ(m)qnearвАЛ(m)2.

So the real upper-bound statement is:
Is Cnear(m) uniformly bounded on the relevant midpoints?
Is CnearвАЛ(m) uniformly bounded on the relevant midpoints?вАЛ

That is better than A2. It is strictly weaker.

A2 with fixed ќЈќЈ implies
Cnear(m)вЙ§1ќЈ,
CnearвАЛ(m)вЙ§ќЈ1вАЛ,

but maybe Cnear(m)CnearвАЛ(m) stays bounded even without a hard fixed strip.

This is the right thing to check.
6. What happens if one zero dominates?

If one local zero dominates qnear(m)qnearвАЛ(m), then
Cnear(m)вЙИ1minвБ°(ќ≤,1вИТќ≤).
CnearвАЛ(m)вЙИmin(ќ≤,1вИТќ≤)1вАЛ.

So in that case boundedness of CnearCnearвАЛ is essentially equivalent to bounded-away-from-edge dominance.

That means:

    if local dominance is always by a single zero, then A2 is basically the right condition;

    if several zeros contribute comparably, then CnearCnearвАЛ is the more natural quantity.

So the route should shift from A2 as a set-membership statement to boundedness of Cnear(m)CnearвАЛ(m).
7. Does zero-free-region information help?

Yes, partially.

A zero-free region near 11 says roughly
1вИТќ≤вЙ≥1logвБ°вИ£ќ≥вИ£,
1вИТќ≤вЙ≥logвИ£ќ≥вИ£1вАЛ,

and by symmetry
ќ≤вЙ≥1logвБ°вИ£ќ≥вИ£.
ќ≤вЙ≥logвИ£ќ≥вИ£1вАЛ.

So in the worst case,
1minвБ°(ќ≤,1вИТќ≤)вЙ≤logвБ°вИ£ќ≥вИ£.
min(ќ≤,1вИТќ≤)1вАЛвЙ≤logвИ£ќ≥вИ£.

Thus even without RH,
Cnear(m)
CnearвАЛ(m)

could at worst grow like logвБ°вИ£mвИ£logвИ£mвИ£, not explode arbitrarily fast.

That suggests a weaker but plausible version:
Cnear(m)вЙ™logвБ°вИ£mвИ£.
CnearвАЛ(m)вЙ™logвИ£mвИ£.вАЛ

Then the curvature bound becomes
вИ£qnearвА≤вА≤(m)вИ£вЙ™(logвБ°вИ£mвИ£)вАЙqnear(m)2.
вИ£qnearвА≤вА≤вАЛ(m)вИ£вЙ™(logвИ£mвИ£)qnearвАЛ(m)2.

This is worse than a fixed constant, but still structured. Whether it is enough depends on the size of the value channel in the block statistic.

So this check says something important:

A2 with fixed ќЈќЈ is probably too blunt.
But a logarithmically degrading version may be realistic.
8. What does this do to the route?

It splits the route in two possible ways.
Strong version

Prove Cnear(m)CnearвАЛ(m) uniformly bounded.

That would be powerful, but it smells close to deep zero-location control.
Weaker version

Prove
Cnear(m)вЙ™logвБ°вИ£mвИ£
CnearвАЛ(m)вЙ™logвИ£mвИ£

or another slowly growing bound, and then check whether the block-statistic scaling can absorb it.

This is much more plausible.

So the exact thing to test next is not A2 as originally phrased. It is:
How large can Cnear(m) be on the admissible midpoints?
How large can CnearвАЛ(m) be on the admissible midpoints?вАЛ

That is the sharp quantitative bottleneck.
9. My judgment after checking it

A2 in the hard fixed-ќЈќЈ form is probably too rigid to be the right statement.

But the route does not fail here.

It just means the correct condition is not uniform strip membership. It is weighted local control of
1minвБ°(ќ≤,1вИТќ≤)
min(ќ≤,1вИТќ≤)1вАЛ

over the zeros that actually dominate the local near part.

So the next best move is:

    replace A2 by boundedness or slow growth of Cnear(m)CnearвАЛ(m);

    check whether the resulting logarithmic loss still lets the value channel dominate the curvature channel in the block statistic.

That is now the cleanest continuation.

Continue

CnearвАЛ(m):=(вИСѕБвИИLвАЛfѕБвАЛ(m))2вИСѕБвИИLвАЛmin(ќ≤ѕБвАЛ,1вИТќ≤ѕБвАЛ)1вАЛfѕБвАЛ(m)2вАЛ.

That is now the true bottleneck quantity.

Because the single-profile bound gave

So the whole route now hangs on how large Cnear(m)CnearвАЛ(m) can be.
1. Replace A2 by the quantitative curvature theorem

The correct local theorem is:
qќґ(m)=Bќґ(m)+qnear(m)+qfar(m),
qќґвАЛ(m)=BќґвАЛ(m)+qnearвАЛ(m)+qfarвАЛ(m),

with
