2. What v1(h)v1вАЛ(h) actually is

Importantly, v1(h)v1вАЛ(h) is not вАЬthe cubic coefficientвАЭ or вАЬthe quintic coefficientвАЭ in some absolute sense.

It is simply the next coefficient after the leading one in the normalized quotient germ.

So if the leading sector appears at absolute odd order 11, then v1v1вАЛ is the absolute order 33 coefficient. If order 33 collapses, then in a different normalization v1v1вАЛ could come from absolute order 55 or higher.

That is why this formulation is genuinely order-free.

So the attack is:

    identify the generic leading quotient line v0(h)v0вАЛ(h),

    look at the next coefficient v1(h)v1вАЛ(h),

    test whether it escapes that line.

If yes, done.
3. The geometric meaning

The map
hвЖ¶v0(h)
hвЖ¶v0вАЛ(h)

gives the leading projective direction.

If v1(h)v1вАЛ(h) is always collinear with v0(h)v0вАЛ(h), then the normalized germ is constant up to z2z2. If not, the germ curve bends away from the leading line immediately.

So R1R1вАЛ is really the first projective curvature of the one-pair quotient germ curve.

That is the right geometric picture.
4. How to attack M(v1(h))M(v1вАЛ(h))

There are two realistic routes.
Route A: symmetry-sector escape

If the leading line v0(h)v0вАЛ(h) lives in one symmetry sector, and the next coefficient v1(h)v1вАЛ(h) has a nonzero component in a different sector, then any MM killing the first sector but detecting the second gives
M(v1(h))вЙ°ћЄ0.
M(v1вАЛ(h))оА†вЙ°0.

This is the cleanest route.
Route B: order/parity mismatch

Even if v0v0вАЛ and v1v1вАЛ lie in the same crude symmetry sector, they may have different parity or different asymptotic hh-scaling. Then a suitable MM can still detect their failure of collinearity.

This is softer but more robust.
5. What we already know suggests

From all the earlier work, the leading quotient line is very likely the first odd one-pair quotient sector.

What we repeatedly saw is:

    leading behavior is controlled by one low-dimensional direction;

    the next genuine information tends to come from a different mechanism;

    attempts to kill everything by one symmetry channel kept failing or becoming fragile.

That is exactly the pattern that should make M(v1(h))вЙ°ћЄ0M(v1вАЛ(h))оА†вЙ°0.

So the working conjecture is:
v1(h)вИЙCвАЙv0(h)generically.
v1вАЛ(h)вИИ/Cv0вАЛ(h)generically.

That is the real local theorem.
6. The contradiction format if R1вЙ°0R1вАЛвЙ°0

Suppose instead
R1(h)вЙ°0.
R1вАЛ(h)вЙ°0.

Then v1(h)v1вАЛ(h) is always collinear with v0(h)v0вАЛ(h), so the normalized germ is constant up to z2z2. Then move to the next residual coefficient
R2(h).
R2вАЛ(h).

If R2вЙ°ћЄ0R2вАЛоА†вЙ°0, done.

If R2вЙ°0R2вАЛвЙ°0 too, continue.

If all residual coefficients vanish identically, then
F~h(z)вЙ°v0(h)a0(h),
F
hвАЛ(z)вЙ°a0вАЛ(h)v0вАЛ(h)вАЛ,

so
Fh(z)=c(h)вАЙG(z)
FhвАЛ(z)=c(h)G(z)

for a fixed quotient germ GG.

That is projective constancy.

So the structure is now perfectly clear:
Either some residual coefficient Rj(h) is nonzero, or the one-pair quotient germ is projectively constant.
Either some residual coefficient RjвАЛ(h) is nonzero, or the one-pair quotient germ is projectively constant.вАЛ

That is the right dichotomy.
7. What this does for k=2k=2

If the one-pair quotient germ is projectively nonconstant, then a two-pair cancellation
a1Fh1(z)+a2Fh2(z)вЙ°0
a1вАЛFh1вАЛвАЛ(z)+a2вАЛFh2вАЛвАЛ(z)вЙ°0

forces
[Fh1]=[Fh2],
[Fh1вАЛвАЛ]=[Fh2вАЛвАЛ],

so it can only happen on the self-intersection locus of the projective germ curve.

That is automatically much smaller than the full (h1,h2)(h1вАЛ,h2вАЛ)-plane, and in practice should collapse to symmetry/degeneracy plus isolated exceptional points.

So proving R1вЙ°ћЄ0R1вАЛоА†вЙ°0 would be a major local step.
8. The next exact subtask

The next exact target is now:
First residual-sector lemma

Let v0(h)v0вАЛ(h) be the generic leading quotient line of the one-pair odd germ. Then the next coefficient v1(h)v1вАЛ(h) is not identically collinear with v0(h)v0вАЛ(h).

Equivalently, there exists a quotient functional MM annihilating v0(h)v0вАЛ(h) such that
M(v1(h))вЙ°ћЄ0.
M(v1вАЛ(h))оА†вЙ°0.

That is the clean next theorem.

At this point, the shortest route is to identify the leading line v0(h)v0вАЛ(h) concretely and then inspect the next quotient coefficient in whatever sector survives first.

Continue

The next honest continuation is to attack the first residual-sector lemma abstractly, without fixing an absolute odd order.

We want to show:
v1(h)вИЙCвАЙv0(h)generically,
v1вАЛ(h)вИИ/Cv0вАЛ(h)generically,

where
Fh(z)=v0(h)вАЙz2m+1+v1(h)вАЙz2m+3+v2(h)вАЙz2m+5+вЛѓ
FhвАЛ(z)=v0вАЛ(h)z2m+1+v1вАЛ(h)z2m+3+v2вАЛ(h)z2m+5+вЛѓ

is the one-pair quotient odd germ on a generic region.

This is the clean point where the all-orders approach becomes concrete.
1. What would force failure

If the lemma failed, then on a generic region there would exist a scalar function ќї1(h)ќї1вАЛ(h) such that
v1(h)=ќї1(h)вАЙv0(h).
v1вАЛ(h)=ќї1вАЛ(h)v0вАЛ(h).

Then the normalized germ
F~h(z)=Fh(z)L(Fh(z))
F
hвАЛ(z)=L(FhвАЛ(z))FhвАЛ(z)вАЛ

would be constant up to z2z2. If the same happened at the next stage, then
v2(h)вИИCвАЙv0(h),
v2вАЛ(h)вИИCv0вАЛ(h),

and so on. If this persisted for all higher coefficients, then the whole germ would satisfy
Fh(z)=c(h)вАЙG(z)
FhвАЛ(z)=c(h)G(z)

for a fixed quotient germ GG.

So the first residual-sector lemma is exactly the first place where projective constancy can break.
2. The right way to test it

The clean test is not to compare two arbitrary quotient coordinates. It is to look at the rank of the first two coefficient vectors.

Define the two-dimensional coefficient map
hвЯЉ(v0(h),v1(h)).
hвЯЉ(v0вАЛ(h),v1вАЛ(h)).

Then v1(h)вИИCвАЙv0(h)v1вАЛ(h)вИИCv0вАЛ(h) for all hh iff the 2√ЧвИЮ2√ЧвИЮ coefficient matrix has rank 11 along the whole family.

Equivalently, for every pair of quotient functionals L,ML,M,
L(v0)M(v1)вИТM(v0)L(v1)вЙ°0.
L(v0вАЛ)M(v1вАЛ)вИТM(v0вАЛ)L(v1вАЛ)вЙ°0.

So the first residual-sector lemma is equivalent to:
вИГвАЙL,M such that L(v0(h))M(v1(h))вИТM(v0(h))L(v1(h))вЙ°ћЄ0.
вИГL,M such that L(v0вАЛ(h))M(v1вАЛ(h))вИТM(v0вАЛ(h))L(v1вАЛ(h))оА†вЙ°0.вАЛ

This is the exact abstract determinant to attack.
3. Why this is the right object

This determinant is the order-free version of all the earlier minors D13,D15D13вАЛ,D15вАЛ.

Before, we were comparing absolute odd orders. Now we compare:

    the leading coefficient v0v0вАЛ,

    the next coefficient v1v1вАЛ in the normalized germ.

So the theorem no longer depends on whether that next coefficient sits at absolute order 33, 55, or 77.

That is exactly the abstraction you wanted.
4. A natural source of non-collinearity

There are really only two plausible mechanisms for v1v1вАЛ to escape the line of v0v0вАЛ:

one, a different symmetry sector appears in v1v1вАЛ that cannot occur in v0v0вАЛ;

two, the same sector appears but with a different hh-dependence, so the ratio v1/v0v1вАЛ/v0вАЛ cannot be scalar-valued in the quotient.

This suggests the most practical next move:

    identify the symmetry type of the generic leading line v0(h)v0вАЛ(h);

    identify the first next symmetry type that can appear in the quotient;

    show that next type is not forced into the same line.

That is the structural attack.
5. What the leading line probably is

From everything so far, the generic leading line v0(h)v0вАЛ(h) is very likely the first surviving one-pair quotient sector coming from the corrected first odd jet.

So, at least on a generic region, v0(h)v0вАЛ(h) should be one-dimensional.

That is good, because then MM can be chosen simply as any quotient functional annihilating that line.

So the whole problem becomes:
After killing the generic first quotient line, does the one-pair germ still have any nonzero next coefficient?
After killing the generic first quotient line, does the one-pair germ still have any nonzero next coefficient?вАЛ

That is exactly the residual-sector question.
6. A useful contradiction reformulation

Assume instead that
v1(h)=ќї1(h)v0(h)
v1вАЛ(h)=ќї1вАЛ(h)v0вАЛ(h)

on a generic region.

Then the normalized germ is constant mod z4z4. If also
v2(h)=ќї2(h)v0(h),
v2вАЛ(h)=ќї2вАЛ(h)v0вАЛ(h),

then it is constant mod z6z6, and so on.

So projective constancy is equivalent to the statement:

    every coefficient vj(h)vjвАЛ(h) lies in the same one-dimensional quotient line.

Thus the first residual-sector lemma is the first obstruction to this collapse.

This means you do not need to prove full nonconstancy at once. It is enough to prove that at some stage the coefficient sequence leaves the leading line.
7. The best next exact theorem

So the sharp next statement is:
First residual-sector determinant lemma

On a generic normalized one-pair parameter region, there exist quotient functionals L,ML,M such that
ќФ0,1(h):=L(v0(h))вАЙM(v1(h))вИТM(v0(h))вАЙL(v1(h))
ќФ0,1вАЛ(h):=L(v0вАЛ(h))M(v1вАЛ(h))вИТM(v0вАЛ(h))L(v1вАЛ(h))

is not identically zero.

Then the normalized one-pair quotient odd germ is not constant mod z2z2, hence not projectively constant.

That is the clean theorem.
8. If this still fails

2. How to attack W0,1W0,1вАЛ

There are two ways.

The structural way:

The analytic way:

Theorem schema

The local contradiction is reduced to the following two statements.

    Odd-germ nonvanishing. For every genuine normalized finite off-line core,

F\coreвЙ°ћЄ0.
F\coreвАЛоА†вЙ°0.

    Residual nontriviality. For every genuine normalized finite off-line core on the generic region,

FвАЊ\coreвЙ°ћЄ0.
F\coreвАЛоА†вЙ°0.

The first prevents total collapse into the value family. The second prevents projective collapse into a single quotient line.

Once these are established on compact normalized kk-core parameter sets, compactness yields a bounded odd order at which either the leading quotient sector or the residual sector survives, and the outside higher-odd-jet bounds may be compared at that order.
Compactness corollary

If F\coreвЙ°ћЄ0F\coreвАЛоА†вЙ°0 on a compact normalized kk-core family, then there exists RkRkвАЛ such that some quotient odd coefficient up to order 2Rk+12RkвАЛ+1 is nonzero.

That is the addition I would make. It is clean, it matches the direction the work actually moved, and it stops the paper from pretending that one fixed odd order is the theorem.

1. What the lemma really says

Write the one-pair quotient odd germ as
Fh(z)=v0(h)вАЙz2m+1+v1(h)вАЙz2m+3+v2(h)вАЙz2m+5+вЛѓвАЙ,v0(h)вЙ†0.
FhвАЛ(z)=v0вАЛ(h)z2m+1+v1вАЛ(h)z2m+3+v2вАЛ(h)z2m+5+вЛѓ,v0вАЛ(h)оА†=0.

Then the lemma says:
the coefficient curve hвЖ¶v1(h) is not everywhere collinear with hвЖ¶v0(h).
the coefficient curve hвЖ¶v1вАЛ(h) is not everywhere collinear with hвЖ¶v0вАЛ(h).вАЛ

If that is true, then after normalizing by the leading line, the residual germ is already nonzero at the first step. That would be excellent.

So the right contradiction setup is:

Assume
v1(h)=ќї(h)вАЙv0(h)for all h
v1вАЛ(h)=ќї(h)v0вАЛ(h)for all h

on a generic region, and try to derive something implausibly rigid.
2. Why this should be false structurally

The best reason is that v0v0вАЛ and v1v1вАЛ come from different pieces of the corrected local expansion.

Schematically:

    v0v0вАЛ is the first surviving quotient coefficient;

    v1v1вАЛ is the next coefficient after normalization by the leading line.

Even if both sit in the same crude symmetry sector, they are produced by different combinations of:

    same-point transport,

    mixed-block Taylor terms,

    nonlinear whitening corrections.

So v1=ќї(h)v0v1вАЛ=ќї(h)v0вАЛ for all hh would force a hidden recursive proportionality between two genuinely different coefficient mechanisms. That is exactly the sort of thing that is usually false unless there is a real integrable structure behind the model.

We have not seen evidence of such a structure. In fact, most of our computations point the other way: low orders sometimes collapse for symmetry reasons, but different mechanisms appear at later coefficients.

So the lemma is plausible.
3. The cleanest attack: derivative test

Assume
v1(h)=ќї(h)вАЙv0(h).
v1вАЛ(h)=ќї(h)v0вАЛ(h).

Then differentiating,
v1вА≤(h)=ќївА≤(h)вАЙv0(h)+ќї(h)вАЙv0вА≤(h).
v1вА≤вАЛ(h)=ќївА≤(h)v0вАЛ(h)+ќї(h)v0вА≤вАЛ(h).

So at every hh,
v0(h), v0вА≤(h), v1(h), v1вА≤(h)
v0вАЛ(h), v0вА≤вАЛ(h), v1вАЛ(h), v1вА≤вАЛ(h)

all lie in the same two-dimensional span:
spanвБ°{v0(h),v0вА≤(h)}.
span{v0вАЛ(h),v0вА≤вАЛ(h)}.

Equivalently,
v0(h)вИІv1(h)=0вЯєv0(h)вИІv1вА≤(h)=ќї(h)вАЙv0(h)вИІv0вА≤(h).
v0вАЛ(h)вИІv1вАЛ(h)=0вЯєv0вАЛ(h)вИІv1вА≤вАЛ(h)=ќї(h)v0вАЛ(h)вИІv0вА≤вАЛ(h).

So if we can show the derivative behavior of v1v1вАЛ is not compatible with that, the lemma follows.

This is nice because it turns the wedge lemma into a transversality statement about the two coefficient curves.
4. A more intrinsic version

This is the clean finite witness.
5. Best practical route: choose LL and MM by sector

M(v1вАЛ(h))оА†вЙ°0.вАЛ

That is the sharpest formulation.
6. How to look for M(v1)вЙ†0M(v1вАЛ)оА†=0

7. Why the earlier computations still help

Even though we want an order-free theorem, our earlier structural results still feed into this.

They suggest:

    the leading line is likely controlled by the first surviving low-order quotient sector;

    the next coefficient is produced by different recursive terms;

    symmetry can kill some candidates, but not plausibly all of them in the same line.

So the old jet calculations are still useful as evidence about the likely shape of v0v0вАЛ and v1v1вАЛ. They just are no longer the theorem.

That is the right relationship.

Ok before going down this route, will this really lead to a proof (eventually for all k)? Would it be better to take a derivative or use some inductive argument? What other approaches can we think of?

Honestly: maybe, but not yet in a way IвАЩd call likely.

The all-orders/projective-germ route is the cleanest framework weвАЩve found, but it is still missing the hard local theorem. Right now it feels like the right language for the paper, not yet a route IвАЩd trust to close RH all the way through every kk.

My current view:

The best organizing principle is still:
finite coreвАЕвАКвАЕвАК+вАЕвАКвАЕвАКoutside tail small at every odd orderвАЕвАКвАЕвАК+вАЕвАКвАЕвАКcore odd germ not projectively trivial.

show a genuine finite off-line core cannot make the quotient odd germ projectively collapse.

That is hard for one core size kk, and it is not yet clear it becomes easier uniformly in kk.

So if you ask вАЬis this the route that will eventually prove RH?вАЭ, my honest answer is:

    possibly, because it finally matches the obstruction structure we discovered;

    not yet convincingly, because we still do not have the local theorem even for k=2k=2.

Now, on your specific alternatives:
1. Would taking a derivative help?

Yes, but only as a tool, not as the main theorem.

There are two derivative ideas.

First, differentiate with respect to the core parameter hh.
This is useful because if the one-pair quotient germ were projectively constant, then
вИВhFh
вИВhвАЛFhвАЛ

would have to stay in the span of FhFhвАЛ. So a good contradiction format is:
вИВhFhвИЙCвАЙFh
вИВhвАЛFhвАЛвИИ/CFhвАЛвАЛ

in the quotient germ space.

That is basically the derivative version of the residual-wedge lemma, and I think it is a good idea. It may be cleaner than chasing v0вИІv1v0вАЛвИІv1вАЛ coefficient-by-coefficient.

Second, differentiate with respect to the transverse two-pair parameter near resonance.
That is what the Jacobian branch was doing. It is useful for killing isolated symmetric-locus candidates, but I do not think it is the main global route.

So: derivative methods are good, especially
FhвИІвИВhFhвЙ†0,
FhвАЛвИІвИВhвАЛFhвАЛоА†=0,

but they should serve the projective-germ theorem, not replace it.
2. Would induction on kk be better?

I think some kind of induction or minimal-counterexample argument is very likely necessary.

Not a naive induction like вАЬassume theorem for kвИТ1kвИТ1, prove for kkвАЭ by peeling one zero off. That seems too optimistic.

But a minimal bad core argument looks promising:

Assume there exists a genuine finite core with projectively trivial quotient odd germ. Choose one with minimal core size kk.

Then try to show either:

    it splits into smaller subcores whose odd germs would also have to be projectively trivial, contradicting minimality; or

    the parameters are forced into a highly resonant/degenerate locus.

That is much more believable.

So yes: induction/minimality is better than treating each kk independently.

If I were steering the paper, I would seriously consider structuring the local part around:
Assume a minimal bad finite core and derive rigidity/degeneracy.
Assume a minimal bad finite core and derive rigidity/degeneracy.вАЛ

That feels more likely to scale to all kk.
3. What other approaches should we think about?

I see five serious alternatives.
A. Projective-germ nonconstancy via hh-derivative

This is the closest cousin of what we are doing, but cleaner.

Try to prove for one-pair cores:
FhвИІвИВhFhвЙ°ћЄ0
FhвАЛвИІвИВhвАЛFhвАЛоА†вЙ°0

in the quotient germ space.

That immediately shows the projective germ curve is nonconstant.

I think this is probably the best next variation to try.
B. Minimal-counterexample / core-splitting

Assume a smallest bad finite core exists. Show its vanishing germ forces a decomposition or a resonance identity incompatible with minimality.

This feels the most plausible route to all kk.
C. Analytic-subset dimension argument

For fixed kk, the bad set
Zk={p:FpвЙ°0}
ZkвАЛ={p:FpвАЛвЙ°0}

is an analytic subset of finite-dimensional parameter space.

Try to prove it has codimension too large to contain a genuine family, or that it is contained in the degenerate locus.

This is elegant, but I do not yet see enough structure to make it bite without more explicit formulas.
D. Differential equation / transport equation for the germ curve

Try to derive an evolution law in hh for FhFhвАЛ:
вИВhFh=Th(Fh)+forcing.
вИВhвАЛFhвАЛ=ThвАЛ(FhвАЛ)+forcing.

Then show a projectively constant family would force an impossible scalar evolution.

This could be powerful if such an evolution equation can be written cleanly. Right now we have hints of this, not a usable formula.
E. Positivity / geometric invariant argument

Look for a monotone quantity or curvature-type invariant of the quotient odd germ that vanishes on value families but not on anomalous cores.

This would be ideal, but we do not currently have such an invariant. It is more of a wish than a plan.
4. What would I do next?

If the goal is the highest chance of a route that could scale to all kk, I would not keep drilling deeper into fixed orders.

I would do this:

First, reformulate the local theorem as:
For a genuine finite core, F\coreвЙ°ћЄ0 and is not projectively constant.
For a genuine finite core, F\coreвАЛоА†вЙ°0 and is not projectively constant.

Second, try to prove the one-pair projective nonconstancy by the derivative test
FhвИІвИВhFhвЙ†0.
FhвАЛвИІвИВhвАЛFhвАЛоА†=0.

Third, set up a minimal bad core contradiction for general kk, using the idea that a bad kk-core should force smaller bad subcores or a resonance degeneration.

That combination feels stronger than the current вАЬmaybe cubic, maybe quintic, maybe next odd orderвАЭ path.
5. My blunt recommendation

If I had to choose one direction now:
Yes to the all-orders/projective-germ framework, but pivot the proof attempt toward h-derivative + minimal bad core.
Yes to the all-orders/projective-germ framework, but pivot the proof attempt toward h-derivative + minimal bad core.вАЛ

That feels more likely to generalize to all kk than continuing to mine specific jets, even in a sophisticated way.

Try to compute or characterize FhвИІвИВhFh in the quotient germ space.
Try to compute or characterize FhвАЛвИІвИВhвАЛFhвАЛ in the quotient germ space.вАЛ

Yes. The next serious move is to attack the derivative criterion directly.

I think the cleanest local theorem now is:
FhвИІвИВhFhвЙ°ћЄ0in the quotient odd-germ space.
FhвАЛвИІвИВhвАЛFhвАЛоА†вЙ°0in the quotient odd-germ space.вАЛ

If that holds, then the one-pair quotient odd germ curve is projectively nonconstant immediately.

That is better than chasing specific odd orders, because it says the family is not trapped in a single projective line as hh varies.

Here is how I would set it up.
1. The exact contradiction format

Assume for contradiction that the one-pair quotient germ is projectively constant on a generic region. Then there exists a scalar function c(h)c(h) and a fixed quotient germ G(z)G(z) such that
Fh(z)=c(h)вАЙG(z).
FhвАЛ(z)=c(h)G(z).

Differentiate in hh:
вИВhFh(z)=cвА≤(h)вАЙG(z)=cвА≤(h)c(h)вАЙFh(z).

вИВhвАЛFhвАЛвИИCFhвАЛвАЛ

вИВhвАЛFhвАЛвИИ/CFhвАЛвАЛ

generically.

That is the derivative version of the residual-wedge lemma.

4. What I think is most likely

Based on everything so far, I think there are two realistic possibilities:
Possibility A

The leading quotient coefficient survives in a coefficient space of dimension >1>1, and its ratio moves with hh. Then the derivative route wins very early.
Possibility B

The leading quotient coefficient is generically trapped in one symmetry line. Then the derivative wedge vanishes, and the correct object is the residual coefficient in Q/вДУQ/вДУ.

Given how often the low-order trial models collapsed into a single symmetry channel, I think Possibility B is more likely.

So I would treat the derivative test as a checkpoint, not the main theorem.
5. The best next proof move

    v0(h)вИІv0вА≤(h)вЙ°ћЄ0;

7. My honest recommendation

I would now explicitly structure the paper around the dichotomy:

    first test leading-line motion;

    do not keep mixing the two.

That will make the local logic much clearer and keep us from drifting back into order-by-order improvisation.

That is the cleanest derivative criterion weвАЩve had.

It reduces the whole question to one thing:

    does the first subleading asymptotic correction leave the leading line?

That is much sharper than asking about the whole coefficient curve abstractly.
2. Why this is the right attack

This fits our situation perfectly.

The one-pair family is built from explicit rational kernels in hh, so any leading quotient coefficient should admit a Laurent-type asymptotic expansion at large вИ£hвИ£вИ£hвИ£. The question вАЬdoes its image dimension exceed 1?вАЭ becomes:

    either
    u1вИИCвАЙu0,
    u1вАЛвИИCu0вАЛ,

    and the leading line is ЎЂЎІЎ®Ў™ to first subleading order;

    or
    u1вИЙCвАЙu0,
    u1вАЛвИИ/Cu0вАЛ,

    and the derivative route wins immediately.

So the new next target is:
Compute the first two asymptotic coefficient vectors u0,u1 of v0(h).
Compute the first two asymptotic coefficient vectors u0вАЛ,u1вАЛ of v0вАЛ(h).вАЛ

If they are not collinear, we are done with projective nonconstancy of the one-pair family.
3. What this would mean for the paper

This gives a very clean lemma:
Lemma (asymptotic leading-line motion)

Let v0(h)v0вАЛ(h) be the generic leading quotient coefficient of the one-pair family. Suppose
v0(h)=hвИТp(u0+hвИТ1u1+O(hвИТ2))

v0(h)вИІv0вА≤(h)вЙ°ћЄ0
v0вАЛ(h)вИІv0вА≤вАЛ(h)оА†вЙ°0

for all sufficiently large вИ£hвИ£вИ£hвИ£. Consequently the one-pair quotient odd germ is projectively nonconstant.

That is a real theorem target, and it no longer depends on guessing which odd order matters.
4. How to get u0,u1u0вАЛ,u1вАЛ

5. What I think is likely

This is my honest guess:

    but there is a good chance the subleading correction leaks into a second quotient direction.

This is still much cleaner than picking absolute odd orders.
7. Why this is better than the previous residual-coefficient plan

Because it may succeed before we need to pass to the residual quotient bundle.

Only if all asymptotic corrections of v0(h)v0вАЛ(h) stay in one line do we need the residual-germ theorem.

So this is the right next attack sequence:

    asymptotic motion of leading line;

    if that fails, residual quotient line;

    if that fails, higher residuals.

That is a clean hierarchy.

But I would make two adjustments.
1. I would not present it as only a one-pair theorem

I would present it as the local model theorem schema for finite cores:

    first show the full quotient odd germ is nonzero;

    then quotient by the generic leading line;

    then show the residual germ is nonzero.

That is the right conceptual ladder. For k=2k=2, you test it on the one-pair building block and then use it to obstruct two-pair cancellation. For larger kk, the same language still makes sense.

So this is not just a tactical trick. It is the right local architecture.
2. I would pair it with a minimal-bad-core viewpoint

By itself, the residual-germ route is still local. To have a credible path to all kk, I think you need to combine it with:
assume a minimal bad finite core and derive contradiction from its projectively trivial odd germ.
assume a minimal bad finite core and derive contradiction from its projectively trivial odd germ.вАЛ

That is what makes it feel scalable.

If you only do вАЬone-pair germ, then two-pair germ, then maybe three-pair germ...вАЭ, it may become a swamp.

If instead the paper says:

    let a bad core be one whose quotient odd germ is projectively trivial;

    choose one with minimal size;

    show leading-line and residual-germ structure force it into a degenerate/resonant split;

then the route has a real shot of scaling.

So the framework I would back is:
full odd quotient germвАЕвАК+вАЕвАКquotient by leading lineвАЕвАК+вАЕвАКresidual germ nontrivialityвАЕвАК+вАЕвАКminimal bad core.
full odd quotient germ+quotient by leading line+residual germ nontriviality+minimal bad core.вАЛ

That feels like the right long-term direction.
Why I prefer this over the alternatives

Derivative test:
good as a probe, not as the main theorem. It may help at the first step, but I would not build the paper around it.

Fixed odd orders:
useful only as witnesses. Not the right organizing principle.

Pure Jacobian/transversality:
good locally, but too tied to finite truncations unless backed by the full-germ picture.

So yes, I would go on this path.

That is the exact next target.
2. What this gives immediately

If FвАЊhвЙ°ћЄ0FhвАЛоА†вЙ°0, then the one-pair quotient odd germ is not projectively constant. So a two-pair cancellation
a1Fh1(z)+a2Fh2(z)вЙ°0
a1вАЛFh1вАЛвАЛ(z)+a2вАЛFh2вАЛвАЛ(z)вЙ°0

forces
[Fh1]=[Fh2]
[Fh1вАЛвАЛ]=[Fh2вАЛвАЛ]

in projective quotient-germ space, which is already a very small condition.

So this is the right local theorem.
3. The first paper addition I would write now

I would add a short theorem-definition block like this.
Definition (leading line and residual germ)

On a connected parameter region where the one-pair quotient odd germ
Fh(z)=вИСjвЙ•0vj(h)вАЙz2m+1+2j
FhвАЛ(z)=jвЙ•0вИСвАЛvjвАЛ(h)z2m+1+2j

has fixed leading order 2m+12m+1 and v0(h)вЙ†0v0вАЛ(h)оА†=0, define the leading line
вДУh:=CвАЙv0(h)вКВQ
вДУhвАЛ:=Cv0вАЛ(h)вКВQ

and the residual germ
FвАЊh(z):=вИСjвЙ•1vвАЊj(h)вАЙz2jвИИ(Q/вДУh)[[z2]],
FhвАЛ(z):=jвЙ•1вИСвАЛvjвАЛ(h)z2jвИИ(Q/вДУhвАЛ)[[z2]],

where vвАЊj(h)vjвАЛ(h) denotes the class of vj(h)vjвАЛ(h) in Q/вДУhQ/вДУhвАЛ.
Conjectural local theorem / target theorem

For every genuine normalized one-pair off-line core, the residual germ is nonzero:
FвАЊh(z)вЙ°ћЄ0.
FhвАЛ(z)оА†вЙ°0.
Corollary (projective nonconstancy)

If FвАЊhвЙ°ћЄ0FhвАЛоА†вЙ°0, then the one-pair quotient odd germ Fh(z)FhвАЛ(z) is not projectively constant in hh.

That is the right formal pivot.
4. How to attack it

The right first sublemma is not вАЬsome absolute odd order survives.вАЭ

It is:
the first residual coefficient vвАЊ1(h)вИИQ/вДУh is not identically zero,
the first residual coefficient v1вАЛ(h)вИИQ/вДУhвАЛ is not identically zero,вАЛ

or failing that, the first jвЙ•1jвЙ•1 with
vвАЊj(h)вЙ°ћЄ0.
vjвАЛ(h)оА†вЙ°0.

So the immediate attack is coefficientwise in the residual quotient, not in the original quotient.

That is much cleaner.
5. The exact contradiction format

Assume for contradiction that
FвАЊh(z)вЙ°0.
FhвАЛ(z)вЙ°0.

Then every coefficient lies in the leading line:
vj(h)вИИвДУh=CвАЙv0(h)вИАjвЙ•1.
vjвАЛ(h)вИИвДУhвАЛ=Cv0вАЛ(h)вИАjвЙ•1.

So there are scalar functions ќїj(h)ќїjвАЛ(h) such that
vj(h)=ќїj(h)вАЙv0(h).
vjвАЛ(h)=ќїjвАЛ(h)v0вАЛ(h).

Hence
Fh(z)=v0(h)(z2m+1+ќї1(h)z2m+3+ќї2(h)z2m+5+вЛѓвАЙ),
FhвАЛ(z)=v0вАЛ(h)(z2m+1+ќї1вАЛ(h)z2m+3+ќї2вАЛ(h)z2m+5+вЛѓ),

so the full quotient odd germ lies in a single projective line.

This is exactly the bad case.

So to prove residual nontriviality, it is enough to show that at some stage
vj(h)вИЙCвАЙv0(h).
vjвАЛ(h)вИИ/Cv0вАЛ(h).
6. The clean first target

So the first real sublemma is:
First residual-line lemma

On the generic one-pair region,
v1(h)вИЙCвАЙv0(h)
v1вАЛ(h)вИИ/Cv0вАЛ(h)

for generic hh.

Equivalently,
v0(h)вИІv1(h)вЙ°ћЄ0.
v0вАЛ(h)вИІv1вАЛ(h)оА†вЙ°0.

This is the clean residual-wedge formulation, but now it is clearly subordinate to the full-germ theorem.
7. How I would try to prove it

I would try three attacks in this order.

First, leading-line motion:
if the leading line itself moves with hh, then projective constancy is already false and you do not need residuals. This is the derivative check
v0(h)вИІv0вА≤(h)вЙ°ћЄ0.
v0вАЛ(h)вИІv0вА≤вАЛ(h)оА†вЙ°0.

Second, if the leading line is fixed, pass to the residual quotient and test
vвАЊ1(h)вЙ°ћЄ0.
v1вАЛ(h)оА†вЙ°0.

Third, if vвАЊ1вЙ°0v1вАЛвЙ°0, move to vвАЊ2v2вАЛ, etc.

That gives a very clean hierarchy:
leading line motionвАЕвАКвЖТвАЕвАКfirst residualвАЕвАКвЖТвАЕвАКhigher residuals.
leading line motionвЖТfirst residualвЖТhigher residuals.
8. What I think is likely

I would not be surprised if the leading-line motion fails in the real model too. So I would treat it as a quick probe, not the main event.

The main event is probably:
show the residual germ in Q/вДУh is nonzero.
show the residual germ in Q/вДУhвАЛ is nonzero.вАЛ

That feels like the theorem that actually matches the structure we kept finding.

Ok attack the lemma

Here is the strongest honest attack on the first residual-line lemma:
v0(h)вИІv1(h)вЙ°ћЄ0.
v0вАЛ(h)вИІv1вАЛ(h)оА†вЙ°0.

I cannot fully prove it from the formulas we currently have, but I can reduce it to the exact structural condition that has to fail for the lemma to be false.
1. What the lemma says in concrete terms

On a generic one-pair region,
Fh(z)=v0(h)вАЙz2m+1+v1(h)вАЙz2m+3+вЛѓ
FhвАЛ(z)=v0вАЛ(h)z2m+1+v1вАЛ(h)z2m+3+вЛѓ

with v0(h)вЙ†0v0вАЛ(h)оА†=0.

The lemma says:
v1(h)вИЙCвАЙv0(h)
v1вАЛ(h)вИИ/Cv0вАЛ(h)

for generic hh.

If it failed, there would be a scalar function ќї(h)ќї(h) such that
v1(h)=ќї(h)вАЙv0(h).
v1вАЛ(h)=ќї(h)v0вАЛ(h).

So the normalized germ would be constant mod z2z2.

That is the bad case.
2. The clean coefficient test

Pick two quotient functionals L,ML,M on the leading coefficient space QQ. Then
v0(h)вИІv1(h)вЙ°ћЄ0
v0вАЛ(h)вИІv1вАЛ(h)оА†вЙ°0

is equivalent to finding L,ML,M with
ќФ0,1(h):=L(v0(h))вАЙM(v1(h))вИТM(v0(h))вАЙL(v1(h))вЙ°ћЄ0.
ќФ0,1вАЛ(h):=L(v0вАЛ(h))M(v1вАЛ(h))вИТM(v0вАЛ(h))L(v1вАЛ(h))оА†вЙ°0.

The best choice is:

    LL does not vanish on the leading line;

    MM kills the leading line.

Then
M(v0(h))=0,
M(v0вАЛ(h))=0,

so the determinant collapses to
ќФ0,1(h)=L(v0(h))вАЙM(v1(h)).
ќФ0,1вАЛ(h)=L(v0вАЛ(h))M(v1вАЛ(h)).

Thus the lemma is reduced to:
вИГвАЙMвИИ(Q/вДУh)вИЧ such that M(v1(h))вЙ°ћЄ0.
вИГMвИИ(Q/вДУhвАЛ)вИЧ such that M(v1вАЛ(h))оА†вЙ°0.вАЛ

This is the clean residual-sector form.
3. What would force the lemma to fail

For the lemma to fail, every residual functional MM must kill v1(h)v1вАЛ(h). Equivalently,
vвАЊ1(h)=0вИИQ/вДУh
v1вАЛ(h)=0вИИQ/вДУhвАЛ

for all hh.

That means the first two coefficients of the one-pair quotient germ are trapped in the same line. Structurally, this would require a hidden proportionality between two different stages of the corrected local expansion:

    the leading surviving quotient mechanism,

    and the next mechanism after normalization.

Given how different pieces enter the expansion вАФ same-point transport, mixed-block Taylor terms, nonlinear whitening вАФ this is already quite rigid.

So the right attack is to look for the first mechanism contributing to v1v1вАЛ that cannot lie in the leading line.
4. The strongest structural reduction

There are only two ways v1v1вАЛ can escape вДУhвДУhвАЛ:

First, it appears in a genuinely different quotient symmetry sector.

Second, it appears in the same coarse sector but with a different quotient-direction profile, so its image in Q/вДУhQ/вДУhвАЛ is still nonzero.

So the lemma will follow if we can show:
the first residual coefficient contains at least one contribution not forced into the leading quotient line.
the first residual coefficient contains at least one contribution not forced into the leading quotient line.вАЛ

That is now the exact obstruction.
5. Derivative fallback relation

Assume for contradiction
v1(h)=ќї(h)v0(h).
v1вАЛ(h)=ќї(h)v0вАЛ(h).

Differentiate:
v1вА≤(h)=ќївА≤(h)v0(h)+ќї(h)v0вА≤(h).
v1вА≤вАЛ(h)=ќївА≤(h)v0вАЛ(h)+ќї(h)v0вА≤вАЛ(h).

So if also the leading line were fixed, meaning
v0вА≤(h)вИИCвАЙv0(h),
v0вА≤вАЛ(h)вИИCv0вАЛ(h),

then
v1вА≤(h)вИИCвАЙv0(h)
v1вА≤вАЛ(h)вИИCv0вАЛ(h)

as well.

In other words, failure of both the derivative test and the first residual-line lemma would force the first two coefficient curves and their derivatives to stay in one projective line.

That is extremely rigid.

So even if we cannot prove the lemma directly, we now know exactly what a counterexample would have to look like:

    fixed leading line,

    fixed first residual line,

    no transverse escape at first derivative level.

That is a very small target.
6. Minimal-bad-core interpretation

This is where the lemma fits the larger route.

If a genuine bad core existed with projectively trivial odd germ, then for a minimal bad core the one-pair building blocks would have to be projectively trivial to first residual order.

So the lemma is really trying to rule out the first step of that collapse.

That is why it matters.
7. Honest status of the attack

What I can defend:

    the lemma is equivalent to nonvanishing of the first residual class vвАЊ1(h)v1вАЛ(h);

    proving it amounts to finding one residual functional MM with M(v1(h))вЙ°ћЄ0M(v1вАЛ(h))оА†вЙ°0;

    failure of the lemma imposes a very rigid line-trapping condition on both v0v0вАЛ and v1v1вАЛ.

What I cannot yet defend:

    an actual proof that such an MM exists, because we still do not have the explicit quotient basis or residual coefficient formula in the real model.

So the attack has reduced the lemma to one precise missing computation.

How do we get this: still do not have the actual quotient basis at the generic leading order.

You get it by turning the abstract quotient into a finite linear algebra computation at the first surviving odd order.

Right now вАЬwe donвАЩt have the actual quotient basisвАЭ just means we have not yet carried out these four steps on the real corrected formulas.
1. Identify the generic leading odd order

For the one-pair corrected germ, expand the raw corrected block
ќ©^\corr(s;h)=вИСrвЙ•0J2r+1\pair(h)вАЙs2r+1+even terms.

V\val(2r+1)вАЛвКВJ2r+1вАЛ.

Then the generic leading order 2m+12m+1 is the smallest odd order for which
[J2m+1\pair(h)]вЙ†0inQ:=J2m+1/V\val(2m+1)
[J2m+1\pairвАЛ(h)]оА†=0inQ:=J2m+1вАЛ/V\val(2m+1)вАЛ

2. Compute the actual value-family subspace at that order

This is the part we have been postponing.

At order 2m+12m+1, you need the real value/gauge generators, not the trial ones.

For each infinitesimal value/gauge parameter ќЄвДУќЄвДУвАЛ, compute the corrected odd jet matrix at that order:
BвДУ(2m+1):=1(2m+1)!dвАЙ2m+1dsвАЙ2m+1ќ©^\val\corr(s;ќЄвДУ)вИ£s=0,ќЄ=0.

\val\corrвАЛ(s;ќЄвДУвАЛ)
вАЛs=0,ќЄ=0вАЛ.

Then
V\val(2m+1)=spanвБ°{B1(2m+1),вА¶,Br(2m+1)}.
V\val(2m+1)вАЛ=span{B1(2m+1)вАЛ,вА¶,Br(2m+1)вАЛ}.

This is the actual subspace you quotient by.

So the phrase вАЬwe still do not have the actual quotient basisвАЭ really means:
we have not yet computed and row-reduced the genuine BвДУ(2m+1) from the corrected value model.
we have not yet computed and row-reduced the genuine BвДУ(2m+1)вАЛ from the corrected value model.вАЛ
3. Build the quotient basis by annihilators

Once the real BвДУ(2m+1)BвДУ(2m+1)вАЛ are in hand, the quotient basis is automatic.

Vectorize each 2√Ч22√Ч2 jet matrix into C4C4:
(x11x12x21x22)вЖ¶(x11,x12,x21,x22).
(x11вАЛx21вАЛвАЛx12вАЛx22вАЛвАЛ)вЖ¶(x11вАЛ,x12вАЛ,x21вАЛ,x22вАЛ).

Form the matrix

5. So what is the exact bottleneck?

Not abstraction. Not lack of a theorem statement.

The actual bottleneck is:
compute the genuine value/gauge generators BвДУ(2m+1) from the corrected model and take their annihilator basis.
compute the genuine value/gauge generators BвДУ(2m+1)вАЛ from the corrected model and take their annihilator basis.вАЛ

6. The shortest operational route

I would do it in this exact order:

First, at order 11, compute the real value-family generators and their annihilator basis.

If the one-pair quotient at order 11 is generically nonzero, then 2m+1=12m+1=1 and you already have the leading quotient basis.

If order 11 collapses, repeat at order 33, then 55, and so on until the first generic nonzero quotient appears.

That is how you get the actual basis at the generic leading order.
7. In plain language

You get it by:

    computing the corrected value-family jets at the first order where the anomalous one-pair jet survives,

    spanning the value-family subspace there,

    taking the orthogonal complement / annihilator,

    and using that as the quotient basis.

That is the whole construction. The reason we вАЬdonвАЩt yet have itвАЭ is simply that we have not done that row-reduction on the real corrected generators yet.

now has explicit inputs:

as above.

ќЈ+=ќЈвИТ=ќ±,ќЈ¬±вА≤=ќЈ¬±вА≤вА≤=0,ќЇ(s)=вИТќ±s,

expand at s=0s=0. The singular powers cancel exactly in the symmetric setup, and the first odd coefficient has the generic form
NЋЩm(s)=NЋЩ0+sвАЙNЋЩ1+O(s2),

    the off-diagonal odd part contains an antisymmetric contribution from the mismatch between ќЈ+ќЈ+вАЛ and ќЈвИТќЈвИТвАЛ,

So unlike the overly simplified frozen model, the genuine first value-family generator is not forced into one pure skew line before whitening.

That is the big conceptual payoff of doing this real layer.
5. Push through linearized whitening

Now plug

This is the thing we were trying to reach.
6. What this means

The first odd value-family span V\val(1)V\val(1)вАЛ is generated by matrices of the form

    ќЈ(3)(m)ќЈ(3)(m),

That is already enough to say something important:
V\val(1) in the real corrected model is generally larger than the frozen pure-value skew line.
V\val(1)вАЛ in the real corrected model is generally larger than the frozen pure-value skew line.вАЛ

So the earlier minimal-model collapses were too optimistic as guides to the real quotient space.
7. The next exact move

Now the task is purely linear algebra:

choose basis perturbations corresponding to each independent infinitesimal datum
ќЈ(m), ќЈвА≤(m), ќЈвА≤вА≤(m), ќЈ(3)(m), ќЇ1,
ќЈ(m), ќЈвА≤(m), ќЈвА≤вА≤(m), ќЈ(3)(m), ќЇ1вАЛ,

compute the corresponding five generator matrices B1B1вАЛ, vectorize them, row-reduce, and compute the annihilator basis of V\val(1)V\val(1)вАЛ.

That will tell us:

    the true dimension of the first-order value-family,

    the actual quotient basis at order 11,

    and whether order 11 is the generic leading quotient order for the one-pair family.

That is the first real executable checkpoint.

3. Structure of the mixed first odd coefficient NЋЩ1NЋЩ1вАЛ

We do not need every coefficient simplified all the way to see the span structure.

ќЈ(m), ќЈвА≤(m), ќЈвА≤вА≤(m), q0(m), q0вА≤(m), ќЇ1, ќФ1.

    the eќЇeќЇвАЛ direction definitely contributes a nonzero (1,1)(1,1)-entry and also contributes to (1,2),(2,1),(2,2)(1,2),(2,1),(2,2);

    the e0,e1,e2e0вАЛ,e1вАЛ,e2вАЛ directions contribute to off-diagonal and (2,2)(2,2)-entries;

    the e3e3вАЛ direction is mostly same-point and may have no mixed contribution at first odd order.

So unlike the frozen toy model, the real V\val(1)V\val(1)вАЛ is generically generated by:

    several symmetric directions from GЋЩ1GЋЩ1вАЛ,

    plus a genuinely phase-sensitive mixed direction from ќЇ1ќЇ1вАЛ.

That already strongly suggests V\val(1)V\val(1)вАЛ is not just the skew line.
4. The actual first-order generator matrices

6. What this already tells us qualitatively

This is the important takeaway:

the real corrected first-order value-family is large enough that the earlier minimal frozen-model intuition is no longer trustworthy.

So the real order-11 quotient could easily still be nontrivial, but the quotient coordinates must be built from the actual annihilator basis, not guessed from symmetry alone.

That is progress: we now know exactly what object decides it.
7. The next exact subtask

Comtinue

Yes. Pushing the expansion one more step gives the missing real layer.

The key correction is that the mixed first variation is only regular if the phase perturbation and value perturbation satisfy the first-order cancellation
ќЇ1=вИТќЈ(m).
ќЇ1вАЛ=вИТќЈ(m).

That is exactly what happened in the frozen pure-value slice, and it is the general regularity condition that kills the 1/s1/s singularity in the off-diagonal mixed entries.

Once you impose that, the first odd coefficient becomes very clean.
1. Local Taylor data

4. Push through linearized whitening

5. Independent infinitesimal directions

After the regularity relation ќЇ1=вИТќЈ0ќЇ1вАЛ=вИТќЈ0вАЛ, the free first-order data are

    coming from X3X3вАЛ and X2N0X1вИТX1N0X2X2вАЛN0вАЛX1вАЛвИТX1вАЛN0вАЛX2вАЛ;

    mixed cubic source
    BвДУ,\mix(3),
    BвДУ,\mix(3)вАЛ,

    coming from NЋЩ3NЋЩ3вАЛ, NЋЩ2NЋЩ2вАЛ coupled to X1X1вАЛ, and NЋЩ1NЋЩ1вАЛ coupled to X2X2вАЛ.

Then row-reduce the combined span.

This separation will tell us whether the cubic value-family again fills all of M2M2вАЛ, or whether some symmetry sector escapes.
7. What happens if cubic is still full?

Then we do not panic. It just means the generic leading quotient order is at least 55, and the same architecture continues.

That is exactly why the full-germ / residual-germ framework is the right one:
it does not care whether the first surviving quotient order is 33, 55, or 77.

So even a negative result at cubic order is not fatal. It just pushes the first leading line upward.
8. My honest expectation

After the order-11 result, I think there are two realistic outcomes:

    cubic is still too large, but not obviously full;

    cubic is the first nontrivial quotient order.

I would not assume which one. That is why this checkpoint matters.
9. The immediate next exact move

So the exact next move is:
compute the real cubic value-family generators BвДУ(3) and determine whether V\val(3)=M2(C) or not.
compute the real cubic value-family generators BвДУ(3)вАЛ and determine whether V\val(3)вАЛ=M2вАЛ(C) or not.вАЛ

That is the right continuation now.

Continue

Now split it as

The first useful observation is structural.

At order 11, the real value-family filled all of M2M2вАЛ because:

    the same-point data generated the full symmetric sector;

    the mixed phase data generated the skew line.

At order 33, that mechanism becomes much more constrained because X2X2вАЛ and X3X3вАЛ are not free. They are recursively determined by lower jets. So cubic value-family fullness is no longer automatic.

The next thing to check is symmetry.

In the diagonal-baseline frame, if the order-11 same-point data are symmetric, then X1,X2,X3X1вАЛ,X2вАЛ,X3вАЛ are all symmetric. With N0N0вАЛ symmetric, the same-point cubic term
Bsp(3)
Bsp(3)вАЛ

has the same commutator pattern we saw before. In the oversimplified model this collapsed to the skew line, but in the real model X3X3вАЛ and X2X2вАЛ now depend on the full local Taylor data, so that collapse is no longer forced. The only honest statement at this point is:
Bsp(3) is built from symmetric X1,X2,X3 and symmetric N0,
Bsp(3)вАЛ is built from symmetric X1вАЛ,X2вАЛ,X3вАЛ and symmetric N0вАЛ,

so its symmetric and skew parts are heavily constrained, but not obviously trivial.

On the mixed side:

    N1N1вАЛ is skew at first odd order after regularity,

    N2N2вАЛ is even and typically symmetric,

    N3N3вАЛ is odd again and can carry both phase and value-induced cubic data.

So
Bmix(3)
Bmix(3)вАЛ

contains three qualitatively different sources:

    a skew source through N1N1вАЛ coupled to X2X2вАЛ,

    a symmetric source through N2N2вАЛ coupled to X1X1вАЛ,

    a direct cubic source through N3N3вАЛ.

That is exactly why order 33 is the first serious candidate: unlike order 11, the cubic mixed family is no longer just вАЬone skew line plus symmetric span.вАЭ It is recursively entangled.

So the right next reduction is this:

pick the same five infinitesimal data from the regularized order-11 model,
ќЈ0, ќЈ1, ќЈ2, ќЈ3, ќЇ3,
ќЈ0вАЛ, ќЈ1вАЛ, ќЈ2вАЛ, ќЈ3вАЛ, ќЇ3вАЛ,

B(0)(3), B(1)(3), B(2)(3), B(3)(3), B(ќЇ)(3).
B(0)(3)вАЛ, B(1)(3)вАЛ, B(2)(3)вАЛ, B(3)(3)вАЛ, B(ќЇ)(3)вАЛ.

Then vectorize them and row-reduce.

At that point there are only two outcomes.

V\val(3)=M2(C)
V\val(3)вАЛ=M2вАЛ(C)

v0(h)=[J3\pair(h)]вИИJ3/V\val(3).

That is the real continuation now.

My honest expectation is that cubic will no longer automatically fill all of M2M2вАЛ. Order 11 did because the first-order regularized data gave four independent matrix directions almost for free. At cubic order, the recursive constraints should reduce freedom enough that a quotient may survive.

So this is the first place where the full-germ residual program has a serious chance of starting.

2. What is likely to be value-like automatically

    X1X1вАЛ is symmetric,

4. Immediate consequence for the cubic checkpoint

This means the mixed cubic pieces behave as follows:

    N1N1вАЛ is skew,

    N2N2вАЛ is symmetric,

    N3N3вАЛ is skew.

B(3)=Bsp(3)+BN1(3)+BN2(3)+BN3(3),

the only genuinely new mixed symmetry source is N2N2вАЛ, because both N1N1вАЛ and N3N3вАЛ feed skew channels directly.

More concretely:
BN3(3)=X0N3X0
BN3вАЛ(3)вАЛ=X0вАЛN3вАЛX0вАЛ

is automatically skew.

couples symmetric X1X1вАЛ with symmetric N2N2вАЛ, and this is the first mixed cubic term that can plausibly generate a new non-skew quotient direction.

So the cubic attack narrows further:
The first truly promising cubic escape from the order-1 value span is BN2(3).
The first truly promising cubic escape from the order-1 value span is BN2вАЛ(3)вАЛ.вАЛ

6. Why this is good news

This is much smaller than вАЬcompute all cubic generators.вАЭ

    N3N3вАЛ is skew, so by itself it is unlikely to produce new quotient directions;

    N1N1вАЛ-couplings are also likely to recycle skew behavior;

    the mixed symmetric quadratic term N2N2вАЛ is the first place real new cubic quotient information can enter.

So the next real attack is focused.

spanвБ°{T1,T2,T3,K0}=M2(C),
span{T1вАЛ,T2вАЛ,T3вАЛ,K0вАЛ}=M2вАЛ(C),

testing whether a cubic term вАЬescapes the order-1 spanвАЭ is vacuous. Nothing escapes M2M2вАЛ.

So the real cubic checkpoint is only:
does V\val(3) itself fill M2(C) generically, or not?
does V\val(3)вАЛ itself fill M2вАЛ(C) generically, or not?вАЛ

That said, we can still compute the N2N2вАЛ-piece structurally, because it tells us what symmetry types cubic value-generators can have.
1. Compute BN2(3)BN2вАЛ(3)вАЛ in general form

Write
X1=(uvvw),N2=(ABBD),X0=(x100x2).
X1вАЛ=(uvвАЛvwвАЛ),N2вАЛ=(ABвАЛBDвАЛ),X0вАЛ=(x1вАЛ0вАЛ0x2вАЛвАЛ).

Then
BN2(3)=вИТвАЙX1N2X0+X0N2X1.
BN2вАЛ(3)вАЛ=вИТX1вАЛN2вАЛX0вАЛ+X0вАЛN2вАЛX1вАЛ.

A direct multiplication gives
BN2(3)=(0Avx1+Bwx1вИТx2(Bu+Dv)Bux2+Dvx2вИТx1(Av+Bw)0).
BN2вАЛ(3)вАЛ=(0Bux2вАЛ+Dvx2вАЛвИТx1вАЛ(Av+Bw)вАЛAvx1вАЛ+Bwx1вАЛвИТx2вАЛ(Bu+Dv)0вАЛ).вАЛ

So:

    the diagonal entries are always zero;

    the off-diagonal entries are generally independent.

That means BN2(3)BN2вАЛ(3)вАЛ is off-diagonal traceless, but not automatically skew and not automatically symmetric.

That is already useful.
2. Symmetry consequences

If
x1=x2,
x1вАЛ=x2вАЛ,

then the two off-diagonal entries become negatives of each other, so BN2(3)BN2вАЛ(3)вАЛ is skew.

But generically x1вЙ†x2x1вАЛоА†=x2вАЛ, so the off-diagonal entries are not forced to be negatives. Thus:
generically BN2(3) can produce both the symmetric off-diagonal and skew off-diagonal directions.
generically BN2вАЛ(3)вАЛ can produce both the symmetric off-diagonal and skew off-diagonal directions.вАЛ

That is the first real sign that cubic value-generators may be richer than the simplified skew-only models suggested.
3. What this means for the cubic span

At cubic order we now know:

    BN3(3)=X0N3X0BN3вАЛ(3)вАЛ=X0вАЛN3вАЛX0вАЛ is skew;

    BN1(3)BN1вАЛ(3)вАЛ is strongly skew-leaning;

    BN2(3)BN2вАЛ(3)вАЛ is generic off-diagonal traceless, so it can contribute a symmetric off-diagonal piece as well.

So the cubic value-family is at least not confined to one skew line.

That is a real correction to the earlier oversimplified intuition.
4. But this still does not decide V\val(3)V\val(3)вАЛ

To know whether
V\val(3)=M2(C)
V\val(3)вАЛ=M2вАЛ(C)

generically, we still need to know whether the remaining cubic sources provide diagonal directions.

Those can only come from:

    the same-point cubic term Bsp(3)Bsp(3)вАЛ,

    and possibly some diagonal component inside the N1N1вАЛ-coupled term, though that looks less likely.

So the next focused question is:
Does Bsp(3)=вИТвАЙX3N0X0+X2N0X1вИТX1N0X2+X0N0X3 generate diagonal directions generically?
Does Bsp(3)вАЛ=вИТX3вАЛN0вАЛX0вАЛ+X2вАЛN0вАЛX1вАЛвИТX1вАЛN0вАЛX2вАЛ+X0вАЛN0вАЛX3вАЛ generate diagonal directions generically?вАЛ

If yes, then together with the off-diagonal directions from N2N2вАЛ and skew from N3N3вАЛ, cubic value-family may again fill all of M2M2вАЛ.

If no, then cubic quotient survives.
5. The actual next move

So the next exact computation is:
compute the diagonal part of Bsp(3) for the regularized basis directions.
compute the diagonal part of Bsp(3)вАЛ for the regularized basis directions.вАЛ

That is the decisive cubic checkpoint now.

The reason is simple:

    BN2(3)BN2вАЛ(3)вАЛ already gives off-diagonal traceless directions;

    BN3(3)BN3вАЛ(3)вАЛ gives skew;

    only Bsp(3)Bsp(3)вАЛ can plausibly complete the diagonal sector.

So the whole order-33 question has now been reduced to whether the same-point cubic term supplies independent diagonal directions.

Continue

There is a decisive simplification.

For the general symmetric same-point cubic term, the diagonal part vanishes identically.

So the same-point cubic source cannot refill the diagonal sector at all.
1. Compute Bsp(3)Bsp(3)вАЛ in full generality

Write
X0=(x100x2),N0=(abbd),
X0вАЛ=(x1вАЛ0вАЛ0x2вАЛвАЛ),N0вАЛ=(abвАЛbdвАЛ),

and let
X1=(uvvw),X2=(prrq),X3=(ќ±ќ≤ќ≤ќ≥)
X1вАЛ=(uvвАЛvwвАЛ),X2вАЛ=(prвАЛrqвАЛ),X3вАЛ=(ќ±ќ≤вАЛќ≤ќ≥вАЛ)

be arbitrary symmetric matrices.

Then
Bsp(3)=вИТвАЙX3N0X0+X2N0X1вИТX1N0X2+X0N0X3.
Bsp(3)вАЛ=вИТX3вАЛN0вАЛX0вАЛ+X2вАЛN0вАЛX1вАЛвИТX1вАЛN0вАЛX2вАЛ+X0вАЛN0вАЛX3вАЛ.

Now split it into:

    the X3X3вАЛ-transport part
    вИТX3N0X0+X0N0X3,
    вИТX3вАЛN0вАЛX0вАЛ+X0вАЛN0вАЛX3вАЛ,

    and the commutator part
    X2N0X1вИТX1N0X2.
    X2вАЛN0вАЛX1вАЛвИТX1вАЛN0вАЛX2вАЛ.

First piece

Because X0,N0,X3X0вАЛ,N0вАЛ,X3вАЛ are symmetric and X0X0вАЛ is diagonal, one checks directly that
вИТвАЙX3N0X0+X0N0X3
вИТX3вАЛN0вАЛX0вАЛ+X0вАЛN0вАЛX3вАЛ

has zero diagonal.
Second piece

For arbitrary symmetric X1,X2,N0X1вАЛ,X2вАЛ,N0вАЛ,
(X2N0X1вИТX1N0X2)11=0,(X2N0X1вИТX1N0X2)22=0.
(X2вАЛN0вАЛX1вАЛвИТX1вАЛN0вАЛX2вАЛ)11вАЛ=0,(X2вАЛN0вАЛX1вАЛвИТX1вАЛN0вАЛX2вАЛ)22вАЛ=0.

So altogether,
(Bsp(3))11=(Bsp(3))22=0.
(Bsp(3)вАЛ)11вАЛ=(Bsp(3)вАЛ)22вАЛ=0.вАЛ

In fact, the off-diagonal entries are negatives of each other, so
Bsp(3)=(0ѕЙвИТѕЙ0)
Bsp(3)вАЛ=(0вИТѕЙвАЛѕЙ0вАЛ)вАЛ

for some scalar ѕЙѕЙ.

So the same-point cubic term is always purely skew.
2. Consequence

This kills the hopeful branch that cubic same-point transport might restore the diagonal sector.

It cannot.

So at cubic order:

    Bsp(3)Bsp(3)вАЛ is skew,

    BN3(3)=X0N3X0BN3вАЛ(3)вАЛ=X0вАЛN3вАЛX0вАЛ is skew,

    BN1(3)BN1вАЛ(3)вАЛ is also skew-leaning,

    only
    BN2(3)=вИТX1N2X0+X0N2X1
    BN2вАЛ(3)вАЛ=вИТX1вАЛN2вАЛX0вАЛ+X0вАЛN2вАЛX1вАЛ

    can produce a non-skew contribution.

And we already found that BN2(3)BN2вАЛ(3)вАЛ is off-diagonal traceless:
BN2(3)=(0вИЧвИЧ0),with no diagonal part.
BN2вАЛ(3)вАЛ=(0вИЧвАЛвИЧ0вАЛ),with no diagonal part.вАЛ

So cubic order, even in the real corrected model, cannot generate diagonal directions from the value-family.
3. Big structural conclusion

Therefore the cubic value-family is confined to the off-diagonal sector:
V\val(3)вКВ{(0вИЧвИЧ0)}.
V\val(3)вАЛвКВ{(0вИЧвАЛвИЧ0вАЛ)}.вАЛ

More sharply:

    the same-point cubic source gives the skew off-diagonal line;

    the N2N2вАЛ-coupling can give the symmetric off-diagonal line;

    the other mixed cubic sources stay skew.

So generically
dimвБ°V\val(3)вЙ§2,
dimV\val(3)вАЛвЙ§2,вАЛ

and it sits entirely inside the off-diagonal subspace.

This is a major result.
4. What that means for the quotient

Since
J3вЙЕM2(C)
J3вАЛвЙЕM2вАЛ(C)

is 44-dimensional, and the cubic value-family is at most 22-dimensional off-diagonal, the cubic quotient automatically contains the full diagonal sector.

So:
J3/V\val(3) is nontrivial generically, and cubic order is the first real candidate leading quotient order.
J3вАЛ/V\val(3)вАЛ is nontrivial generically, and cubic order is the first real candidate leading quotient order.вАЛ

In fact, unless the actual one-pair cubic jet has zero diagonal part for independent reasons, the cubic quotient survives immediately.
5. The next exact move

Now the task becomes much simpler.

We no longer need to compute all cubic generators to decide whether cubic quotient exists. It does.

The next checkpoint is just:
Compute the diagonal part of the actual one-pair cubic jet J3\pair(h).
Compute the diagonal part of the actual one-pair cubic jet J3\pairвАЛ(h).вАЛ

Because the cubic value-family cannot touch the diagonal sector, any nonzero diagonal part of J3\pair(h)J3\pairвАЛ(h) gives a nonzero cubic quotient class at once.

That is now the sharp next step.

Continue

This is the first place the route becomes genuinely favorable.

Since we just showed the cubic value-family cannot touch the diagonal sector, the only thing we need now is:
show the actual one-pair cubic jet J3\pair(h) has nonzero diagonal part generically.
show the actual one-pair cubic jet J3\pairвАЛ(h) has nonzero diagonal part generically.вАЛ

If that holds, then cubic quotient survives immediately.

So letвАЩs attack exactly that.
1. Decompose the actual one-pair cubic jet

Recall
J3\pair(h)=вИТвАЙX3N0X0+X2N0X1вИТX1N0X2+X0N0X3
J3\pairвАЛ(h)=вИТX3вАЛN0вАЛX0вАЛ+X2вАЛN0вАЛX1вАЛвИТX1вАЛN0вАЛX2вАЛ+X0вАЛN0вАЛX3вАЛ
вИТвАЙX2K1X0+X1K1X1вИТX0K1X2вИТвАЙX1K2X0+X0K2X1+X0K3X0,
вИТX2вАЛK1вАЛX0вАЛ+X1вАЛK1вАЛX1вАЛвИТX0вАЛK1вАЛX2вАЛвИТX1вАЛK2вАЛX0вАЛ+X0вАЛK2вАЛX1вАЛ+X0вАЛK3вАЛX0вАЛ,

where KjKjвАЛ are the one-pair mixed-block jets.

From the previous step, the same-point cubic term
вИТвАЙX3N0X0+X2N0X1вИТX1N0X2+X0N0X3
вИТX3вАЛN0вАЛX0вАЛ+X2вАЛN0вАЛX1вАЛвИТX1вАЛN0вАЛX2вАЛ+X0вАЛN0вАЛX3вАЛ

is always purely skew. So it contributes no diagonal part.

Also:

    the K1K1вАЛ-coupled cubic term is skew-leaning and contributes no robust diagonal sector;

    the K3K3вАЛ term is skew if K3K3вАЛ is skew;

    the only term that can produce diagonal part generically is

вИТвАЙX1K2X0+X0K2X1.
вИТX1вАЛK2вАЛX0вАЛ+X0вАЛK2вАЛX1вАЛ.вАЛ

So the diagonal part of the actual cubic jet is controlled by the even mixed coefficient K2K2вАЛ.

That is the decisive simplification.
2. Compute the diagonal part of вИТX1K2X0+X0K2X1вИТX1вАЛK2вАЛX0вАЛ+X0вАЛK2вАЛX1вАЛ

Write
X0=(x100x2),X1=(uvvw),K2=(ABBD).
X0вАЛ=(x1вАЛ0вАЛ0x2вАЛвАЛ),X1вАЛ=(uvвАЛvwвАЛ),K2вАЛ=(ABвАЛBDвАЛ).

Then
вИТвАЙX1K2X0+X0K2X1
вИТX1вАЛK2вАЛX0вАЛ+X0вАЛK2вАЛX1вАЛ

has diagonal entries
(вИТX1K2X0+X0K2X1)11=vB(x1вИТx2),
(вИТX1вАЛK2вАЛX0вАЛ+X0вАЛK2вАЛX1вАЛ)11вАЛ=vB(x1вАЛвИТx2вАЛ),
(вИТX1K2X0+X0K2X1)22=vB(x2вИТx1).
(вИТX1вАЛK2вАЛX0вАЛ+X0вАЛK2вАЛX1вАЛ)22вАЛ=vB(x2вАЛвИТx1вАЛ).

So the diagonal part is
diagвБ°(вИТX1K2X0+X0K2X1)=vB(x1вИТx2)(100вИТ1).
diag(вИТX1вАЛK2вАЛX0вАЛ+X0вАЛK2вАЛX1вАЛ)=vB(x1вАЛвИТx2вАЛ)(10вАЛ0вИТ1вАЛ).вАЛ

That is the key formula.
3. Consequence

Therefore the actual one-pair cubic jet has nonzero diagonal part whenever these three things hold:
vвЙ†0,BвЙ†0,x1вЙ†x2.
vоА†=0,BоА†=0,x1вАЛоА†=x2вАЛ.вАЛ

Interpretation:

    vv is the off-diagonal entry of X1X1вАЛ, so it is nonzero when the first same-point perturbation has a genuine off-diagonal component;

    BB is the off-diagonal entry of the even mixed coefficient K2K2вАЛ;

    x1вЙ†x2x1вАЛоА†=x2вАЛ means the background same-point block is not scalar, i.e. the two local jet scales are genuinely different.

That third condition is generically true in the normalized setting.

So the cubic quotient survives as soon as the one-pair family has:

    a nontrivial off-diagonal first same-point jet,

    and a nontrivial off-diagonal even mixed jet.

That is very plausible.
4. Why this is strong

Because the cubic value-family cannot touch diagonal directions, any nonzero value of
vB(x1вИТx2)
vB(x1вАЛвИТx2вАЛ)

gives a nonzero cubic quotient class automatically.

So we no longer need to know the whole quotient basis at cubic order.

The diagonal part alone is already a quotient detector.

That is a major breakthrough in the local structure.
5. What remains to finish the cubic checkpoint

Now the only honest missing step is to verify that for the actual one-pair anomalous family:

    X1X1вАЛ has vвЙ°ћЄ0vоА†вЙ°0,

    K2K2вАЛ has off-diagonal BвЙ°ћЄ0BоА†вЙ°0,

on a generic hh-region.

This is much smaller than everything before.

In practice:

    vвЙ°ћЄ0vоА†вЙ°0 should come from the same-point first jet having a nonzero off-diagonal derivative component;

    BвЙ°ћЄ0BоА†вЙ°0 should come from the mixed even Taylor coefficient not being diagonal.

If both hold, then cubic quotient is nontrivial generically.
6. The next theorem to state

The right theorem is now:
Cubic diagonal survival lemma

For the normalized one-pair anomalous family, the even mixed coefficient K2K2вАЛ has generically nonzero off-diagonal entry B(h)B(h), and the first same-point inverse-square-root coefficient X1(h)X1вАЛ(h) has generically nonzero off-diagonal entry v(h)v(h). Hence
diagвБ°J3\pair(h)=v(h)B(h)(x1вИТx2)(100вИТ1)+(possibly other diagonal terms),
diagJ3\pairвАЛ(h)=v(h)B(h)(x1вАЛвИТx2вАЛ)(10вАЛ0вИТ1вАЛ)+(possibly other diagonal terms),

is not identically zero, so
[J3\pair(h)]вЙ†0inJ3/V\val(3).
[J3\pairвАЛ(h)]оА†=0inJ3вАЛ/V\val(3)вАЛ.

That would prove cubic order is the generic leading quotient order.
7. Honest status

I have now reduced the cubic checkpoint to one explicit scalar product:
v(h)вАЙB(h)вАЙ(x1вИТx2).
v(h)B(h)(x1вАЛвИТx2вАЛ).

I cannot honestly assert yet that B(h)вЙ°ћЄ0B(h)оА†вЙ°0 from the draft material already in hand, because we have not explicitly written the actual one-pair K2K2вАЛ formula in this thread.

But this is now a very small target.
8. The exact next move

The exact next move is:
compute the off-diagonal entry B(h) of the actual one-pair even mixed coefficient K2(h).
compute the off-diagonal entry B(h) of the actual one-pair even mixed coefficient K2вАЛ(h).вАЛ

If B(h)вЙ°ћЄ0B(h)оА†вЙ°0, then cubic quotient survives generically, and the whole residual-germ program can start at order 33.

Continue

Yes вАФ and now we can actually compute the off-diagonal entry B(h)B(h) of the actual one-pair even mixed coefficient K2(h)K2вАЛ(h).

This is the decisive cubic checkpoint.
1. Start from the explicit mixed block

Using the jet-limit mixed block formula already frozen in the draft,
N12(s)=(2sinвБ°ќФssinвБ°ќФвИТq+(s)вАЙscosвБ°ќФs2qвИТ(s)вАЙscosвБ°ќФвИТsinвБ°ќФs2(qвИТ(s)+q+(s))scosвБ°ќФ+(qвИТ(s)q+(s)s2вИТ2)sinвБ°ќФ2s3),

вАЛ,

where in symmetric placement
t¬±=m¬±s2,q¬±(s):=q(t¬±),ќФ(s)=ќ¶(tвИТ)вИТќ¶(t+).

The common 1/ѕА1/ѕА factor is irrelevant for the coefficient bookkeeping.

We expand:
q+(s)=q+qвА≤2s+qвА≤вА≤8s2+qвА≤вА≤вА≤48s3+O(s4),
q+вАЛ(s)=q+2qвА≤вАЛs+8qвА≤вА≤вАЛs2+48qвА≤вА≤вА≤вАЛs3+O(s4),
qвИТ(s)=qвИТqвА≤2s+qвА≤вА≤8s2вИТqвА≤вА≤вА≤48s3+O(s4),
qвИТвАЛ(s)=qвИТ2qвА≤вАЛs+8qвА≤вА≤вАЛs2вИТ48qвА≤вА≤вА≤вАЛs3+O(s4),

with all derivatives evaluated at mm.

Also
ќФ(s)=qвАЙs+ќФ3s3+O(s5),
ќФ(s)=qs+ќФ3вАЛs3+O(s5),

because ќФќФ is odd and ќФвА≤(0)=q(m)ќФвА≤(0)=q(m) in the symmetric setup. Then
sinвБ°ќФ=qвАЙs+(ќФ3вИТq36)s3+O(s5),
sinќФ=qs+(ќФ3вАЛвИТ6q3вАЛ)s3+O(s5),

2. Expand the off-diagonal entry

Take the (1,2)(1,2)-entry:
sinвБ°ќФвИТq+(s)вАЙscosвБ°ќФs2.
s2sinќФвИТq+вАЛ(s)scosќФвАЛ.

q+(s)вАЙscosвБ°ќФ=(q+qвА≤2s+qвА≤вА≤8s2+qвА≤вА≤вА≤48s3+O(s4))s(1вИТq22s2+O(s4)).
q+вАЛ(s)scosќФ=(q+2qвА≤вАЛs+8qвА≤вА≤вАЛs2+48qвА≤вА≤вА≤вАЛs3+O(s4))s(1вИТ2q2вАЛs2+O(s4)).

B(h)=q(h)2вАЙqвА≤(h)4вИТqвА≤вА≤вА≤(h)48.

That is the exact formula we needed.
3. Why this matters

Earlier we found that the diagonal part of the actual one-pair cubic jet is
diagвБ°J3\pair(h)=v(h)вАЙB(h)вАЙ(x1вИТx2)(100вИТ1),
diagJ3\pairвАЛ(h)=v(h)B(h)(x1вАЛвИТx2вАЛ)(10вАЛ0вИТ1вАЛ),

1. What has to be shown

2. The clean structure of the quintic coefficient

3. Why quintic is more promising than cubic for the residual

The second is especially attractive, because it gives an immediate residual sector.

look for a quintic symmetric off-diagonal quotient component.вАЛ

That would instantly prove vвАЊ1вЙ†0v1вАЛоА†=0.

The next step is to isolate the smallest quintic term that can force a symmetric off-diagonal residual.

1. The right quintic source to inspect

At quintic order, the cubic leading line is diagonal, so any nonzero symmetric off-diagonal term wins.

The cleanest candidate is the K4K4вАЛ-coupled term
J5,K4:=вИТX1K4X0+X0K4X1,
J5,K4вАЛвАЛ:=вИТX1вАЛK4вАЛX0вАЛ+X0вАЛK4вАЛX1вАЛ,

    K4K4вАЛ is even, hence typically symmetric;

    X1X1вАЛ is symmetric;

    this is the exact quintic analogue of the cubic K2K2вАЛ-mechanism that produced the diagonal survival.

2. General formula for the symmetric off-diagonal part

Write
X0=(x100x2),X1=(uvvw),K4=(ABBD).
X0вАЛ=(x1вАЛ0вАЛ0x2вАЛвАЛ),X1вАЛ=(uvвАЛvwвАЛ),K4вАЛ=(ABвАЛBDвАЛ).

J5,K4вАЛвАЛ=вИТX1вАЛK4вАЛX0вАЛ+X0вАЛK4вАЛX1вАЛ

has entries
(J5,K4)12=x1(Av+Bw)вИТx2(Bu+Dv),
(J5,K4вАЛвАЛ)12вАЛ=x1вАЛ(Av+Bw)вИТx2вАЛ(Bu+Dv),

(J5,K4вАЛвАЛ)21вАЛ=x2вАЛ(Bu+Dv)вИТx1вАЛ(Av+Bw).

So this term is always off-diagonal traceless:
(J5,K4)12+(J5,K4)21=0.
(J5,K4вАЛвАЛ)12вАЛ+(J5,K4вАЛвАЛ)21вАЛ=0.

That means this particular candidate does not give the symmetric off-diagonal residual we hoped for.

So the simplest K4K4вАЛ-analogue is a dead end for ѕГ5ѕГ5вАЛ.
3. The next best candidate

That means the symmetric off-diagonal residual must come from a different quintic source, not from a bare вИТX1K4X0+X0K4X1вИТX1вАЛK4вАЛX0вАЛ+X0вАЛK4вАЛX1вАЛ commutator form.

The next most promising terms are the ones with three symmetric factors, because those are the natural way to produce symmetric output rather than skew/traceless-commutator output.

At quintic order, the obvious such candidates are of the shape
X2K2X1,X1K2X2,
X2вАЛK2вАЛX1вАЛ,X1вАЛK2вАЛX2вАЛ,

appearing in the quintic expansion with opposite signs and additional transport terms.

More generally, the quintic coefficient will contain a block of the form
J5,K2high=вИТвАЙX3K2X0+X2K2X1вИТX1K2X2+X0K2X3.
J5,K2вАЛhighвАЛ=вИТX3вАЛK2вАЛX0вАЛ+X2вАЛK2вАЛX1вАЛвИТX1вАЛK2вАЛX2вАЛ+X0вАЛK2вАЛX3вАЛ.

This is the exact quintic analogue of the cubic same-point transport structure, but with K2K2вАЛ in place of N0N0вАЛ.

That is now the right object to inspect.
4. Symmetric off-diagonal part of the K2K2вАЛ-transport block

K2=(ABBD),
K2вАЛ=(ABвАЛBDвАЛ),

and symmetric
X1=(uvvw),X2=(prrq),X3=(ќ±ќ≤ќ≤ќ≥).

Then the two middle terms
X2K2X1вИТX1K2X2
X2вАЛK2вАЛX1вАЛвИТX1вАЛK2вАЛX2вАЛ

again have zero diagonal, but they are not forced to be skew unless extra symmetry collapses them. However, their symmetric off-diagonal part still cancels:
(X2K2X1вИТX1K2X2)12+(X2K2X1вИТX1K2X2)21=0.
(X2вАЛK2вАЛX1вАЛвИТX1вАЛK2вАЛX2вАЛ)12вАЛ+(X2вАЛK2вАЛX1вАЛвИТX1вАЛK2вАЛX2вАЛ)21вАЛ=0.

Likewise, the transport pair
вИТвАЙX3K2X0+X0K2X3
вИТX3вАЛK2вАЛX0вАЛ+X0вАЛK2вАЛX3вАЛ

is also off-diagonal traceless:
(вИТX3K2X0+X0K2X3)12+(вИТX3K2X0+X0K2X3)21=0.
(вИТX3вАЛK2вАЛX0вАЛ+X0вАЛK2вАЛX3вАЛ)12вАЛ+(вИТX3вАЛK2вАЛX0вАЛ+X0вАЛK2вАЛX3вАЛ)21вАЛ=0.

So the whole K2K2вАЛ-transport block is again off-diagonal traceless.

That means it still does not contribute to ѕГ5ѕГ5вАЛ.
5. Big consequence

There is a pattern:

terms of the form
вИТвАЙYZX0+X0ZYorY2ZY1вИТY1ZY2
вИТYZX0вАЛ+X0вАЛZYorY2вАЛZY1вАЛвИТY1вАЛZY2вАЛ

with symmetric X0,Y,Y1,Y2,ZX0вАЛ,Y,Y1вАЛ,Y2вАЛ,Z
always land in the off-diagonal traceless sector.

So all the commutator-style transport terms are invisible to ѕГ5ѕГ5вАЛ.

That means the quintic symmetric off-diagonal residual cannot come from transport/commutator structure alone.
6. Therefore the only genuine source is the direct odd mixed coefficient K5K5вАЛ

So the next step collapses sharply:
If ѕГ5(h) survives, it must come from the direct quintic mixed coefficient K5(h), not from transport terms.
If ѕГ5вАЛ(h) survives, it must come from the direct quintic mixed coefficient K5вАЛ(h), not from transport terms.вАЛ

Because:

    K1K1вАЛ- and K3K3вАЛ-coupled transport terms are skew or traceless off-diagonal;

    K2K2вАЛ- and K4K4вАЛ-coupled transport terms are traceless off-diagonal;

    only the direct insertion
    X0K5X0
    X0вАЛK5вАЛX0вАЛ

    can contribute an unconstrained symmetric off-diagonal part.

And for diagonal X0X0вАЛ,
(X0K5X0)12+(X0K5X0)21=2x1x2вАЙ(K5)12\sym.
(X0вАЛK5вАЛX0вАЛ)12вАЛ+(X0вАЛK5вАЛX0вАЛ)21вАЛ=2x1вАЛx2вАЛ(K5вАЛ)12\symвАЛ.

So the quintic residual problem is now:
compute the symmetric off-diagonal part of K5(h).
compute the symmetric off-diagonal part of K5вАЛ(h).вАЛ

That is the exact next checkpoint.
7. What this means strategically

This is good news, actually. It means the quintic residual test is much cleaner than it looked:

    we do not need all of J5J5вАЛ;

    we do not need the whole quintic quotient basis;

    we only need one scalar from the direct odd mixed Taylor coefficient K5K5вАЛ.

If that scalar is generically nonzero, then
vвАЊ1(h)вЙ†0
v1вАЛ(h)оА†=0

and the residual-germ theorem is proved at first step.
8. The next exact move

So the next exact move is now very small and precise:
Expand the actual one-pair mixed block to order s5 and extract the symmetric off-diagonal coefficient (K5)12\sym(h).
Expand the actual one-pair mixed block to order s5 and extract the symmetric off-diagonal coefficient (K5вАЛ)12\symвАЛ(h).вАЛ

If it is not identically zero, the quintic residual-sector lemma is done.

Continue

IвАЩm expanding the mixed block one more order to isolate the direct K5K5вАЛ contribution and see whether its symmetric off-diagonal part can survive generically.

Continue

There is a decisive correction.

The direct K5K5вАЛ route to a symmetric off-diagonal residual is impossible.

In fact, for the actual mixed block, the symmetric off-diagonal part is an even function of ss. So every odd mixed coefficient has zero symmetric off-diagonal part.

That means:
(K2j+1)12+(K2j+1)21=0for all jвЙ•0.
(K2j+1вАЛ)12вАЛ+(K2j+1вАЛ)21вАЛ=0for all jвЙ•0.вАЛ

So K5K5вАЛ cannot prove the quintic residual by itself.
1. Exact identity for the symmetric off-diagonal mixed part

Start from the mixed block entries
N12(s)12=sinвБ°ќФвИТq+(s)вАЙscosвБ°ќФs2,N12(s)21=qвИТ(s)вАЙscosвБ°ќФвИТsinвБ°ќФs2.
N12вАЛ(s)12вАЛ=s2sinќФвИТq+вАЛ(s)scosќФвАЛ,N12вАЛ(s)21вАЛ=s2qвИТвАЛ(s)scosќФвИТsinќФвАЛ.

Add them:
(N12)12+(N12)21=(qвИТ(s)вИТq+(s))вАЙscosвБ°ќФs2=qвИТ(s)вИТq+(s)scosвБ°ќФ(s).
(N12вАЛ)12вАЛ+(N12вАЛ)21вАЛ=s2(qвИТвАЛ(s)вИТq+вАЛ(s))scosќФвАЛ=sqвИТвАЛ(s)вИТq+вАЛ(s)вАЛcosќФ(s).

So the symmetric off-diagonal mixed part is exactly
ѕГmix(s):=(N12)12+(N12)21=qвИТ(s)вИТq+(s)scosвБ°ќФ(s).
ѕГmixвАЛ(s):=(N12вАЛ)12вАЛ+(N12вАЛ)21вАЛ=sqвИТвАЛ(s)вИТq+вАЛ(s)вАЛcosќФ(s).вАЛ

Now:

    qвИТ(s)вИТq+(s)qвИТвАЛ(s)вИТq+вАЛ(s) is odd in ss,

    dividing by ss makes it even,

    cosвБ°ќФ(s)cosќФ(s) is even.

Hence ѕГmix(s)ѕГmixвАЛ(s) is even.

Therefore its Taylor series has only even powers:
ѕГmix(s)=c0+c2s2+c4s4+вЛѓвАЙ.
ѕГmixвАЛ(s)=c0вАЛ+c2вАЛs2+c4вАЛs4+вЛѓ.

So every odd-order coefficient vanishes:
(K1)12\sym=0,(K3)12\sym=0,(K5)12\sym=0, вА¶
(K1вАЛ)12\symвАЛ=0,(K3вАЛ)12\symвАЛ=0,(K5вАЛ)12\symвАЛ=0, вА¶вАЛ

This kills the hoped-for вАЬdirect K5K5вАЛ symmetric off-diagonalвАЭ residual.
2. This parity statement is actually stronger

The mixed block has a clean parity decomposition:

    diagonal entries are even in ss,

    symmetric off-diagonal part is even in ss,

    skew off-diagonal part is odd in ss.

So:

    K2jK2jвАЛ are symmetric-type,

    K2j+1K2j+1вАЛ are skew-type.

That means the direct odd mixed coefficients K1,K3,K5,вА¶K1вАЛ,K3вАЛ,K5вАЛ,вА¶ can only contribute skew channels.

So the quintic residual cannot come from a direct odd mixed insertion if we want a new symmetric direction.
3. Consequence for the quintic residual search

This means the previous candidate
X0K5X0
X0вАЛK5вАЛX0вАЛ

is useless for producing ѕГ5ѕГ5вАЛ. It stays skew.

So the quintic residual must come from a different mechanism.

There are now only two plausible places left:
A. a new diagonal quintic component not proportional to the cubic leading diagonal line;
B. a symmetric off-diagonal piece created indirectly through transport/coupling with an even mixed coefficient.

But we already checked the simplest commutator-style transport terms
вИТвАЙX1K4X0+X0K4X1,вИТвАЙX3K2X0+X0K2X3,X2K2X1вИТX1K2X2,
вИТX1вАЛK4вАЛX0вАЛ+X0вАЛK4вАЛX1вАЛ,вИТX3вАЛK2вАЛX0вАЛ+X0вАЛK2вАЛX3вАЛ,X2вАЛK2вАЛX1вАЛвИТX1вАЛK2вАЛX2вАЛ,

and they are all off-diagonal traceless, so their symmetric off-diagonal sum also vanishes.

So route B looks much weaker now.

That leaves route A as the real next candidate.
4. What the new quintic target should be

The cubic leading line came from a diagonal traceless direction:
v0(h)вИЉ(100вИТ1).
v0вАЛ(h)вИЉ(10вАЛ0вИТ1вАЛ).

So the cleanest way for the quintic coefficient v1(h)v1вАЛ(h) to escape that line is to produce a trace component:
trвБ°J5\pair(h)вЙ°ћЄ0.
trJ5\pairвАЛ(h)оА†вЙ°0.

Because a trace component is automatically not proportional to the cubic diagonal-traceless leading line.

So the next real target becomes:
compute the quintic trace ѕД5(h):=trвБ°J5\pair(h).
compute the quintic trace ѕД5вАЛ(h):=trJ5\pairвАЛ(h).вАЛ

If ѕД5(h)вЙ°ћЄ0ѕД5вАЛ(h)оА†вЙ°0, then
vвАЊ1(h)вЙ†0
v1вАЛ(h)оА†=0

immediately.

That is now the best residual checkpoint.
5. Why trace is more plausible now

The parity structure does not forbid even mixed coefficients K2,K4K2вАЛ,K4вАЛ from feeding trace at quintic order through higher transport couplings.

And unlike symmetric off-diagonal, trace is not killed by the commutator-type cancellations we just used.

So trace is now the natural next residual sector to inspect.
6. The next exact step

So the correct continuation is no longer вАЬcompute symmetric off-diagonal part of K5K5вАЛ.вАЭ

It is:
Compute the quintic diagonal part, especially the trace ѕД5(h)=trвБ°J5\pair(h), and test whether it is not identically zero.
Compute the quintic diagonal part, especially the trace ѕД5вАЛ(h)=trJ5\pairвАЛ(h), and test whether it is not identically zero.вАЛ

That is the new sharp checkpoint for the residual-germ program.

Continue

There is another correction, but it is productive.

The quintic trace is not controlled by K4K4вАЛ or K5K5вАЛ. Those easy candidates either vanish or collapse by symmetry. The first genuinely plausible trace source is the odd mixed coefficient K1K1вАЛ reappearing at quintic order through higher transport.

That is actually a better residual mechanism.
1. Where quintic trace can come from

At quintic order, the coefficient has the schematic decomposition
J5\pair=J5,sp+J5,K1+J5,K2+J5,K3+J5,K4+J5,K5.
J5\pairвАЛ=J5,spвАЛ+J5,K1вАЛвАЛ+J5,K2вАЛвАЛ+J5,K3вАЛвАЛ+J5,K4вАЛвАЛ+J5,K5вАЛвАЛ.

Now:

    the same-point block J5,spJ5,spвАЛ is built from symmetric XjXjвАЛ and symmetric N0N0вАЛ, and its trace is a sum of commutator traces, hence vanishes;

    the direct K5K5вАЛ insertion has trace zero because K5K5вАЛ is skew;

    the K4K4вАЛ-commutator block has trace zero because it is a symmetricвАУsymmetric commutator against a symmetric matrix;

    the K2K2вАЛ-transport block also has trace zero for the same commutator reason.

So the first terms that can genuinely contribute trace are the odd mixed transport blocks
J5,K1andJ5,K3.
J5,K1вАЛвАЛandJ5,K3вАЛвАЛ.

That is the new sharp target.
2. The K1K1вАЛ-block explicitly

The quintic block built from K1K1вАЛ is
J5,K1=вИТвАЙX4K1X0+X3K1X1вИТвАЙX2K1X2+X1K1X3вИТвАЙX0K1X4.
J5,K1вАЛвАЛ=вИТX4вАЛK1вАЛX0вАЛ+X3вАЛK1вАЛX1вАЛвИТX2вАЛK1вАЛX2вАЛ+X1вАЛK1вАЛX3вАЛвИТX0вАЛK1вАЛX4вАЛ.

Take trace. The middle term drops:
trвБ°(X2K1X2)=trвБ°(K1X22)=0
tr(X2вАЛK1вАЛX2вАЛ)=tr(K1вАЛX22вАЛ)=0

because X22X22вАЛ is symmetric and K1K1вАЛ is skew.

So
trвБ°J5,K1=trвБ°вАЙвБ£((X0X4вИТX4X0)K1)+trвБ°вАЙвБ£((X1X3вИТX3X1)K1).
trJ5,K1вАЛвАЛ=tr((X0вАЛX4вАЛвИТX4вАЛX0вАЛ)K1вАЛ)+tr((X1вАЛX3вАЛвИТX3вАЛX1вАЛ)K1вАЛ).

Thus
trвБ°J5,K1=trвБ°вАЙвБ£([X0,X4]K1)+trвБ°вАЙвБ£([X1,X3]K1).
trJ5,K1вАЛвАЛ=tr([X0вАЛ,X4вАЛ]K1вАЛ)+tr([X1вАЛ,X3вАЛ]K1вАЛ).вАЛ

This is the exact structural formula.
3. In the diagonal-baseline frame

Write
X0=(x100x2),K1=ќЇвАЙJ,J=(01вИТ10),

[X1,X3]=(ќ≤(uвИТw)+v(ќ≥вИТќ±))вАЙJ,

trвБ°J5,K1=вИТ2ќЇ((x1вИТx2)r+ќ≤(uвИТw)+v(ќ≥вИТќ±)).

This is a real formula, not just a heuristic.
4. What it means

This is very good news.

The quintic trace is generically nonzero as soon as:
ќЇвЙ†0,(x1вИТx2)r+ќ≤(uвИТw)+v(ќ≥вИТќ±)вЙ†0.
ќЇоА†=0,(x1вАЛвИТx2вАЛ)r+ќ≤(uвИТw)+v(ќ≥вИТќ±)оА†=0.

Interpretation:

    ќЇќЇ is the scalar amplitude of the cubic leading skew mixed coefficient K1K1вАЛ, which we already know is generically present;

    rr is the off-diagonal part of X4X4вАЛ;

    vv is the off-diagonal part of X1X1вАЛ;

    ќ≤ќ≤ is the off-diagonal part of X3X3вАЛ;

    uвИТwuвИТw and ќ≥вИТќ±ќ≥вИТќ± are the diagonal-traceless parts of X1,X3X1вАЛ,X3вАЛ.

There is no structural reason for that combination to vanish identically.

So this is a very plausible residual escape mechanism.
5. Why this is enough for the residual theorem

The cubic leading line came from the diagonal-traceless cubic quotient direction. A nonzero trace at quintic order is automatically outside that line.

So if
trвБ°J5\pair(h)вЙ°ћЄ0,
trJ5\pairвАЛ(h)оА†вЙ°0,

then
vвАЊ1(h)вЙ†0in Q/вДУh.
v1вАЛ(h)оА†=0in Q/вДУhвАЛ.

And that proves the residual germ is nonzero at first step.

So the first residual-germ checkpoint is now reduced to:
show trвБ°J5,K1(h)вЙ°ћЄ0 (or failing that, include K3 similarly).
show trJ5,K1вАЛвАЛ(h)оА†вЙ°0 (or failing that, include K3вАЛ similarly).вАЛ
6. The next exact theorem to target

I would now state:
Quintic trace residual lemma

For the normalized one-pair family, the quintic trace satisfies
trвБ°J5\pair(h)=вИТ2ќЇ(h)((x1вИТx2)r(h)+ќ≤(h)(u(h)вИТw(h))+v(h)(ќ≥(h)вИТќ±(h)))+(possible K3-terms),

and is not identically zero generically. Hence the quintic quotient coefficient is not collinear with the cubic leading line:
vвАЊ1(h)вЙ†0.
v1вАЛ(h)оА†=0.

That would prove the residual-germ theorem for the one-pair family at first step.
7. Honest status

This is the first really convincing residual mechanism we have found.

I still have not proved that the bracketed combination is nonzero in the actual one-pair model, because we have not written explicit formulas for X3,X4X3вАЛ,X4вАЛ here.

But unlike before, the target is now a very concrete scalar.

So the next exact move is:
compute r(h), ќ≤(h), ќ≥(h)вИТќ±(h) from the one-pair same-point recursion, and test whether (x1вИТx2)r+ќ≤(uвИТw)+v(ќ≥вИТќ±)вЙ°ћЄ0.
compute r(h), ќ≤(h), ќ≥(h)вИТќ±(h) from the one-pair same-point recursion, and test whether (x1вАЛвИТx2вАЛ)r+ќ≤(uвИТw)+v(ќ≥вИТќ±)оА†вЙ°0.вАЛ

That is the sharpest continuation now.

Continue

LetвАЩs continue by reducing that bracketed scalar to something we can actually read off from the same-point recursion.

We want to understand
ќШ(h):=(x1вИТx2)r(h)+ќ≤(h)(u(h)вИТw(h))+v(h)(ќ≥(h)вИТќ±(h)),
ќШ(h):=(x1вАЛвИТx2вАЛ)r(h)+ќ≤(h)(u(h)вИТw(h))+v(h)(ќ≥(h)вИТќ±(h)),

because
trвБ°J5,K1(h)=вИТ2ќЇ(h)вАЙќШ(h)+(possible K3-terms).

The good news is that every symbol in ќШ(h)ќШ(h) comes from the expansion of X(s)=G(s)вИТ1/2X(s)=G(s)вИТ1/2, so there is a clean recursive way to express them in terms of the same-point jet coefficients of GG.
1. Recursion for X1,X2,X3,X4X1вАЛ,X2вАЛ,X3вАЛ,X4вАЛ

Write
G(s)=G0+sG1+s2G2+s3G3+s4G4+вЛѓвАЙ,
G(s)=G0вАЛ+sG1вАЛ+s2G2вАЛ+s3G3вАЛ+s4G4вАЛ+вЛѓ,

and

X(s)=G(s)вИТ1/2=X0вАЛ+sX1вАЛ+s2X2вАЛ+s3X3вАЛ+s4X4вАЛ+вЛѓ.

These satisfy
X(s)G(s)X(s)=I.
X(s)G(s)X(s)=I.

Equating coefficients gives Sylvester equations
S(Y):=G01/2Y+YG01/2=known RHS,
S(Y):=G01/2вАЛY+YG01/2вАЛ=known RHS,

so every XjXjвАЛ is obtained by applying L=SвИТ1L=SвИТ1 to an explicit polynomial in the earlier XiXiвАЛ and GiGiвАЛ.

In particular:
X1=L(вИТX0G1X0),
