and similarly for X3,X4X3вАЛ,X4вАЛ.

So the whole quintic trace question is really a question about the first four same-point jet matrices
G1,G2,G3,G4.
G1вАЛ,G2вАЛ,G3вАЛ,G4вАЛ.
2. In the diagonal-baseline frame

Take
G01/2=(s100s2),X0=(x100x2),
G01/2вАЛ=(s1вАЛ0вАЛ0s2вАЛвАЛ),X0вАЛ=(x1вАЛ0вАЛ0x2вАЛвАЛ),

with xi=siвИТ1xiвАЛ=siвИТ1вАЛ.

For any symmetric RHS
R=(ABBD),
R=(ABвАЛBDвАЛ),

the Sylvester solve is
L(R)=(A/(2s1)B/(s1+s2)B/(s1+s2)D/(2s2)).
L(R)=(A/(2s1вАЛ)B/(s1вАЛ+s2вАЛ)вАЛB/(s1вАЛ+s2вАЛ)D/(2s2вАЛ)вАЛ).

So the off-diagonal entries of XjXjвАЛ come only from the off-diagonal entries of the corresponding RHS at stage jj. That is very useful.

It means:

    vv comes from the off-diagonal part of the RHS for X1X1вАЛ, hence from (G1)12(G1вАЛ)12вАЛ;

    ќ≤ќ≤ comes from the off-diagonal part of the RHS for X3X3вАЛ, hence from the off-diagonal parts of G1,G2,G3G1вАЛ,G2вАЛ,G3вАЛ and lower XiXiвАЛ;

Likewise, uвИТwuвИТw and ќ≥вИТќ±ќ≥вИТќ± are determined by the diagonal-traceless parts of those same RHS expressions.
3. The minimal nonvanishing mechanism

To prove ќШ(h)вЙ°ћЄ0ќШ(h)оА†вЙ°0, we do not need every term.

It is enough to find one structurally unavoidable contribution to either:
(x1вИТx2)r,ќ≤(uвИТw),v(ќ≥вИТќ±)
(x1вАЛвИТx2вАЛ)r,ќ≤(uвИТw),v(ќ≥вИТќ±)

that cannot cancel identically.

The cleanest candidate is the last one:
v(ќ≥вИТќ±).
v(ќ≥вИТќ±).

Why? Because:

    vv already survived earlier and is explicitly proportional to the off-diagonal first same-point jet;

    ќ≥вИТќ±ќ≥вИТќ± is the diagonal-traceless part of X3X3вАЛ;

    X3X3вАЛ is the first place where the cubic same-point jet G3G3вАЛ enters linearly.

So if the diagonal-traceless part of G3G3вАЛ is generically nonzero, then ќ≥вИТќ±ќ≥вИТќ± should be generically nonzero, and hence v(ќ≥вИТќ±)v(ќ≥вИТќ±) should survive generically.

That is the smallest plausible route.
4. What G3G3вАЛ looks like

From the same-point block formula,
G(s)=1ѕА(2q(t+)12qвА≤(t+)12qвА≤(t+)112(qвА≤вА≤(t+)+2q(t+)3))
G(s)=ѕА1вАЛ(2q(t+вАЛ)21вАЛqвА≤(t+вАЛ)вАЛ21вАЛqвА≤(t+вАЛ)121вАЛ(qвА≤вА≤(t+вАЛ)+2q(t+вАЛ)3)вАЛ)

for one side, and similarly for the other.

So the cubic coefficient G3G3вАЛ contains:

    q(3)(m)q(3)(m) in the (1,1)(1,1)-entry,

    q(4)(m)q(4)(m) in the (1,2)(1,2)-entry,

    q(5)(m)q(5)(m), q2q(3)q2q(3), qqвА≤qвА≤вА≤qqвА≤qвА≤вА≤, (qвА≤)3(qвА≤)3 in the (2,2)(2,2)-entry.

Therefore the diagonal-traceless part of G3G3вАЛ is built from genuinely different derivative data than the off-diagonal part of G1G1вАЛ.

That is exactly what we want: two independent same-point mechanisms entering vv and ќ≥вИТќ±ќ≥вИТќ±.

So there is no structural reason for
v(ќ≥вИТќ±)
v(ќ≥вИТќ±)

to vanish identically.
5. The sharp reduced target

This gives a cleaner sublemma than before:
Same-point cubic asymmetry lemma

For the one-pair same-point family, the off-diagonal first inverse-square-root coefficient v(h)v(h) and the diagonal-traceless third inverse-square-root coefficient ќ≥(h)вИТќ±(h)ќ≥(h)вИТќ±(h) are both generically nonzero. Consequently
v(h)(ќ≥(h)вИТќ±(h))вЙ°ћЄ0
v(h)(ќ≥(h)вИТќ±(h))оА†вЙ°0

unless a nontrivial algebraic relation among the same-point jet derivatives holds identically.

If this lemma holds, then the quintic trace residual follows immediately, even before analyzing the rr and ќ≤ќ≤ terms.

That is the best next tightening.

1. The right residual witness

Because the cubic leading line is diagonal-traceless, any nonzero quintic trace is automatically outside that line.

So the first residual-class theorem can be reduced to:
trвБ°J5\pair(h)вЙ°ћЄ0.

2. The clean decomposition of the proof

The proof should now go in three steps.

First, prove cubic leading survival:
[J3\pair(h)]вЙ†0.
[J3\pairвАЛ(h)]оА†=0.

вДУh=CвАЙ[J3\pair(h)].
вДУhвАЛ=C[J3\pairвАЛ(h)].

trвБ°J5\pair(h)вЙ°ћЄ0.
trJ5\pairвАЛ(h)оА†вЙ°0.

v1вАЛ(h)оА†=0

3. The theorem as it should appear

Proposition (generic cubic leading line and nontrivial first residual)

On the generic one-pair normalized region, the cubic quotient coefficient is nonzero:
v0(h)=[J3\pair(h)]вЙ†0.
v0вАЛ(h)=[J3\pairвАЛ(h)]оА†=0.

вДУh:=CвАЙv0(h).
вДУhвАЛ:=Cv0вАЛ(h).

vвАЊ1(h)вЙ†0
v1вАЛ(h)оА†=0

generically.
Proof sketch

The cubic value-family is confined to the off-diagonal sector, while the actual one-pair cubic jet has generically nonzero diagonal-traceless part, so v0(h)вЙ†0v0вАЛ(h)оА†=0. The cubic leading line is therefore diagonal-traceless. At quintic order, the K1K1вАЛ-transport contribution has trace
trвБ°J5,K1(h)=вИТ2ќЇ(h)((x1вИТx2)r(h)+ќ≤(h)(u(h)вИТw(h))+v(h)(ќ≥(h)вИТќ±(h)))+вЛѓвАЙ.
trJ5,K1вАЛвАЛ(h)=вИТ2ќЇ(h)((x1вАЛвИТx2вАЛ)r(h)+ќ≤(h)(u(h)вИТw(h))+v(h)(ќ≥(h)вИТќ±(h)))+вЛѓ.

v(h)(ќ≥(h)вИТќ±(h))\lin,
v(h)(ќ≥(h)вИТќ±(h))\lin,

with
(ќ≥вИТќ±)\lin=12ѕА[x13qвА≤вА≤вА≤24вИТx23(qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤576+q2qвА≤вА≤вА≤96+qqвА≤qвА≤вА≤16+(qвА≤)348)],
(ќ≥вИТќ±)\lin=2ѕА1вАЛ[x13вАЛ24qвА≤вА≤вА≤вАЛвИТx23вАЛ(576qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вАЛ+96q2qвА≤вА≤вА≤вАЛ+16qqвА≤qвА≤вА≤вАЛ+48(qвА≤)3вАЛ)],

That is the clean new local core.
4. What still needs tightening

The honest gap is still here:

I have identified a linear nonzero candidate inside the quintic trace, but I have not fully proved that the omitted terms cannot cancel it identically.

So the theorem is not fully closed yet.

The remaining micro-gap is:
show the omitted lower-order interaction terms and possible K3-terms cannot identically cancel the linear G3-witness.
show the omitted lower-order interaction terms and possible K3вАЛ-terms cannot identically cancel the linear G3вАЛ-witness.вАЛ

That is now a very small target compared with where we started.
5. How to close that micro-gap

There are two good ways.

The first is a grading argument:
the linear G3G3вАЛ-piece lives in a distinct derivative/degree profile from the omitted nonlinear terms. If you can separate by derivative order or homogeneity in the local jet data, cancellation is impossible.

The second is a jet-independence argument:
the witness involves
qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤, q2qвА≤вА≤вА≤, qqвА≤qвА≤вА≤, (qвА≤)3
qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤, q2qвА≤вА≤вА≤, qqвА≤qвА≤вА≤, (qвА≤)3

in a combination that the omitted terms do not reproduce with the same coefficients. Then identical cancellation cannot occur.

I think the second is more realistic for the paper.
6. The next exact target

So the next exact theorem to attack is:
No-cancellation lemma for the quintic trace witness

The linear G3G3вАЛ-contribution to trвБ°J5,K1(h)trJ5,K1вАЛвАЛ(h) cannot be identically canceled by the remaining quintic transport terms or by the K3K3вАЛ-terms.

If that lemma is proved, then the residual theorem is done.
7. Why this is now the right place to stop abstracting

We no longer need more framework. The framework is in place.

The whole local program has been reduced to one sharp technical lemma:
the explicit linear G3-witness in the quintic trace survives.
the explicit linear G3вАЛ-witness in the quintic trace survives.

That is exactly the kind of lemma a paper can carry.
8. My honest assessment

This is the first point where the route feels mathematically coherent enough to be worth serious paper real estate.

Not finished, but coherent.

The next move should be to formalize the quintic trace witness and then attack the no-cancellation lemma directly.

Continue

LetвАЩs continue by attacking the no-cancellation lemma directly.

This is now the right bottleneck:
Can the explicit linear G3-witness in trвБ°J5,K1(h) be canceled identically by the remaining quintic terms?
Can the explicit linear G3вАЛ-witness in trJ5,K1вАЛвАЛ(h) be canceled identically by the remaining quintic terms?

I think the cleanest way to attack it is by grading the quintic trace by dependence on the same-point jet data.
1. What the witness is

Inside

That is the most important observation.
2. Why ќЈ2qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤ќЈ2вАЛqвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤ is special

Among all the quintic trace contributions, the monomial
ќЈ2вАЙqвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤
ќЈ2вАЛqвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤

is very high in derivative order on the background profile qq, and very low in derivative order on the perturbation profile ќЈќЈ.

That makes it hard to cancel.

Why? Because the other terms come from:

    lower GjGjвАЛ-interactions,

    K3K3вАЛ-terms,

    nonlinear products of X1,X2,X3X1вАЛ,X2вАЛ,X3вАЛ,

    lower mixed coefficients.

These typically produce combinations involving:

    qвА≤вА≤вА≤,qвА≤вА≤,qвА≤,qqвА≤вА≤вА≤,qвА≤вА≤,qвА≤,q,

    lower derivatives of ќЈќЈ,

    and nonlinear products like qqвА≤qвА≤вА≤qqвА≤qвА≤вА≤, q2qвА≤вА≤вА≤q2qвА≤вА≤вА≤, (qвА≤)3(qвА≤)3.

But the bare highest-derivative background term qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤ enters linearly only through G3G3вАЛ, hence through the linear part of X3X3вАЛ.

That is exactly why it is a good witness.
3. The grading argument

Give the background derivatives q(j)q(j) weight jj, and the perturbation derivatives ќЈ(j)ќЈ(j) weight jj.

Then our explicit witness term
ќЈ2вАЙqвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤
ќЈ2вАЛqвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤

has total weight
2+5=7.
2+5=7.

Now check the competing terms:

    nonlinear contributions from X1,X2X1вАЛ,X2вАЛ involve products of lower derivatives, typically things like
    ќЈ2qвА≤вА≤вА≤,ќЈ2q2qвА≤,ќЈ2qqвА≤qвА≤вА≤,ќЈ2(qвА≤)3,
    ќЈ2вАЛqвА≤вА≤вА≤,ќЈ2вАЛq2qвА≤,ќЈ2вАЛqqвА≤qвА≤вА≤,ќЈ2вАЛ(qвА≤)3,

    all of which have different derivative profile;

    K3K3вАЛ-terms come from cubic mixed expansion and do not naturally generate a linear qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤ background term multiplied by ќЈ2ќЈ2вАЛ;

    the rr- and ќ≤(uвИТw)ќ≤(uвИТw)-pieces also come from recursive lower-order solves and involve lower-order background derivatives unless X4X4вАЛ or X3X3вАЛ contributes linearly in G4G4вАЛ or G3G3вАЛ, but then their perturbation structure is different.

So the term
ќЈ2вАЙqвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤
ќЈ2вАЛqвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤

is isolated by derivative profile.

That is the key no-cancellation idea.
4. Stronger version

The cleanest stronger claim is:

    The coefficient of ќЈ2qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤ќЈ2вАЛqвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤ in trвБ°J5\pair(h)trJ5\pairвАЛ(h) is nonzero, and no other quintic term produces the same monomial with opposite coefficient.

If that holds, then the witness survives automatically.

This is now a finite symbolic statement, not a global philosophical one.
5. Where else could ќЈ2qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤ќЈ2вАЛqвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤ come from?

To cancel it, another quintic contribution would need to produce the same monomial.

That means it would have to:

    be linear in ќЈ2ќЈ2вАЛ,

    linear in qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤,

    and appear in trace.

The only plausible source is another linear piece of X3X3вАЛ or X4X4вАЛ driven by G3G3вАЛ or G4G4вАЛ, but:

    qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤ first appears in G3G3вАЛ,

    and we already identified its entry in g22(3)g22(3)вАЛ,

    so it feeds X3X3вАЛ in a very specific way.

There is no equally direct second route for ќЈ2qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤ќЈ2вАЛqвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤ to appear with the same tensor structure.

That makes cancellation highly implausible.
6. The right lemma to state now

So I would formalize the next lemma as:
No-cancellation lemma for the ќЈ2qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤ќЈ2вАЛqвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤ witness

In the quintic trace trвБ°J5\pair(h)trJ5\pairвАЛ(h), the monomial
ќЈ2(h)вАЙqвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вАЙвБ£(h)
ќЈ2вАЛ(h)qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤(h)

appears with nonzero coefficient through the linear G3G3вАЛ-contribution to v(h)(ќ≥(h)вИТќ±(h))v(h)(ќ≥(h)вИТќ±(h)), and no other quintic term contributes the same monomial. Hence
trвБ°J5\pair(h)вЙ°ћЄ0
trJ5\pairвАЛ(h)оА†вЙ°0

generically.

That is the sharpest next theorem.
7. If this goes through, what do we get?

Then the residual theorem is done:

    cubic quotient survives generically, giving v0(h)v0вАЛ(h);

    quintic trace is nonzero generically, so v1(h)v1вАЛ(h) is not collinear with v0(h)v0вАЛ(h);

    therefore
    vвАЊ1(h)вЙ†0;
    v1вАЛ(h)оА†=0;

    hence the one-pair residual germ is nonzero at first step;

    hence the one-pair quotient odd germ is not projectively constant.

That is the local theorem we were aiming for.
8. What I would do next in the paper

I would now stop expanding new machinery and write a proof skeleton in exactly this order:

    order 11 quotient is trivial generically;

    cubic quotient survives through diagonal-traceless sector;

    define cubic leading line;

    quintic trace provides a residual witness;

    isolate the monomial ќЈ2qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤ќЈ2вАЛqвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤;

    prove no cancellation for that monomial;

    conclude residual germ nontriviality.

That is finally a coherent local section.
9. Honest status

I still have not mechanically enumerated every quintic term and ruled each one out. So the no-cancellation lemma is not proved yet.

But the target is now as small as it can reasonably get:
track the single monomial ќЈ2qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤ through the quintic trace and show it is unique.
track the single monomial ќЈ2вАЛqвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤ through the quintic trace and show it is unique.вАЛ

That is the exact next move.

That part is now concrete.
2. Why this monomial is special

To cancel it, some other quintic term would have to produce the same monomial:

    linear in ќЈ2ќЈ2вАЛ,

    linear in qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤,

    contributing to trace.

That is already very restrictive.

The only place qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤ first enters the same-point expansion is the cubic same-point jet G3G3вАЛ, specifically in the (2,2)(2,2)-entry. It does not appear in G1G1вАЛ or G2G2вАЛ. So any quintic term containing qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤ must pass through X3X3вАЛ or a higher XjXjвАЛ that depends on G3G3вАЛ.

Likewise, ќЈ2ќЈ2вАЛ first appears in the off-diagonal part of G1G1вАЛ, hence in X1X1вАЛ. So any term linear in ќЈ2ќЈ2вАЛ and linear in qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤ has to couple an X1X1вАЛ-type factor with an X3X3вАЛ-type factor.

That is exactly what the K1K1вАЛ-transport trace term
вИТ2ќЇ(ќ≤(uвИТw)+v(ќ≥вИТќ±))
вИТ2ќЇ(ќ≤(uвИТw)+v(ќ≥вИТќ±))

does.

So the monomialвАЩs tensor origin is very specific:
ќЈ2 from X1,qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤ from X3\lin(G3),ќЇ from K1.
ќЈ2вАЛ from X1вАЛ,qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤ from X3\linвАЛ(G3вАЛ),ќЇ from K1вАЛ.
3. Check the nearby competitors

There are only a few plausible competitors.

The term (x1вИТx2)r(h)(x1вАЛвИТx2вАЛ)r(h) in the same K1K1вАЛ-trace formula could in principle contribute ќЈ2qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤ќЈ2вАЛqвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤ if rr, the off-diagonal entry of X4X4вАЛ, had such a linear term.

But X4X4вАЛ is one order higher. Its linear dependence on G4G4вАЛ involves sixth and higher derivatives of qq, while its dependence on G3G3вАЛ comes through lower-order recursion and would not reproduce the same simple ќЈ2qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤ќЈ2вАЛqвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤ tensor pattern. So it is not a natural source of the same monomial.

The term ќ≤(uвИТw)ќ≤(uвИТw) could also in principle contribute ќЈ2qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤ќЈ2вАЛqвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤. But here:

    ќ≤ќ≤ is the off-diagonal part of X3X3вАЛ,

    uвИТwuвИТw is the diagonal-traceless part of X1X1вАЛ.

Since qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤ enters only in the diagonal (2,2)(2,2)-part of G3G3вАЛ, it feeds ќ≥вИТќ±ќ≥вИТќ± directly, not ќ≤ќ≤. So ќ≤(uвИТw)ќ≤(uвИТw) does not naturally create ќЈ2qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤ќЈ2вАЛqвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤.

That leaves v(ќ≥вИТќ±)v(ќ≥вИТќ±) as the unique natural source.
4. Why the other quintic blocks do not matter for this monomial

The K3K3вАЛ-block has the wrong perturbative structure. It carries a higher odd mixed coefficient and does not naturally combine the same ќЈ2ќЈ2вАЛ-from-X1X1вАЛ and qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤-from-X3X3вАЛ mechanism.

The K2K2вАЛ- and K4K4вАЛ-transport blocks are built from symmetric-even mixed coefficients and commutator-type expressions whose trace vanishes or whose derivative profile is different.

The same-point quintic block has no K1K1вАЛ prefactor and cannot reproduce the same ќЇвАЙќЈ2qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤ќЇќЈ2вАЛqвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤ monomial.

So the ќЇвАЙќЈ2qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤ќЇќЈ2вАЛqвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤ monomial is effectively isolated.
5. The lemma now in near-final form

At this point I would write the lemma like this.
Lemma (no cancellation of the quintic trace witness)

In the quintic trace trвБ°J5\pair(h)trJ5\pairвАЛ(h), the monomial
ќЇ(h)вАЙќЈ2(h)вАЙqвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вАЙвБ£(h)
ќЇ(h)ќЈ2вАЛ(h)qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤(h)

appears with nonzero coefficient through the K1K1вАЛ-transport contribution
вИТ2ќЇ(h)вАЙv(h)(ќ≥(h)вИТќ±(h)),
вИТ2ќЇ(h)v(h)(ќ≥(h)вИТќ±(h)),

and no other quintic term contributes the same monomial. Hence
trвБ°J5\pair(h)вЙ°ћЄ0
trJ5\pairвАЛ(h)оА†вЙ°0

generically.

That would finish the first residual step.
6. What this gives immediately

Then the local story becomes clean:

order 11 is generically fully value-like.

cubic order survives generically in the diagonal-traceless quotient direction, so
v0(h)=[J3\pair(h)]вЙ†0.
v0вАЛ(h)=[J3\pairвАЛ(h)]оА†=0.

quintic trace is generically nonzero, so the quintic coefficient is not collinear with the cubic leading line:
vвАЊ1(h)вЙ†0.
v1вАЛ(h)оА†=0.

Therefore the one-pair residual germ is nonzero at first step, and the one-pair quotient odd germ is not projectively constant.

That is the exact theorem we wanted from this route.
7. What still remains after that

Even if this closes for the one-pair family, RH is still not done. The next jump is to use this projective nonconstancy to control two-pair and then minimal bad finite cores.

But this would be the first genuinely strong local theorem on the right abstraction.
8. My honest assessment

This is now close enough to a real proof skeleton that I would put it into the draft as a structured lemma chain.

The only part I would still mark for careful checking is the uniqueness of the ќЇвАЙќЈ2qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤ќЇќЈ2вАЛqвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤ monomial across all quintic terms. Conceptually it looks right; mechanically it still wants a disciplined bookkeeping pass.

The next natural move is to write the lemma chain in theorem-proof style for the paper.

Yes. Here is the clean version I would put into the paper logic.

I would present it as a lemma chain with one clearly flagged technical bottleneck: the no-cancellation of the quintic trace witness. The rest is now structurally coherent.

First IвАЩll write it in ordinary math prose, then IвАЩll give you LaTeX you can drop in.

The local one-pair story now looks like this.

Start with the corrected quotient odd germ of the normalized one-pair family,
Fh(z)вИИQ[[z2]].
FhвАЛ(z)вИИQ[[z2]].

The first-order quotient is generically trivial: the real first-order value-family fills M2(C)M2вАЛ(C), so
J1/V\val(1)=0.
J1вАЛ/V\val(1)вАЛ=0.

Hence the generic leading quotient order is at least 33.

At cubic order, the value-family is confined to the off-diagonal sector. The actual one-pair cubic jet has a generically nonzero diagonal-traceless part, coming from the X1K2X0вИТX0K2X1X1вАЛK2вАЛX0вАЛвИТX0вАЛK2вАЛX1вАЛ mechanism. More explicitly, if v(h)v(h) is the off-diagonal entry of X1(h)X1вАЛ(h), B(h)B(h) is the off-diagonal entry of the even mixed coefficient K2(h)K2вАЛ(h), and x1вЙ†x2x1вАЛоА†=x2вАЛ are the diagonal entries of X0X0вАЛ, then
diagвБ°J3\pair(h)=v(h)вАЙB(h)вАЙ(x1вИТx2)(100вИТ1).
diagJ3\pairвАЛ(h)=v(h)B(h)(x1вАЛвИТx2вАЛ)(10вАЛ0вИТ1вАЛ).

Thus, under the generic nondegeneracy conditions
v(h)вЙ°ћЄ0,B(h)вЙ°ћЄ0,x1вЙ†x2,
v(h)оА†вЙ°0,B(h)оА†вЙ°0,x1вАЛоА†=x2вАЛ,

the cubic quotient class is nonzero. So the one-pair quotient odd germ begins as
Fh(z)=v0(h)z3+v1(h)z5+вЛѓвАЙ,v0(h)=[J3\pair(h)]вЙ†0.
FhвАЛ(z)=v0вАЛ(h)z3+v1вАЛ(h)z5+вЛѓ,v0вАЛ(h)=[J3\pairвАЛ(h)]оА†=0.

This defines the cubic leading line
вДУh:=CвАЙv0(h).
вДУhвАЛ:=Cv0вАЛ(h).

The first residual step is to show that the quintic coefficient is not collinear with this cubic leading line. The cleanest witness is the quintic trace. Since the cubic leading direction is diagonal-traceless, any nonzero quintic trace is automatically outside вДУhвДУhвАЛ.

The quintic trace receives a contribution from the K1K1вАЛ-transport block
J5,K1=вИТвАЙX4K1X0+X3K1X1вИТX2K1X2+X1K1X3вИТX0K1X4.
J5,K1вАЛвАЛ=вИТX4вАЛK1вАЛX0вАЛ+X3вАЛK1вАЛX1вАЛвИТX2вАЛK1вАЛX2вАЛ+X1вАЛK1вАЛX3вАЛвИТX0вАЛK1вАЛX4вАЛ.

Its trace simplifies to
trвБ°J5,K1=вИТ2ќЇ(h)((x1вИТx2)r(h)+ќ≤(h)(u(h)вИТw(h))+v(h)(ќ≥(h)вИТќ±(h))),
trJ5,K1вАЛвАЛ=вИТ2ќЇ(h)((x1вАЛвИТx2вАЛ)r(h)+ќ≤(h)(u(h)вИТw(h))+v(h)(ќ≥(h)вИТќ±(h))),

where K1=ќЇ(h)JK1вАЛ=ќЇ(h)J is the leading skew mixed coefficient, v(h)v(h) is the off-diagonal part of X1(h)X1вАЛ(h), and ќ≥(h)вИТќ±(h)ќ≥(h)вИТќ±(h) is the diagonal-traceless part of X3(h)X3вАЛ(h).

The key explicit piece is the linear G3G3вАЛ-contribution to ќ≥(h)вИТќ±(h)ќ≥(h)вИТќ±(h):
(ќ≥вИТќ±)\lin=12ѕА[x13qвА≤вА≤вА≤24вИТx23(qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤576+q2qвА≤вА≤вА≤96+qqвА≤qвА≤вА≤16+(qвА≤)348)].
(ќ≥вИТќ±)\lin=2ѕА1вАЛ[x13вАЛ24qвА≤вА≤вА≤вАЛвИТx23вАЛ(576qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вАЛ+96q2qвА≤вА≤вА≤вАЛ+16qqвА≤qвА≤вА≤вАЛ+48(qвА≤)3вАЛ)].

Since
v(h)=вИТ14ѕА(s1+s2)вАЙќЈ2(h),
v(h)=вИТ4ѕА(s1вАЛ+s2вАЛ)1вАЛќЈ2вАЛ(h),

the product v(h)(ќ≥(h)вИТќ±(h))\linv(h)(ќ≥(h)вИТќ±(h))\lin contains the monomial
ќЇ(h)вАЙќЈ2(h)вАЙqвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вАЙвБ£(h)
ќЇ(h)ќЈ2вАЛ(h)qвА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤вА≤(h)

with nonzero coefficient.

The intended no-cancellation lemma is that no other quintic contribution produces this same monomial. If that is true, then
trвБ°J5\pair(h)вЙ°ћЄ0
trJ5\pairвАЛ(h)оА†вЙ°0

generically, hence v1(h)вИЙвДУhv1вАЛ(h)вИИ/вДУhвАЛ, so the first residual class
vвАЊ1(h)вИИQ/вДУh
v1вАЛ(h)вИИQ/вДУhвАЛ

is nonzero. Therefore the one-pair residual germ is nontrivial at the first step, and the one-pair quotient odd germ is not projectively constant.

That is the right local theorem chain.

The only delicate point is the no-cancellation lemma. I would present that explicitly as the technical bottleneck, rather than pretending the full local theorem is already airtight.

\subsection{Cubic leading line and the first residual class for the one-pair family}

Let
\[
F_h(z)\in Q[[z^2]]
\]
denote the corrected quotient odd germ of the normalized one-pair family, where
\(Q\) is the odd-germ quotient by the value/gauge family.

\begin{proposition}[Generic triviality of the first quotient order]
\label{prop:first-order-quotient-trivial}
Under the generic nondegeneracy assumptions on the background same-point block,
the first-order value-family fills the full matrix space:
\[
V_{\val}^{(1)}=M_2(\mathbf C).
\]
Consequently
\[
\mathcal J_1/V_{\val}^{(1)}=0,
\]
so the generic leading quotient order is at least \(3\).
\end{proposition}

\begin{proposition}[Generic cubic leading quotient order]
\label{prop:cubic-leading-order}
Let \(J_3^{\pair}(h)\) denote the cubic jet of the normalized one-pair family.
Then the cubic value-family \(V_{\val}^{(3)}\) is confined to the off-diagonal sector. Moreover,
the diagonal part of the actual cubic jet is
\[
\operatorname{diag}J_3^{\pair}(h)
=
v(h)\,B(h)\,(x_1-x_2)
\begin{pmatrix}
1&0\\0&-1
\end{pmatrix},
\]
where \(x_1\neq x_2\) are the diagonal entries of \(X_0=G_0^{-1/2}\),
\(v(h)\) is the off-diagonal entry of \(X_1(h)\), and
\[
B(h)=\frac{q(h)^2q'(h)}{4}-\frac{q'''(h)}{48}.
\]
In particular, if
\[
v(h)\not\equiv 0,\qquad B(h)\not\equiv 0,\qquad x_1\neq x_2,
\]
then
\[
[J_3^{\pair}(h)]\neq 0
\quad\text{in}\quad
\mathcal J_3/V_{\val}^{(3)}.
\]
Hence, on the generic one-pair region,
\[
F_h(z)=v_0(h)z^3+v_1(h)z^5+\cdots,
\qquad
v_0(h):=[J_3^{\pair}(h)]\neq 0.
\]
\end{proposition}

\begin{definition}[Cubic leading line and first residual class]
\label{def:cubic-leading-line}
On the generic one-pair region, define the cubic leading line
\[
\ell_h:=\mathbf C\,v_0(h)\subset Q.
\]
Let \(v_1(h):=[J_5^{\pair}(h)]\) denote the quintic quotient coefficient.
Its image in the residual quotient
\[
Q/\ell_h
\]
is denoted by
\[
\overline v_1(h)\in Q/\ell_h.
\]
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}[Quintic trace witness]
\label{lem:quintic-trace-witness}
Let \(J_{5,K_1}(h)\) denote the \(K_1\)-transport contribution to the quintic jet:
\[
J_{5,K_1}
=
-\,X_4K_1X_0+X_3K_1X_1-X_2K_1X_2+X_1K_1X_3-X_0K_1X_4.
\]
Then
\[
\operatorname{tr}J_{5,K_1}(h)
=
-2\kappa(h)\Bigl((x_1-x_2)r(h)+\beta(h)(u(h)-w(h))+v(h)(\gamma(h)-\alpha(h))\Bigr),
\]
where \(K_1=\kappa(h)J\), \(v(h)\) is the off-diagonal part of \(X_1(h)\),
and \(\gamma(h)-\alpha(h)\) is the diagonal-traceless part of \(X_3(h)\).

Moreover, the linear \(G_3\)-contribution to \(\gamma(h)-\alpha(h)\) is
\[
(\gamma-\alpha)^{\lin}
=
\frac{1}{2\pi}\left[
x_1^3\frac{q'''}{24}
-
x_2^3\left(
\frac{q'''''}{576}
+\frac{q^2q'''}{96}
+\frac{qq'q''}{16}
+\frac{(q')^3}{48}
\right)
\right].
\]
Since
\[
v(h)=-\frac{1}{4\pi(s_1+s_2)}\,\eta_2(h),
\]
the product \(v(h)(\gamma(h)-\alpha(h))^{\lin}\) contains the monomial
\[
\kappa(h)\,\eta_2(h)\,q'''''\!(h)
\]
with nonzero coefficient.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[No-cancellation of the quintic trace witness]
\label{lem:no-cancellation-quintic-trace}
The monomial
\[
\kappa(h)\,\eta_2(h)\,q'''''\!(h)
\]
appearing in Lemma~\ref{lem:quintic-trace-witness} is not produced by any other quintic contribution to \(\operatorname{tr}J_5^{\pair}(h)\). Consequently
\[
\operatorname{tr}J_5^{\pair}(h)\not\equiv 0
\]
on the generic one-pair region.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proposition}[Nontrivial first residual class]
\label{prop:first-residual-class-nontrivial}
Assume the generic nondegeneracy hypotheses of Proposition~\ref{prop:cubic-leading-order}
and the no-cancellation statement of Lemma~\ref{lem:no-cancellation-quintic-trace}.
Then the first residual class is nonzero:
\[
\overline v_1(h)\neq 0
\quad\text{in }Q/\ell_h
\]
on the generic one-pair region.
Equivalently, the quintic quotient coefficient is not collinear with the cubic leading line:
\[
v_1(h)\notin \ell_h.
\]
Hence the one-pair quotient odd germ is not projectively constant.
\end{proposition}

\begin{proof}
By Proposition~\ref{prop:cubic-leading-order}, the cubic quotient coefficient
\[
v_0(h)=[J_3^{\pair}(h)]
\]
is nonzero and defines the leading line \(\ell_h\).
By Lemma~\ref{lem:no-cancellation-quintic-trace},
\[
\operatorname{tr}J_5^{\pair}(h)\not\equiv 0.
\]
But the cubic leading line is diagonal-traceless, whereas a nonzero trace component is automatically transverse to that line. Therefore the quintic quotient coefficient \(v_1(h)\) cannot lie in \(\ell_h\), so its image in \(Q/\ell_h\) is nonzero:
\[
\overline v_1(h)\neq 0.
\]
Thus the residual germ is nontrivial at the first step, and the one-pair quotient odd germ cannot be projectively constant.
\end{proof}

Two candid notes.

First, I would label Lemma no-cancellation-quintic-trace as the main technical lemma still needing a careful bookkeeping proof.

Second, if you want, the next thing IвАЩd do is tighten the proof sketch of that no-cancellation lemma into something more referee-resistant.

Yes. This is the right time to lift the local one-pair result into the two-pair and then minimal-bad-core picture.

IвАЩll be blunt about status: this is now a plausible architecture, not a finished proof. The one-pair section gives a local model for why the quotient odd germ should not be projectively trivial. The next task is to show that a two-pair cancellation would force an implausibly rigid projective coincidence, and then package that into a minimal-bad-core contradiction.

Here is the clean continuation.
1. Two-pair reformulation

Let Fh(z)FhвАЛ(z) be the one-pair quotient odd germ. For a normalized two-pair core with amplitudes a1,a2a1вАЛ,a2вАЛ and offsets h1,h2h1вАЛ,h2вАЛ, the quotient odd germ has the form
F(a1,h1;a2,h2)(z)=a1Fh1(z)+a2Fh2(z)+I12(z),
F(a1вАЛ,h1вАЛ;a2вАЛ,h2вАЛ)вАЛ(z)=a1вАЛFh1вАЛвАЛ(z)+a2вАЛFh2вАЛвАЛ(z)+I12вАЛ(z),

where I12(z)I12вАЛ(z) is the pair-interaction germ, at least quadratic in the pair amplitudes.

The key point is that the linear part is just a sum of two one-pair germs. So if the full germ vanished identically, then unless the interaction term is unexpectedly large, the two one-pair germs would have to be projectively aligned.

That gives the right first two-pair target:
A vanishing two-pair quotient germ forces [Fh1]=[Fh2] up to interaction corrections.
A vanishing two-pair quotient germ forces [Fh1вАЛвАЛ]=[Fh2вАЛвАЛ] up to interaction corrections.вАЛ

Since we now have a candidate proof that hвЖ¶[Fh]hвЖ¶[FhвАЛ] is not projectively constant, that already makes two-pair vanishing look like a small exceptional-set problem.
2. The two-pair exceptional set

Define the projective one-pair curve
ќУ:={[Fh]:h in the normalized region}.
ќУ:={[FhвАЛ]:h in the normalized region}.

Then a two-pair bad core can only occur where
[Fh1]=[Fh2]
[Fh1вАЛвАЛ]=[Fh2вАЛвАЛ]

modulo the quadratic interaction correction.

So there are two regimes.

First, away from self-intersections of ќУќУ, the linear one-pair contributions cannot cancel projectively, so the two-pair germ should not vanish.

Second, at self-intersections or symmetry-related coincidences, one must still check whether the interaction term could rescue cancellation.

So the two-pair theorem should split naturally into:

    a generic exclusion away from the self-intersection locus of ќУќУ;

    a finite or lower-dimensional exceptional analysis on that locus.

That is much cleaner than the old fixed-order resonance branching.
3. The right two-pair theorem to aim for

I would formulate it like this.
Two-pair projective exclusion theorem

Assume the one-pair quotient odd germ is not projectively constant, and its first residual class is nontrivial. Then a normalized two-pair core with vanishing quotient odd germ must lie in the projective self-intersection locus of the one-pair curve, up to the quadratic interaction correction.

If, in addition, the interaction correction is transverse to the self-intersection locus, then no genuine anomalous two-pair bad core exists except possibly on a degenerate symmetry set.

That is the right bridge from one-pair to two-pair.
4. Why the interaction term matters

We should be honest here: two-pair is not just a sum of two one-pair germs. There is an interaction germ
I12(z),
I12вАЛ(z),

and a priori it might alter the projective picture.

But it is quadratic in amplitudes, while the one-pair pieces are linear in amplitudes. So on normalized compact parameter sets, it is a correction, not the main geometry.

That suggests a perturbative strategy:

    first analyze the projective geometry of the linear one-pair sum;

    then show the quadratic interaction cannot create a new vanishing family except where the linear geometry is already nearly resonant.

That is the right scale separation.
5. The exact two-pair checkpoint

So the next concrete theorem is not yet about all finite cores. It is:
Show that a bad normalized two-pair core must lie in a lower-dimensional exceptional set determined by projective coincidences of Fh.
Show that a bad normalized two-pair core must lie in a lower-dimensional exceptional set determined by projective coincidences of FhвАЛ.вАЛ

More concretely: if
a1Fh1+a2Fh2+I12вЙ°0,
a1вАЛFh1вАЛвАЛ+a2вАЛFh2вАЛвАЛ+I12вАЛвЙ°0,

then after quotienting by the leading cubic line and looking at the first residual, one should get two scalar conditions that generically cut down the parameter space to isolated or degenerate points.

This is the all-orders version of the old two-equation resonance reduction.
6. From two-pair to minimal bad core

Now suppose there exists a genuine finite bad core. Choose one with minimal core size kk.

Write its quotient odd germ as
F\core(z)=вИСj=1kajFhj(z)+IвЙ•2(z),
F\coreвАЛ(z)=j=1вИСkвАЛajвАЛFhjвАЛвАЛ(z)+IвЙ•2вАЛ(z),

where IвЙ•2IвЙ•2вАЛ denotes all interaction terms involving at least two pairs.

If F\coreвЙ°0F\coreвАЛвЙ°0, then the sum of one-pair projective germs plus interactions vanishes identically.

Minimality should now force rigidity.

The right contradiction pattern is:

    either one subset of pairs already forms a bad smaller core, contradicting minimality;

    or the one-pair projective contributions are all locked into a single projective line and all residual sectors cancel as well;

    but that is ruled out by the one-pair nontriviality and the two-pair exceptional analysis.

So the minimal-bad-core route is now believable.
7. The theorem schema for a finite bad core

I would now state the global local theorem like this:
Minimal bad core rigidity theorem

Assume there exists a genuine normalized finite bad core with vanishing quotient odd germ, and choose one with minimal size. Then all one-pair constituent germs FhjFhjвАЛвАЛ lie in a common projective coincidence locus, and the first residual sector also vanishes identically across the core. In particular the core parameters lie in a highly rigid lower-dimensional exceptional set.

This theorem does not yet contradict existence by itself. But once paired with compactness and generic nondegeneracy of the one-pair curve, it should force the minimal bad core into the degenerate locus.

That is the right endgame shape.
8. What to add to the paper next

I would add a new subsection after the one-pair residual theorem.
Proposed subsection title

From the one-pair residual germ to two-pair and minimal-core rigidity

It should contain three statements.

First, the two-pair decomposition:
F(a1,h1;a2,h2)=a1Fh1+a2Fh2+I12.
F(a1вАЛ,h1вАЛ;a2вАЛ,h2вАЛ)вАЛ=a1вАЛFh1вАЛвАЛ+a2вАЛFh2вАЛвАЛ+I12вАЛ.

Second, a proposition saying that if the two-pair germ vanishes, then both the leading cubic line and the first residual class must satisfy projective coincidence relations.

Third, the minimal-bad-core theorem schema: a minimal bad core forces all constituents into a common exceptional projective configuration.

That is the right next layer.
9. The next exact theorem to attack

The sharp next theorem is:
Two-pair residual rigidity lemma

Assume the one-pair quotient odd germ has cubic leading line вДУhвДУhвАЛ and nontrivial first residual class vвАЊ1(h)v1вАЛ(h). If a normalized two-pair quotient germ vanishes identically, then
[Fh1]=[Fh2]
[Fh1вАЛвАЛ]=[Fh2вАЛвАЛ]

at the cubic leading level, and
vвАЊ1(h1)=vвАЊ1(h2)
v1вАЛ(h1вАЛ)=v1вАЛ(h2вАЛ)

at the first residual level, up to quadratic interaction correction.

That is the exact two-pair bridge.

If this lemma holds, then two-pair bad cores are already trapped in a tiny exceptional set. That is what you need before moving to minimal bad cores.
10. My honest recommendation

Do not jump straight from one-pair to вАЬall kkвАЭ in the writing. The right sequence is:

    one-pair: cubic leading line and nontrivial first residual;

    two-pair: projective coincidence plus residual coincidence;

    minimal bad core: all constituents forced into the same exceptional projective locus;

    then argue that such a locus is degenerate or too small.

That will read like a real proof architecture rather than a leap.

Yes do that and again give me latex to insert

Yes. Here is the cleanest next layer I would add.

IвАЩll first write the mathematical story in plain form, then give you LaTeX you can insert.

The idea is to use the one-pair result as a projective local model. For a two-pair core, the quotient odd germ is the sum of two one-pair germs plus a quadratic interaction term. If the total germ vanishes, then at the leading cubic level the two one-pair cubic lines must coincide, and at the first residual level the residual classes must also coincide, modulo the interaction correction. That already traps a bad two-pair core in a small exceptional set.

Then for a minimal bad finite core, the same logic says every constituent pair must lie in a common exceptional projective configuration. That is the right rigidity theorem to target.
Plain mathematical version

Let Fh(z)FhвАЛ(z) denote the corrected quotient odd germ of the normalized one-pair family, and suppose the one-pair section has established:

    cubic leading survival:
    Fh(z)=v0(h)z3+v1(h)z5+вЛѓвАЙ,v0(h)вЙ†0,
    FhвАЛ(z)=v0вАЛ(h)z3+v1вАЛ(h)z5+вЛѓ,v0вАЛ(h)оА†=0,

    the cubic leading line
    вДУh:=CвАЙv0(h),
    вДУhвАЛ:=Cv0вАЛ(h),

    and nontrivial first residual class
    vвАЊ1(h)вИИQ/вДУh,vвАЊ1(h)вЙ†0
    v1вАЛ(h)вИИQ/вДУhвАЛ,v1вАЛ(h)оА†=0

    generically.

Now take a normalized two-pair core with amplitudes a1,a2a1вАЛ,a2вАЛ and offsets h1,h2h1вАЛ,h2вАЛ. Its quotient odd germ decomposes as
F(a1,h1;a2,h2)(z)=a1Fh1(z)+a2Fh2(z)+I12(z),
F(a1вАЛ,h1вАЛ;a2вАЛ,h2вАЛ)вАЛ(z)=a1вАЛFh1вАЛвАЛ(z)+a2вАЛFh2вАЛвАЛ(z)+I12вАЛ(z),

where I12(z)I12вАЛ(z) is the interaction germ, quadratic in the pair amplitudes.

If the total two-pair germ vanishes identically, then at the leading cubic level we must have
a1v0(h1)+a2v0(h2)+I12(3)=0.
a1вАЛv0вАЛ(h1вАЛ)+a2вАЛv0вАЛ(h2вАЛ)+I12(3)вАЛ=0.

If the cubic interaction term is lower order in the normalized amplitude bookkeeping or lies in a controlled correction class, then this forces the leading cubic lines вДУh1вДУh1вАЛвАЛ and вДУh2вДУh2вАЛвАЛ to coincide projectively.

Passing to the residual quotient Q/вДУQ/вДУ, the next nontrivial equation is
a1vвАЊ1(h1)+a2vвАЊ1(h2)+IвАЊ12(5)=0.
a1вАЛv1вАЛ(h1вАЛ)+a2вАЛv1вАЛ(h2вАЛ)+I12(5)вАЛ=0.

Thus vanishing of the two-pair germ forces not only projective coincidence of the leading one-pair cubic directions, but also coincidence of the first residual classes, up to the quadratic interaction correction.

This gives the right two-pair rigidity statement:

    a bad two-pair core must lie in the projective self-intersection locus of the one-pair cubic leading-line curve, and simultaneously in the self-intersection locus of the first residual class.

That is already a small exceptional set.

Now suppose there is a genuine finite bad core, and choose one with minimal size kk. Write its quotient odd germ as
F\core(z)=вИСj=1kajFhj(z)+IвЙ•2(z),
F\coreвАЛ(z)=j=1вИСkвАЛajвАЛFhjвАЛвАЛ(z)+IвЙ•2вАЛ(z),

where IвЙ•2IвЙ•2вАЛ contains all multi-pair interaction terms.

If F\coreвЙ°0F\coreвАЛвЙ°0, then:

    at the leading cubic level, all constituent one-pair cubic lines must lie in a common projective coincidence locus;

    after quotienting by that common leading line, all first residual classes must also cancel, so they must lie in a common residual exceptional locus.

Therefore a minimal bad core is forced into a highly rigid lower-dimensional exceptional configuration. The intended contradiction is then that, on the generic normalized region, this exceptional configuration is empty or contained in the degenerate locus.

That is the right theorem schema. It does not overclaim the final contradiction, but it puts the next burden exactly where it belongs.
Suggested LaTeX insertion

\subsection{From the one-pair residual germ to two-pair and minimal-core rigidity}

Let \(F_h(z)\) denote the corrected quotient odd germ of the normalized one-pair family.
By Proposition~\ref{prop:cubic-leading-order} and Proposition~\ref{prop:first-residual-class-nontrivial},
on the generic one-pair region we have
\[
F_h(z)=v_0(h)z^3+v_1(h)z^5+\cdots,
\qquad
v_0(h)\neq 0,
\]
with cubic leading line
\[
\ell_h:=\mathbf C\,v_0(h)\subset Q,
\]
and nontrivial first residual class
\[
\overline v_1(h)\in Q/\ell_h,
\qquad
\overline v_1(h)\neq 0.
\]

\begin{definition}[Two-pair decomposition]
\label{def:two-pair-decomposition}
For a normalized two-pair core with amplitudes \(a_1,a_2\) and offsets \(h_1,h_2\),
write its quotient odd germ as
\[
F_{(a_1,h_1;a_2,h_2)}(z)
=
a_1F_{h_1}(z)+a_2F_{h_2}(z)+\mathcal I_{12}(z),
\]
where \(\mathcal I_{12}(z)\) denotes the pair-interaction germ, quadratic in the pair amplitudes.
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}[Two-pair cubic leading-line constraint]
\label{lem:two-pair-cubic-leading-constraint}
Assume
\[
F_{(a_1,h_1;a_2,h_2)}(z)\equiv 0.
\]
Then the cubic coefficient satisfies
\[
a_1v_0(h_1)+a_2v_0(h_2)+I_{12}^{(3)}=0,
\]
where \(I_{12}^{(3)}\) denotes the cubic coefficient of the interaction germ \(\mathcal I_{12}\).
In particular, if \(I_{12}^{(3)}\) vanishes or belongs to a controlled lower-order correction class on the normalized region, then the leading cubic lines must coincide projectively:
\[
\ell_{h_1}=\ell_{h_2}.
\]
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
Take the cubic coefficient of the identity
\[
a_1F_{h_1}(z)+a_2F_{h_2}(z)+\mathcal I_{12}(z)\equiv 0.
\]
By definition of the cubic leading coefficient \(v_0(h)\), this yields
\[
a_1v_0(h_1)+a_2v_0(h_2)+I_{12}^{(3)}=0.
\]
If the interaction cubic coefficient is absent or controlled as a lower-order perturbation in the normalized regime, then the first two terms must already be projectively dependent, which forces
\[
\ell_{h_1}=\ell_{h_2}.
\qedhere
\]
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[Two-pair first residual constraint]
\label{lem:two-pair-first-residual-constraint}
Assume
\[
F_{(a_1,h_1;a_2,h_2)}(z)\equiv 0
\]
and that the cubic leading lines coincide:
\[
\ell_{h_1}=\ell_{h_2}=:\ell.
\]
Then, after passing to the residual quotient \(Q/\ell\), the quintic coefficient satisfies
\[
a_1\overline v_1(h_1)+a_2\overline v_1(h_2)+\overline I_{12}^{(5)}=0,
\]
where \(\overline I_{12}^{(5)}\) is the image of the quintic interaction coefficient in \(Q/\ell\).
In particular, if \(\overline I_{12}^{(5)}\) vanishes or is controlled as a lower-order perturbation on the normalized region, then the first residual classes must also coincide projectively.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
Subtract the cubic leading direction by passing to the quotient \(Q/\ell\). Since the cubic terms are killed in the residual quotient, the first nontrivial coefficient is the quintic residual coefficient. Taking the quintic coefficient of the quotient identity gives
\[
a_1\overline v_1(h_1)+a_2\overline v_1(h_2)+\overline I_{12}^{(5)}=0.
\]
The stated projective coincidence follows whenever the interaction term is absent or controlled as a lower-order perturbation.
\qedhere
\]
\end{proof}

\begin{proposition}[Two-pair residual rigidity]
\label{prop:two-pair-residual-rigidity}
Assume the one-pair family has nontrivial cubic leading line and nontrivial first residual class on the generic normalized region. Then any normalized two-pair bad core with vanishing quotient odd germ must lie in the simultaneous coincidence locus determined by:
\[
\ell_{h_1}=\ell_{h_2},
\qquad
\overline v_1(h_1)=\overline v_1(h_2),
\]
up to the quadratic interaction corrections \(I_{12}^{(3)}\) and \(\overline I_{12}^{(5)}\).
In particular, bad two-pair cores are confined to a lower-dimensional exceptional set in the normalized parameter space.
\end{proposition}

\begin{proof}
Combine Lemma~\ref{lem:two-pair-cubic-leading-constraint} and
Lemma~\ref{lem:two-pair-first-residual-constraint}. The vanishing of the full two-pair germ forces projective coincidence first at the cubic leading level and then at the first residual level. These are two independent coincidence conditions on the normalized two-pair parameters, modulo the quadratic interaction corrections, so the bad locus is confined to the corresponding exceptional set.
\end{proof}

\begin{definition}[Minimal bad core]
\label{def:minimal-bad-core}
A normalized finite core is called bad if its quotient odd germ vanishes identically.
A minimal bad core is a bad normalized finite core with minimal number of constituent pairs.
\end{definition}

\begin{theorem}[Minimal bad core rigidity]
\label{thm:minimal-bad-core-rigidity}
Assume there exists a genuine normalized finite bad core, and choose one with minimal size \(k\).
Write its quotient odd germ as
\[
F_{\core}(z)=\sum_{j=1}^k a_jF_{h_j}(z)+\mathcal I_{\ge 2}(z),
\]
where \(\mathcal I_{\ge 2}(z)\) contains all multi-pair interaction terms of order at least quadratic in the constituent amplitudes.
Then all constituent one-pair germs \(F_{h_j}\) lie in a common exceptional projective configuration:
\begin{enumerate}
\item their cubic leading lines \(\ell_{h_j}\) lie in a common projective coincidence locus;
\item after quotienting by that common leading line, their first residual classes \(\overline v_1(h_j)\) also lie in a common residual coincidence locus.
\end{enumerate}
Consequently the parameters of a minimal bad core are confined to a highly rigid lower-dimensional exceptional set.
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}[Proof sketch]
Take the leading cubic coefficient of the identity
\[
F_{\core}(z)\equiv 0.
\]
As in the two-pair case, vanishing forces the constituent cubic leading lines to satisfy a common projective coincidence relation, modulo the interaction corrections. Passing to the residual quotient by this common leading line and taking the next nontrivial coefficient yields the corresponding residual coincidence relation for the first residual classes. Since the core is minimal, no proper subcollection of constituent pairs can already form a bad core, so these coincidence relations must hold simultaneously across the full collection. Hence the core parameters lie in a lower-dimensional exceptional configuration.
\end{proof}

A few honest notes on how IвАЩd position this in the paper.

The strongest fully justified parts are the definitions and the structural propositions. The places that still want tightening are the assumptions about the interaction terms I12(3)I12(3)вАЛ and IвАЊ12(5)I12(5)вАЛ being absent, negligible, or lower-order in the normalized regime. I would keep those explicit rather than burying them.

If you want, the next best move is to tighten exactly those interaction-correction statements so the two-pair rigidity proposition looks less conditional.

ok now, write a very clear description of the next steps, and the key facts we know going into them including any key findings we discovered during failed routes. describe what we need to get all the way to RH

Here is the clearest picture I can give of where this stands, what we actually know, what failed, and what the next steps are if the goal is to push this all the way to RH.
Current high-level architecture

The right local object is no longer a single jet or a single detector. It is the full quotient odd germ of a finite core.

For a normalized core, the local corrected odd germ is
F\core(z)вИИG\odd/V\val,
F\coreвАЛ(z)вИИG\oddвАЛ/V\valвАЛ,

where V\valV\valвАЛ is the value/gauge family.

The program now has three layers:

    show the core quotient odd germ is not zero;

    quotient by the generic leading line of that germ;

    show the resulting residual germ is still nonzero.

For one pair, this means:

    the cubic quotient coefficient gives the leading line;

    the quintic coefficient should give the first residual witness.

For two pairs and then general finite cores, the idea is:

    vanishing of the full core germ forces projective coincidence of one-pair germs;

    the residual germ forces a second coincidence;

    a minimal bad core should therefore lie in a very rigid exceptional set.

That is the present roadmap.
The most important facts we now know
1. Order 11 is generically useless

This was a real discovery, and it matters.

We found that the real first-order value-family is generically large enough to fill all of M2(C)M2вАЛ(C). In practical terms:
V\val(1)=M2(C)
V\val(1)вАЛ=M2вАЛ(C)

generically.

So:
J1/V\val(1)=0.
J1вАЛ/V\val(1)вАЛ=0.

This kills the old hope that first odd order would directly give a robust quotient detector.

This is not a setback conceptually. It tells us something real: the first nontrivial quotient information starts later.
2. The cubic value-family is much smaller

At cubic order, the picture changes dramatically.

We found that the cubic value-family is confined to the off-diagonal sector. More specifically:

    same-point cubic value terms are purely skew;

    direct odd mixed cubic terms are skew;

    the only even mixed cubic mechanism can produce off-diagonal traceless terms;

    there is no diagonal cubic value-family contribution.

So:
V\val(3)вКВ{(0вИЧвИЧ0)},dimвБ°V\val(3)вЙ§2.
V\val(3)вАЛвКВ{(0вИЧвАЛвИЧ0вАЛ)},dimV\val(3)вАЛвЙ§2.

This is a major structural result.
3. The actual one-pair cubic jet has a generic diagonal-traceless part

We found an explicit cubic diagonal survival mechanism:
diagвБ°J3\pair(h)=v(h)вАЙB(h)вАЙ(x1вИТx2)(100вИТ1),
diagJ3\pairвАЛ(h)=v(h)B(h)(x1вАЛвИТx2вАЛ)(10вАЛ0вИТ1вАЛ),

with
B(h)=q(h)2qвА≤(h)4вИТqвА≤вА≤вА≤(h)48.
B(h)=4q(h)2qвА≤(h)вАЛвИТ48qвА≤вА≤вА≤(h)вАЛ.

Since the cubic value-family cannot touch the diagonal sector, any nonzero value of that scalar immediately gives a nonzero cubic quotient class.

So the generic leading quotient order for the one-pair family is overwhelmingly likely to be cubic:
Fh(z)=v0(h)z3+v1(h)z5+вЛѓвАЙ,v0(h)вЙ†0.
FhвАЛ(z)=v0вАЛ(h)z3+v1вАЛ(h)z5+вЛѓ,v0вАЛ(h)оА†=0.

This is the first solid local foothold.
4. The cubic leading line is diagonal-traceless

That means the cubic leading line is of the form
вДУh=CвАЙv0(h),
вДУhвАЛ=Cv0вАЛ(h),

where v0(h)v0вАЛ(h) lives in the diagonal-traceless direction.

This is what makes the quintic trace the natural first residual witness: trace is automatically outside the cubic leading line.
5. The best residual witness found so far is the quintic trace

We tried several other directions first:

    fixed-order detectors,

    symmetric off-diagonal quintic candidates,

    direct odd mixed K5K5вАЛ insertion,

    several transport/commutator blocks.

Most of those failed for real structural reasons:

    parity killed some;

    commutator structure killed some;

    some only recycled skew or traceless off-diagonal sectors.

The strongest surviving mechanism is the quintic trace coming from the K1K1вАЛ-transport block:
trвБ°J5,K1(h)=вИТ2ќЇ(h)((x1вИТx2)r(h)+ќ≤(h)(u(h)вИТw(h))+v(h)(ќ≥(h)вИТќ±(h)))+вЛѓвАЙ.
trJ5,K1вАЛвАЛ(h)=вИТ2ќЇ(h)((x1вАЛвИТx2вАЛ)r(h)+ќ≤(h)(u(h)вИТw(h))+v(h)(ќ≥(h)вИТќ±(h)))+вЛѓ.

Inside this, the cleanest explicit witness is the linear G3G3вАЛ-part of v(h)(ќ≥(h)вИТќ±(h))v(h)(ќ≥(h)вИТќ±(h)), and it contains the monomial
ќЇ(h)вАЙќЈ2(h)вАЙq(5)(h)
ќЇ(h)ќЈ2вАЛ(h)q(5)(h)

with nonzero coefficient.

That is currently the best candidate for proving that the first residual class is nonzero.
What failed, and what we learned from those failures

These failures were useful. They told us what not to build the proof around.
Failed route A: fixed first odd jet

This failed because order 11 is generically fully value-like.

Lesson:

    do not organize the proof around the first odd jet.

Failed route B: one magical detector

We kept looking for a single scalar detector that would survive everything.

Lesson:

    symmetry can kill individual detectors;

    the right invariant is the full quotient odd germ, not one hand-picked scalar.

Failed route C: simplistic cubic detectors from frozen models

In simplified models, cubic often collapsed into a skew line.

Lesson:

    the frozen models were useful heuristics, but not reliable enough to decide the real quotient structure;

    the real corrected model is richer, especially once derivative/curvature and regularity constraints are included.

Failed route D: quintic symmetric off-diagonal from direct odd mixed terms

We hoped K5K5вАЛ might directly produce a symmetric off-diagonal residual.

That failed for a strong reason:

    the symmetric off-diagonal part of the mixed block is even in ss;

    therefore odd mixed coefficients K1,K3,K5,вА¶K1вАЛ,K3вАЛ,K5вАЛ,вА¶ cannot directly supply it.

Lesson:

    parity structure matters a lot;

    the right residual witness is trace, not symmetric off-diagonal.

Failed route E: leading-line derivative test as main theorem

We considered proving projective nonconstancy via
FhвИІвИВhFhвЙ†0.
FhвАЛвИІвИВhвАЛFhвАЛоА†=0.

Lesson:

    still a good probe,

    but not the main route.
    Because order 11 is trivial and the leading-line motion is not the easiest thing to compute, the derivative route is secondary to the residual-germ route.

What the next steps are, in order

These are the real next steps. I would not branch away from them now.
Step 1. Tighten the cubic leading-order theorem

Right now, we have a very plausible cubic-leading theorem, but it still depends on generic nondegeneracy conditions like:
v(h)вЙ°ћЄ0,B(h)вЙ°ћЄ0,x1вЙ†x2.
v(h)оА†вЙ°0,B(h)оА†вЙ°0,x1вАЛоА†=x2вАЛ.

What to do:

    write this as a proper theorem with hypotheses;

    make explicit which hypotheses are genuinely generic and which need proof;

    if possible, tighten the argument that B(h)вЙ°ћЄ0B(h)оА†вЙ°0 generically.

Goal:

    make the cubic leading line of the one-pair germ a stable theorem, not just a compelling calculation.

Step 2. Prove the no-cancellation lemma for the quintic trace witness

This is the current bottleneck.

The key claim is:

    the monomial
    ќЇ(h)вАЙќЈ2(h)вАЙq(5)(h)
    ќЇ(h)ќЈ2вАЛ(h)q(5)(h)

    appears in trвБ°J5\pair(h)trJ5\pairвАЛ(h) with nonzero coefficient;

    no other quintic contribution produces the same monomial;

    therefore
    trвБ°J5\pair(h)вЙ°ћЄ0.
    trJ5\pairвАЛ(h)оА†вЙ°0.

What to do:

    explicitly enumerate the quintic trace contributions;

    classify them by derivative profile;

    isolate the ќЇќЈ2q(5)ќЇќЈ2вАЛq(5) monomial;

    show uniqueness.

Goal:

    prove the first residual class is nonzero:
    vвАЊ1(h)вЙ†0.
    v1вАЛ(h)оА†=0.

This is the single most important unresolved local step.
Step 3. Lock the one-pair projective nonconstancy theorem

Once Step 2 is done, we get:

    cubic leading line вДУhвДУhвАЛ,

    first residual class vвАЊ1(h)вЙ†0v1вАЛ(h)оА†=0,

    therefore the one-pair quotient odd germ is not projectively constant.

This should be stated cleanly as the one-pair local theorem.

Goal:

    produce a finished one-pair theorem that the later multi-pair sections can cite without hedging.

Step 4. Build the two-pair rigidity theorem

For a two-pair normalized core:
F(a1,h1;a2,h2)(z)=a1Fh1(z)+a2Fh2(z)+I12(z).
F(a1вАЛ,h1вАЛ;a2вАЛ,h2вАЛ)вАЛ(z)=a1вАЛFh1вАЛвАЛ(z)+a2вАЛFh2вАЛвАЛ(z)+I12вАЛ(z).

What to prove:

    if the two-pair germ vanishes, then the cubic leading lines must coincide projectively;

    after quotienting by that common line, the first residual classes must also coincide;

    this traps bad two-pair cores in a lower-dimensional exceptional set.

The main technical issue here is the interaction germ I12I12вАЛ.

What to do:

    quantify its cubic and quintic coefficients;

    show they are lower-order or harmless in the normalized regime, or at least controllable enough that they do not create a new vanishing family.

Goal:

    turn the two-pair section from вАЬprojective schemaвАЭ into a real rigidity theorem.

Step 5. Move to minimal bad finite core

Assume there exists a genuine bad finite core, and choose one with minimal size kk.

Write
F\core(z)=вИСj=1kajFhj(z)+IвЙ•2(z).
F\coreвАЛ(z)=j=1вИСkвАЛajвАЛFhjвАЛвАЛ(z)+IвЙ•2вАЛ(z).

What to prove:

    all constituent one-pair germs must lie in a common projective coincidence locus at cubic level;

    all first residual classes must also lie in a common residual coincidence locus;

    this exceptional configuration is too small / degenerate / empty.

This is where the argument becomes global over all finite cores.

Goal:

    prove no minimal bad core exists.

Step 6. Close the bridge from finite bad core exclusion to RH

This is the final conceptual step.

The overall architecture is:

    outside-tail odd coefficients are small at every odd order;

    any genuine finite core would have a nontrivial quotient odd germ, with leading and residual structure;

    therefore a vanishing WeilвАУGram obstruction cannot come from a genuine finite off-line core;

    escaping components vanish in the normalized limit;

    hence no bad core remains;

    hence RH.

This final bridge still needs a clean writeup that connects:

    the tail estimates,

    the normalized finite-core compactness argument,

    and the minimal bad core exclusion.

Goal:

    show that every normalized obstruction either disappears in the tail or yields a forbidden finite bad core.

What is needed to get all the way to RH

In my view, there are four genuine proof bottlenecks left.
Bottleneck 1: Quintic no-cancellation

This is the most immediate one.
Until this is proved, the one-pair residual theorem is not fully closed.
Bottleneck 2: Two-pair interaction control

We need to know that I12I12вАЛ does not destroy the projective coincidence argument.
Bottleneck 3: Minimal bad core contradiction

This is the вАЬall kkвАЭ step.
We need a real theorem that a minimal bad core must lie in an impossible exceptional configuration.
Bottleneck 4: Final compactness / exclusion bridge

We need the theorem that every would-be obstruction produces a bad finite core in the normalized limit, and since bad finite cores do not exist, RH follows.

Those are the four steps between the current draft and a full RH proof.
My honest assessment of the current state

The paper now has a real architecture. That is a big improvement.

What I believe is genuinely strong right now:

    the shift from single jets to the full quotient odd germ;

    the discovery that order 11 is generically trivial;

    the identification of cubic as the first serious quotient order;

    the identification of the quintic trace as the natural first residual witness;

    the one-pair вЖТ two-pair вЖТ minimal bad core architecture.

What is still not done:

    the no-cancellation lemma;

    a quantitative two-pair interaction theorem;

    the actual contradiction for minimal bad cores;

    the final RH bridge.

So the next work should be extremely focused:

    finish the quintic no-cancellation lemma;

    then finish the two-pair interaction control;

    then finish minimal bad core rigidity.

That is the shortest honest path from here to RH.

do you know this definition of "Definition/Structure theorem for the gauge family." ??

I checked the draft itself, and the answer is: **no, the commutator model is not actually encoded yet**.

What the file currently gives is only this much:

it introduces (Q) as вАЬthe odd-germ quotient by the value/gauge family,вАЭ defines
[
F_h(z)\in Q[[z^2]],
]
and then proves the two concrete value-family facts
[
V_{\val}^{(1)}=M_2(\mathbf C)
\qquad\text{and}\qquad
V_{\val}^{(3)}\subset
\left{\begin{pmatrix}0&*\ *&0\end{pmatrix}\right}.
]
But it does **not** define what the вАЬgauge familyвАЭ actually is, nor its infinitesimal generators.

That matters a lot. The whole commutator route depends on being allowed to say:

1. scalar normalization acts by
   [
   A_5\mapsto A_5+c_2A_3+c_4A_1,
   ]
   which becomes harmless after passing to (Q/\ell_h);

2. basis-change gauge acts infinitesimally by commutators
   [
   A_5\mapsto A_5+[B_2,A_3]+[B_4,A_1]+\cdots,
   ]
   hence contributes only traceless directions.

The draft currently does **not** supply item 2. So that bridge is not proved from the present text.

There is a second reason to be cautious. Elsewhere the draft explicitly says that coefficient extraction under the global normalization is delicate, and that a separate coefficient-normalization audit at odd orders is still missing. In particular, the paper deliberately avoids claiming the stronger odd-jet coefficient statement because that audit has not been done.

So the honest conclusion is:

[
\boxed{
\text{the current draft does not yet justify }
\text{вАЬgauge = scalar renormalization + infinitesimal conjugation.вАЭ}
}
]

That means we cannot honestly finish the residual-trace argument from the file as it stands.

What *is* now clear is the exact missing theorem. It should be something like:

[
\textbf{Definition/Structure theorem for the gauge family.}
]
The odd-germ quotient (Q) is obtained by modding out by:
an explicit pure value family,
an explicit even scalar renormalization family,
and an explicit even basis-change family acting by conjugation.

Then one proves:

* scalar renormalization only changes (v_1) by a multiple of (v_0), so it dies in (Q/\ell_h);
* conjugation contributes only commutators, hence only traceless directions;
* the pure value-channel odd jets are traceless.

At that point raw trace really would descend to the residual quotient, and the open-dense residual theorem would follow from the already-proved non-identical vanishing of (\operatorname{tr}J_5^{\pair}(h)). The current file already has the pieces (V_{\val}^{(1)}=M_2(\mathbf C)), the cubic line (v_0(h)\neq 0) on (\mathcal U_{\mathrm{cub}}), and the generic quintic trace witness; what it lacks is the structural identification of the gauge directions.

So the real status is:

* the **mathematical bottleneck is now sharply identified**;
* it is **not** quintic bookkeeping anymore;
* it is the **missing definition and structure theorem for the gauge family**.

The best next move is to add that definition explicitly to the paper, rather than trying to squeeze a proof out of an undefined quotient.

Yes вАФ I think your diagnosis is basically right.

the draft still lacks a precise definition and structure theorem for the gauge family.
the draft still lacks a precise definition and structure theorem for the gauge family.вАЛ

And I agree with the consequence:

So the main issue is no longer вАЬwhich quintic term survives?вАЭ in isolation. It is:

    what exactly are we quotienting by,

    how do those gauge directions act on the odd jet coefficients,

    and what survives after modding them out.

That is the right place to focus.
My honest view

I think this is more fundamental than the quintic bookkeeping.

The quintic witness story is only useful if we know that:

    scalar renormalizations only change the quintic coefficient by a multiple of the cubic leading line,

    basis changes contribute only commutator-type directions,

    and pure value-channel odd jets lie in the appropriate trace/traceless subspaces.

If those structure facts are not stated and proved, then saying вАЬthe quintic trace descends to the residual quotientвАЭ is premature.

So yes: the next serious theorem should be a Definition / Structure theorem for the gauge family.
What I think that theorem should say

There are really three families mixed together under the word вАЬgaugeвАЭ:
1. Pure value family

This is the one already partly analyzed. It is the local deformation coming from changing the scalar phase/value profile while staying in the corrected local model.

This contributes the spaces
V\val(1),V\val(3),V\val(5),вА¶
V\val(1)вАЛ,V\val(3)вАЛ,V\val(5)вАЛ,вА¶

This part is already the most concrete in the draft.
2. Even scalar renormalization family

This is the freedom to multiply the odd germ by an even scalar germ
1+c2z2+c4z4+вЛѓвАЙ.
1+c2вАЛz2+c4вАЛz4+вЛѓ.

If
F(z)=A3z3+A5z5+A7z7+вЛѓвАЙ,
F(z)=A3вАЛz3+A5вАЛz5+A7вАЛz7+вЛѓ,

then scalar renormalization sends
A3вЖ¶A3,
A3вАЛвЖ¶A3вАЛ,
A5вЖ¶A5+c2A3,
A5вАЛвЖ¶A5вАЛ+c2вАЛA3вАЛ,
A7вЖ¶A7+c2A5+c4A3,
A7вАЛвЖ¶A7вАЛ+c2вАЛA5вАЛ+c4вАЛA3вАЛ,

and so on.

So at the first residual step, after quotienting by the cubic leading line вДУh=CA3вДУhвАЛ=CA3вАЛ, the scalar renormalization is harmless:
it changes A5A5вАЛ only by a multiple of A3A3вАЛ, hence dies in Q/вДУhQ/вДУhвАЛ.

That part should be stated explicitly.
3. Even basis-change / conjugation family

This is the missing structural piece.

If the local jet block is expressed in a jet basis, then an even basis change should have the form
P(z)=I+z2B2+z4B4+вЛѓвАЙ.
P(z)=I+z2B2вАЛ+z4B4вАЛ+вЛѓ.

Then the transformed odd germ should be
F(z)вЖ¶P(z)F(z)P(z)вИТ1.
F(z)вЖ¶P(z)F(z)P(z)вИТ1.

Expanding, this gives
A3вЖ¶A3,
A3вАЛвЖ¶A3вАЛ,
A5вЖ¶A5+[B2,A3],
A5вАЛвЖ¶A5вАЛ+[B2вАЛ,A3вАЛ],
A7вЖ¶A7+[B2,A5]+[B4,A3]+вЛѓвАЙ.
A7вАЛвЖ¶A7вАЛ+[B2вАЛ,A5вАЛ]+[B4вАЛ,A3вАЛ]+вЛѓ.

This is the bridge your quoted text is pointing at.

And yes: the current draft does not yet prove this.

But if it is true, it is exactly the structure theorem we need.

Because then:

    scalar renormalization contributes multiples of the leading line;

    conjugation contributes commutators;

    commutators are traceless.

So if the cubic leading line is diagonal-traceless, then the quintic trace descends automatically to the residual quotient.

That is the missing bridge.
So what should be added to the paper

I would add a subsection titled something like:
Definition and structure of the gauge family.
Definition and structure of the gauge family.

And I would make it contain the following.
Definition

The gauge family consists of:

    the explicit pure value family;

    the explicit even scalar renormalization family;

    the explicit even jet-basis change family.

Structure theorem

If
F(z)=A3z3+A5z5+A7z7+вЛѓвАЙ,
F(z)=A3вАЛz3+A5вАЛz5+A7вАЛz7+вЛѓ,

then under gauge:
scalar renormalization
A5вЖ¶A5+c2A3,
A5вАЛвЖ¶A5вАЛ+c2вАЛA3вАЛ,
basis change
A5вЖ¶A5+[B2,A3],
A5вАЛвЖ¶A5вАЛ+[B2вАЛ,A3вАЛ],

and therefore modulo the cubic leading line вДУh=CA3вДУhвАЛ=CA3вАЛ, the first residual coefficient transforms by
[A5]вЖ¶[A5+[B2,A3]]in Q/вДУh.
[A5вАЛ]вЖ¶[A5вАЛ+[B2вАЛ,A3вАЛ]]in Q/вДУhвАЛ.

If moreover A3A3вАЛ is diagonal-traceless, then [B2,A3][B2вАЛ,A3вАЛ] is traceless, so the trace of A5A5вАЛ is gauge-invariant in the residual quotient.

That is the theorem the current draft is missing.
Why I think this is the right bottleneck

Because once this theorem is in place, the local route becomes much cleaner:

    order 11 quotient is trivial;

    cubic quotient survives and defines the leading line;

    scalar renormalization dies in Q/вДУhQ/вДУhвАЛ;

    basis-change gauge contributes only commutators, hence only traceless directions;

    therefore a nonzero quintic trace proves the first residual class is nonzero.

So the residual-trace argument becomes legitimate.

Without this theorem, the residual-trace argument is only heuristic.

So yes: I think your quoted diagnosis is right.
What I would do next

I would not try to squeeze more out of the quintic expansions until this is stated.

The next steps should be:
Step 1

Add the definition of the gauge family explicitly.
Step 2

Prove the structure theorem at the first residual level:
A5вЖ¶A5+c2A3+[B2,A3].
A5вАЛвЖ¶A5вАЛ+c2вАЛA3вАЛ+[B2вАЛ,A3вАЛ].
Step 3

Deduce the residual invariance of trace when A3A3вАЛ is diagonal-traceless.
Step 4

Then return to the quintic trace witness and use it to prove
vвАЊ1(h)вЙ†0.
v1вАЛ(h)оА†=0.

That is the clean order.
Bottom line

So my answer is:
Yes вАФ this is the right missing theorem, and it is more important than the remaining quintic bookkeeping.
